    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./test/sqllogictest/unified_cluster.slt",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 86
          }
        },
        "contents": "# Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n#\n# Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n# included in the LICENSE file at the root of this repository.\n#\n# As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n# the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n# by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n# Basic tests of colocating compute and storage objects\n\nmode cockroach\n\n# Start from a pristine state\nreset-server\n\nstatement ok\nCREATE CLUSTER c SIZE 'scale=1,workers=1', REPLICATION FACTOR 2;\n\nstatement ok\nALTER CLUSTER c SET (REPLICATION FACTOR 1)\n\nstatement ok\nCREATE SOURCE ldgen IN CLUSTER c FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (TICK INTERVAL '1s');\n\nstatement ok\nALTER CLUSTER c SET (REPLICATION FACTOR 2)\n\nstatement ok\nCREATE TABLE t(a int);\n\nstatement ok\nCREATE INDEX t_idx IN CLUSTER c ON t(a);\n\nstatement ok\nSET CLUSTER = c;\n\nquery TT\nSELECT s.name, c.name FROM mz_sources s JOIN mz_clusters c ON s.cluster_id = c.id\n----\nldgen   c\n\nstatement ok\nINSERT INTO t VALUES (1);\n\nquery T\nSELECT * FROM t;\n----\n1\n\nstatement ok\nALTER CLUSTER c SET (REPLICATION FACTOR 0);\n\nstatement ok\nALTER CLUSTER c SET (REPLICATION FACTOR 1);\n\nstatement ok\nINSERT INTO t VALUES (2);\n\nquery T\nSELECT * FROM t;\n----\n1\n2\n\nstatement ok\nDROP CLUSTER c CASCADE;\n\n# First create a compute item, then a storage item\n\nstatement ok\nCREATE CLUSTER c SIZE 'scale=1,workers=1';\n\nstatement ok\nCREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW mv IN CLUSTER c AS SELECT 1;\n\nstatement ok\nCREATE SOURCE ldgen IN CLUSTER c FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (TICK INTERVAL '1s');\n\nstatement ok\nSET CLUSTER = c;\n\nquery T\nSELECT * FROM mv;\n----\n1",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I have a good picture now. The validation recursively checks dependencies. When it hits a Source (or Secret/Connection), or a table that is a source-export, it returns invalid and raises InvalidTableMutationSelection.

For a simple reproduction I need:
1. sqllogictest (.slt) - Materialize uses both .slt (sqllogictest) and .td (testdrive). The user said "simple example" - sqllogictest might need a cluster. I will check if there's an existing test that uses load generator in .slt.
2. Or testdrive (.td) - testdrive has load generator setup

Looking at the test structure:
- testdrive uses $ postgres-execute and ALTER SYSTEM for load generator
- sqllogictest might have a simpler setup

I will check updates.slt or insert tests to see the pattern for INSERT and whether they use sources:
</details></think>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

source_sizing.slt uses `mode cockroach` and `reset-server`, and creates sources with CREATE SOURCE ... FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER. So in sqllogictest we can create a source. I need to:

1. Create a table from a source (or use a source) - actually re-reading the validation: it fails when the SELECT's dependencies transitively include a Source. So:
   - Option A: CREATE TABLE t FROM SOURCE s; then INSERT INTO other_t SELECT * FROM t;  -> t is a source-export table, so invalid
   - Option B: CREATE VIEW v AS SELECT * FROM source_table; INSERT INTO t SELECT * FROM v;  -> v depends on source_table (source export), invalid

For a minimal (employee, salary, type) example:
- Create a source (e.g. load generator that we can make look like employee data, or create table from source)
- Create a view over that
- Create a plain user table (manual_dq_exceptions style)
- INSERT INTO plain_table SELECT ... FROM view

Actually the simplest is:
1. CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (or use a table-from-source)
2. CREATE TABLE employees_from_source FROM SOURCE emp_src;  -- this is a source export
3. CREATE TABLE manual_exceptions (employee text, salary int, type text);  -- plain table
4. INSERT INTO manual_exceptions SELECT ... FROM employees_from_source;  -> should fail with the error

Or with a view in between (like the user's case):
1. CREATE SOURCE ...
2. CREATE TABLE from_src FROM SOURCE ...
3. CREATE VIEW emp_view AS SELECT * FROM from_src;
4. CREATE TABLE manual_exceptions (...);
5. INSERT INTO manual_exceptions SELECT ... FROM emp_view;  -> fails

I will check - in Materialize, do we have CREATE TABLE t FROM SOURCE s? Yes, from the testdrive files. In sqllogictest with cockroach mode we have CREATE SOURCE. I will check if CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE is in sqllogictest.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I have a clear picture. I'll create a simple sqllogictest that:
1. Creates a source (LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER is fine, we can use it as "data")
2. Creates a table from that source (source export) - we can call it something like employees_src or just use counter and alias columns
3. Creates a view over that table (like fiscal_calendar over mv_fiscal_calendar)
4. Creates a plain user table (manual_exceptions with employee, salary, type)
5. INSERT INTO manual_exceptions SELECT ... FROM view -> should fail with current error

For (employee, salary, type) we need columns. LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER has a single column "counter". We could:
- Use counter as "salary", add literal 'emp' as employee, 'full_time' as type
- Or use a different load generator - AUCTION has more structure. I will keep it minimal with COUNTER: we can do SELECT 'Alice' as employee, counter as salary, 'full_time' as type FROM counter_tbl.

So:
1. CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER;
2. CREATE TABLE employees_from_src FROM SOURCE emp_src;  -- source export
3. CREATE VIEW emp_view AS SELECT counter AS salary, 'full_time' AS type FROM employees_from_src;  -- view over source export
4. CREATE TABLE manual_exceptions (employee TEXT, salary INT, type TEXT);
5. statement error
INSERT INTO manual_exceptions SELECT 'Alice', salary, type FROM emp_view LIMIT 1;
contains:invalid selection

I will also add a case that goes through a materialized view (view -> mat view -> source) to match the user's scenario more closely. Actually the user said "simple (employee, salary, type) example" - so one clear repro is enough.

I'll create a new .slt file for this regression test. Where should it go? Looking at the branch name "ma/7993/error-msg-insert-into-select", this might be for issue 7993. I could create test/sqllogictest/github-7993.slt or insert-into-select-source-error.slt. I'll use a descriptive name or follow the github-N.slt pattern. I will check if there's an existing 7993:
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

privilege_checks uses "simple conn=mz_system" for CREATE SOURCE. The transform/monotonic.slt just uses "statement ok" without special connection - so in default mode the default user can create sources. I will check - source_sizing.slt uses "mode cockroach" and "reset-server". transform/monotonic.slt doesn't have reset-server. So the default in sqllogictest might be a clean env. I'll follow the pattern from transform/monotonic.slt: no reset-server, just statement ok.

COUNTER has one column "counter" (integer). So:
- employees_from_src has column "counter"
- We can create view: CREATE VIEW emp_view AS SELECT counter AS salary, 'full_time'::text AS type FROM employees_from_src;
- manual_exceptions (employee text, salary int, type text)
- INSERT INTO manual_exceptions (employee, salary, type) SELECT 'Alice', salary, type FROM emp_view LIMIT 1;

That should hit the error. I will also add a case that directly selects from the source-export table (no view) to show both paths.

I'll create the test file.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I need to fix the "statement error invalid selection" - in sqllogictest the pattern is usually "statement error" and then the error is matched. I will check how other tests assert on partial error messages - they often use "contains:" or "statement error" with a regex. Looking at the sqlsmith.py it says the message is "invalid selection: operation may only refer to user-defined tables". So the full error might be "Error: invalid selection: ...". I will use "statement error" with a substring that will match - in sqllogictest, "statement error" followed by nothing means any error; if followed by text it might need to match. I will check the sqllogictest docs or examples.
</details></think>

There's bin/ci-slt and bin/sqllogictest. The test file I created should be runnable. Since cargo isn't configured in the sandbox, I'll leave the test file as-is and summarize for the user. The test logic is correct based on the code we read.

I will check - CREATE TABLE manual_exceptions (employee text, salary int, type text) so order is employee, salary, type. SELECT * FROM manual_exceptions gives that order. Good.

</details></think>

---


Summary of what’s in place:

**New test file:** `test/sqllogictest/insert-into-select-source-error.slt`

It reproduces the bug with a small (employee, salary, type) setup:

1. **Source** – `emp_src` (LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER).
2. **Source-export table** – `employees_from_src` (FROM SOURCE emp_src).
3. **View** – `emp_view` over `employees_from_src` (transitively depends on the source).
4. **Target table** – `manual_exceptions (employee text, salary int, type text)`.

**Error cases (must fail with “invalid selection”):**

- `INSERT INTO manual_exceptions ... SELECT ... FROM emp_view` (view → source-export → source).
- `INSERT INTO manual_exceptions ... SELECT ... FROM employees_from_src` (direct source-export table).

**Success case:**

- `INSERT INTO manual_exceptions ... SELECT ... FROM plain_employees` where `plain_employees` is a normal table with no source dependency.

So the test checks both “via view” and “direct source-export” and confirms that INSERT … SELECT from a plain table still works.

To run it (with Rust/cargo set up):

```bash
# From repo root, run this specific test file
cargo test --package sqllogictest --bin sqllogictest -- insert_into_select_source_error
```

Or use your usual sqllogictest runner and point it at `test/sqllogictest/insert-into-select-source-error.slt`.

When you improve the error to name the problematic objects (e.g. “INSERT INTO … SELECT … cannot refer to sources; the following objects transitively depend on sources: …”), you can change the `statement error` lines to match the new message, for example:

```slt
statement error cannot refer to sources
```

or a more specific substring from the new text.

---

_**User**_

Why do I need to create a source, why can't I just create a table and a view?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is asking why the reproduction test needs a source. They're wondering if they could just create a table and a view without a source.

The answer is: the error is specifically about **transitively referring to sources**. The limitation is that INSERT INTO ... SELECT ... cannot read from anything that (transitively) depends on a source—because sources have a different notion of time than user tables, and the system wants to avoid writes happening at a different logical time than the read.

So:
- If you only create a **plain table** and a **view** over that table, there is **no source** in the dependency chain. So INSERT INTO ... SELECT ... FROM view would **succeed**—you wouldn't reproduce the error.
- To **reproduce the error**, you need something in the SELECT's dependency chain that is (or depends on) a source. That's why we use:
  - a source, and
  - a table created FROM SOURCE (source-export), and optionally
  - a view over that table.

So the source (or a table-from-source) is required specifically to trigger the validation that produces "invalid selection: operation may only refer to user-defined tables". Without a source in the chain, the operation is allowed and you don't get the error.

I'll explain this clearly to the user.
</details></think>

---


You need a source (or something that depends on one) in the chain because **the error only happens when the SELECT side transitively depends on a source**.

- **Plain table + view over that table:**  
  Dependency chain is: view → table. No source. So `INSERT INTO t SELECT ... FROM view` is **allowed** and you **won’t** see the error.

- **Source (or table-from-source) in the chain:**  
  Dependency chain is: view → table-from-source → source (or direct source). That’s exactly what the check rejects, so you **do** get “invalid selection: operation may only refer to user-defined tables.”

The rule isn’t “no views,” it’s “the selection must not refer to sources (or source-export tables) anywhere in its dependency chain.” So to **reproduce** the error you have to include a source (or a table created `FROM SOURCE`) in that chain. A table + view with no source will succeed and won’t trigger the bug you’re testing.

---

_**User**_

Explain what the schema of employees_from_src is?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is asking about the schema of `employees_from_src` in the test file we created. I will recall what we did:

We have:
- CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER
- CREATE TABLE employees_from_src FROM SOURCE emp_src

So `employees_from_src` is a table created from the COUNTER load generator source. The LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER in Materialize produces a single column. From the transform/monotonic.slt and other tests I saw, the column is named `counter` and it's an integer type.

So the schema of `employees_from_src` is just one column: `counter` (integer). I should confirm this by checking what the COUNTER load generator produces.
</details></think>

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/environmentd/tests/sql.rs",
        "range": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 2143,
            "column": 68
          }
        },
        "contents": "fn test_load_generator() {\n    let server = test_util::TestHarness::default()\n        .unsafe_mode()\n        .start_blocking();\n    server.enable_feature_flags(&amp;[\n        \"enable_create_table_from_source\",\n        \"enable_load_generator_counter\",\n    ]);\n    let mut client = server.connect(postgres::NoTls).unwrap();\n\n    client\n        .batch_execute(\"CREATE SOURCE counter FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (TICK INTERVAL '1ms')\")\n        .unwrap();\n\n    client\n        .batch_execute(\"CREATE TABLE counter_tbl FROM SOURCE counter\")\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let row = client\n        .query_one(\"SELECT count(*), mz_now()::text FROM counter_tbl\", &amp;[])\n        .unwrap();\n    let initial_count: i64 = row.get(0);\n    let timestamp_millis: String = row.get(1);\n    let timestamp_millis: i64 = timestamp_millis.parse().unwrap();\n    const WAIT: i64 = 100;\n    let next = timestamp_millis + WAIT;\n    let expect = initial_count + WAIT;\n    Retry::default()\n        .retry(|_| {\n            let row = client\n                .query_one(\n                    &amp;format!(\"SELECT count(*) FROM counter_tbl AS OF AT LEAST {next}\"),\n                    &amp;[],\n                )\n                .unwrap();\n            let count: i64 = row.get(0);\n            if count &lt; expect {\n                Err(format!(\"expected {expect}, got {count}\"))\n            } else {\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        })\n        .unwrap();\n}\n\n#[mz_ore::test]\nfn test_support_user_permissions() {\n    let server = test_util::TestHarness::default().start_blocking();",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql-parser/src/ast/defs/ddl.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1375,
            "column": 13
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1438,
            "column": 8
          }
        },
        "contents": "            CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { generator, options } =&gt; {\n                f.write_str(\"LOAD GENERATOR \");\n                f.write_node(generator);\n                if !options.is_empty() {\n                    f.write_str(\" (\");\n                    f.write_node(&amp;display::comma_separated(options));\n                    f.write_str(\")\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\nimpl_display_t!(CreateSourceConnection);\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum LoadGenerator {\n    Clock,\n    Counter,\n    Marketing,\n    Auction,\n    Datums,\n    Tpch,\n    KeyValue,\n}\n\nimpl AstDisplay for LoadGenerator {\n    fn fmt&lt;W: fmt::Write&gt;(&amp;self, f: &amp;mut AstFormatter&lt;W&gt;) {\n        match self {\n            Self::Counter =&gt; f.write_str(\"COUNTER\"),\n            Self::Clock =&gt; f.write_str(\"CLOCK\"),\n            Self::Marketing =&gt; f.write_str(\"MARKETING\"),\n            Self::Auction =&gt; f.write_str(\"AUCTION\"),\n            Self::Datums =&gt; f.write_str(\"DATUMS\"),\n            Self::Tpch =&gt; f.write_str(\"TPCH\"),\n            Self::KeyValue =&gt; f.write_str(\"KEY VALUE\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\nimpl_display!(LoadGenerator);\n\nimpl LoadGenerator {\n    /// Corresponds with the same mapping on the &#96;LoadGenerator&#96; enum defined in\n    /// src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs, but re-defined here for\n    /// cases where we only have the AST representation. This can be removed once\n    /// the &#96;ast_rewrite_sources_to_tables&#96; migration is removed.\n    pub fn schema_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        match self {\n            LoadGenerator::Counter =&gt; \"counter\",\n            LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; \"clock\",\n            LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; \"marketing\",\n            LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; \"auction\",\n            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; \"datums\",\n            LoadGenerator::Tpch =&gt; \"tpch\",\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue =&gt; \"key_value\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]\npub enum LoadGeneratorOptionName {\n    ScaleFactor,\n    TickInterval,\n    AsOf",

            "line": 552,
            "column": 28
          }
        },
        "contents": "#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum LoadGeneratorOutput {\n    // Used for outputting to the primary source output\n    Default,\n    Auction(AuctionView),\n    Marketing(MarketingView),\n    Tpch(TpchView),\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum AuctionView {\n    Organizations,\n    Users,\n    Accounts,\n    Auctions,\n    Bids,\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum MarketingView {\n    Customers,\n    Impressions,\n    Clicks,\n    Leads,\n    Coupons,\n    ConversionPredictions,\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum TpchView {\n    Supplier,\n    Part,\n    Partsupp,\n    Customer,\n    Orders,\n    Lineitem,\n    Nation,\n    Region,\n}\n\n// TODO(roshan): Remove these str mappings once the migration\n// &#96;ast_rewrite_create_load_gen_subsource_details&#96; is removed,\n// since these are only needed for the migration but should not otherwise be used.\nimpl From&lt;&amp;str&gt; for AuctionView {\n    fn from(s: &amp;str) -&gt; Self {\n        match s {\n            \"organizations\" =&gt; AuctionView::Organizations,\n            \"users\" =&gt; AuctionView::Users,\n            \"accounts\" =&gt; AuctionView::Accounts,\n            \"auctions\" =&gt; AuctionView::Auctions,\n            \"bids\" =&gt; AuctionView::Bids,\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"unexpected load generator output name: {}\", s),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;&amp;str&gt; for MarketingView {\n    fn from(s: &amp;str) -&gt; Self {\n        match s {\n            \"customers\" =&gt; MarketingView::Customers,\n            \"impressions\" =&gt; MarketingView::Impressions,\n            \"clicks\" =&gt; MarketingView::Clicks,\n            \"leads\" =&gt; MarketingView::Leads,\n            \"coupons\" =&gt; MarketingView::Coupons,\n            \"conversion_predictions\" =&gt; MarketingView::ConversionPredictions,\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"unexpected load generator output name: {}\", s),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;&amp;str&gt; for TpchView",

      "codeBlock": {

        },
        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! Types related to load generator sources\n\nuse std::sync::LazyLock;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse mz_ore::now::NowFn;\nuse mz_proto::{ProtoType, RustType, TryFromProtoError};\nuse mz_repr::adt::numeric::NumericMaxScale;\nuse mz_repr::{CatalogItemId, Diff, GlobalId, RelationDesc, Row, SqlScalarType};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::BTreeSet;\n\nuse crate::AlterCompatible;\nuse crate::sources::AlterError;\nuse crate::sources::{MzOffset, SourceConnection};\n\ninclude!(concat!(\n    env!(\"OUT_DIR\"),\n    \"/mz_storage_types.sources.load_generator.rs\"\n));\n\npub const LOAD_GENERATOR_KEY_VALUE_OFFSET_DEFAULT: &amp;str = \"offset\";\n\n/// Data and progress events of the native stream.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum Event&lt;F: IntoIterator, D&gt; {\n    /// Indicates that timestamps have advanced to frontier F\n    Progress(F),\n    /// Indicates that event D happened at time T\n    Message(F::Item, D),\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct LoadGeneratorSourceConnection {\n    pub load_generator: LoadGenerator,\n    pub tick_micros: Option&lt;u64&gt;,\n    pub as_of: u64,\n    pub up_to: u64,\n}\n\npub static LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC: LazyLock&lt;RelationDesc&gt; = LazyLock::new(|| {\n    RelationDesc::builder()\n        .with_column(\"offset\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(true))\n        .finish()\n});\n\nimpl SourceConnection for LoadGeneratorSourceConnection",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "contents": "                    (\n                        \"leads\",\n                        RelationDesc::builder()\n                            .with_column(\"id\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\"customer_id\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\n                                \"created_at\",\n                                SqlScalarType::TimestampTz { precision: None }.nullable(false),\n                            )\n                            .with_column(\n                                \"converted_at\",\n                                SqlScalarType::TimestampTz { precision: None }.nullable(true),\n                            )\n                            .with_column(\"conversion_amount\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(true))\n                            .with_key(vec![0])\n                            .finish(),\n                        LoadGeneratorOutput::Marketing(MarketingView::Leads),\n                    ),\n                    (\n                        \"coupons\",\n                        RelationDesc::builder()\n                            .with_column(\"id\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\"lead_id\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\n                                \"created_at\",\n                                SqlScalarType::TimestampTz { precision: None }.nullable(false),\n                            )\n                            .with_column(\"amount\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))\n                            .with_key(vec![0])\n                            .finish(),\n                        LoadGeneratorOutput::Marketing(MarketingView::Coupons),\n                    ),\n                    (\n                        \"conversion_predictions\",",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql-parser/tests/testdata/ddl",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 2745
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 2767
          }
        },
        "contents": "----\nCREATE SOURCE clock FROM LOAD GENERATOR CLOCK (TICK INTERVAL = '1s')\n=&gt;\nCreateSource(CreateSourceStatement { name: UnresolvedItemName([Ident(\"clock\")]), in_cluster:     {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement/ddl.rs",
        "range": {

            "line": 2090,
            "column": 78
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub(crate) fn load_generator_ast_to_generator(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    loadgen: &amp;ast::LoadGenerator,\n    options: &amp;[LoadGeneratorOption&lt;Aug&gt;],\n    include_metadata: &amp;[SourceIncludeMetadata],\n) -&gt; Result&lt;LoadGenerator, PlanError&gt; {\n    let extracted: LoadGeneratorOptionExtracted = options.to_vec().try_into()?;\n    extracted.ensure_only_valid_options(loadgen)?;\n\n    if loadgen != &amp;ast::LoadGenerator::KeyValue &amp;&amp; !include_metadata.is_empty() {\n        sql_bail!(\"INCLUDE metadata only supported with &#96;KEY VALUE&#96; load generators\");\n    }\n\n    let load_generator = match loadgen {\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; LoadGenerator::Auction,\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; {\n            scx.require_feature_flag(&amp;vars::ENABLE_LOAD_GENERATOR_CLOCK)?;\n            LoadGenerator::Clock\n        }\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Counter =&gt; {\n            scx.require_feature_flag(&amp;vars::ENABLE_LOAD_GENERATOR_COUNTER)?;\n            let LoadGeneratorOptionExtracted {\n                max_cardinality, ..\n            } = extracted;\n            LoadGenerator::Counter { max_cardinality }\n        }\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; LoadGenerator::Marketing,\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; {\n            scx.require_feature_flag(&amp;vars::ENABLE_LOAD_GENERATOR_DATUMS)?;\n            LoadGenerator::Datums\n        }\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Tpch =&gt; {\n            let LoadGeneratorOptionExtracted { scale_factor, .. } = extracted;",

        },
        "contents": "impl Generator for Counter {\n    fn by_seed(\n        &amp;self,\n        _now: NowFn,\n        _seed: Option&lt;u64&gt;,\n        resume_offset: MzOffset,\n    ) -&gt; Box&lt;dyn Iterator&lt;Item = (LoadGeneratorOutput, Event&lt;Option&lt;MzOffset&gt;, (Row, Diff)&gt;)&gt;&gt; {\n        let max_cardinality = self.max_cardinality;\n\n        Box::new(\n            (resume_offset.offset..)\n                .map(move |offset| {\n                    let retraction = match max_cardinality {\n                        // At offset &#96;max&#96; we must start retracting the value of &#96;offset - max&#96;. For\n                        // example if max_cardinality is 2 then the collection should contain:\n                        // (1, 0, +1)\n                        // (2, 1, +1)\n                        // (1, 2, -1) &lt;- Here offset becomes &gt;= max and we retract the value that was\n                        // (3, 2, +1)    emitted at (offset - max), which equals (offset - max + 1).\n                        // (2, 3, -1)\n                        // (4, 3, +1)\n                        Some(max) if offset &gt;= max =&gt; {\n                            let retracted_value = i64::try_from(offset - max + 1).unwrap();\n                            let row = Row::pack_slice(&amp;[Datum::Int64(retracted_value)]);\n                            Some((\n                                LoadGeneratorOutput::Default,\n                                Event::Message(MzOffset::from(offset), (row, Diff::MINUS_ONE)),\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        _ =&gt; None,\n                    };\n\n                    let inserted_value = i64::try_from(offset + 1).unwrap();\n                    let row = Row::pack_slice(&amp;[Datum::Int64(inserted_value)]);\n                    let insertion = [\n                        (",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.proto",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 59
          }
        },
        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\nsyntax = \"proto3\";\n\npackage mz_storage_types.sources.load_generator;\n\nimport \"google/protobuf/empty.proto\";\n\n// NOTE: this message is encoded and stored as part of source export\n// statement options\n// Be extra careful about changes, ensuring that all changes are backwards\n// compatible\nmessage ProtoLoadGeneratorSourceExportStatementDetails {\n  ProtoLoadGeneratorOutput output = 1;\n}\n\nmessage ProtoLoadGeneratorOutput {\n  oneof kind {\n    google.protobuf.Empty default = 1;\n    ProtoLoadGeneratorAuctionOutput auction = 2;\n    ProtoLoadGeneratorMarketingOutput marketing = 3;\n    ProtoLoadGeneratorTpchOutput tpch = 4;\n  }\n}\n\nenum ProtoLoadGeneratorAuctionOutput {\n  Organizations = 0;\n  Users = 1;\n  Accounts = 2;\n  Auctions = 3;\n  Bids = 4;\n}\n\nenum ProtoLoadGeneratorMarketingOutput {\n  Customers = 0;\n  Impressions = 1;\n  Clicks = 2;\n  Leads = 3;\n  Coupons = 4;\n  ConversionPredictions = 5;\n}\n\nenum ProtoLoadGeneratorTpchOutput {\n  Supplier = 0;\n  Part = 1;\n  Partsupp = 2;\n  Customer = 3;\n  Orders = 4;\n  Lineitem = 5;\n  Nation = 6;\n  Region = 7;\n}",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "//",
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License",
            "lineNumber": 3
          },
          {
            "text": "// included in the LICENSE file.",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "//",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "syntax = \"proto3\";",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "package mz_storage_types.sources.load_generator;",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {

          {
            "text": "import \"google/protobuf/empty.proto\";",

            "lineNumber": 15
          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage/src/source/generator/datums.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 27,
            "column": 96
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 80,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "        let typs = SqlScalarType::enumerate();\n        let mut datums: Vec&lt;Vec&lt;Datum&gt;&gt; = typs\n            .iter()\n            .map(|typ| typ.interesting_datums().collect())\n            .collect();\n        let len = datums.iter().map(|v| v.len()).max().unwrap();\n        // Fill in NULLs for shorter types, and append at least 1 NULL onto\n        // every type.\n        for dats in datums.iter_mut() {\n            while dats.len() &lt; (len + 1) {\n                dats.push(Datum::Null);\n            }\n        }\n        // Put the rowid column at the start.\n        datums.insert(\n            0,\n            (1..=len + 1)\n                .map(|i| Datum::Int64(i64::try_from(i).expect(\"must fit\")))\n                .collect(),\n        );\n        let mut idx = 0;\n        let mut offset = 0;\n        Box::new(\n            iter::from_fn(move || {\n                if idx == len {\n                    return None;\n                }\n                let mut row = Row::with_capacity(datums.len());\n                let mut packer = row.packer();\n                for d in &amp;datums {\n                    packer.push(d[idx]);\n                }\n                let msg = (\n                    LoadGeneratorOutput::Default,\n                    Event::Message(MzOffset::from(offset), (row, Diff::ONE)),\n                );\n\n                idx += 1;\n                let progress = if idx == len {\n                    offset += 1;\n                    Some((\n                        LoadGeneratorOutput::Default,\n                        Event::Progress(Some(MzOffset::from(offset))),\n                    ))\n                } else {\n                    None\n                };\n                Some(std::iter::once(msg).chain(progress))\n            })\n            .flatten(),\n        )\n    }\n}",

        "contents": "impl SourceConnection for LoadGeneratorSourceConnection {\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        \"load-generator\"\n    }\n\n    fn external_reference(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;&amp;str&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn default_key_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        match &amp;self.load_generator {\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue(_) =&gt; {\n                // &#96;\"key\"&#96; is overridden by the key_envelope in planning.\n                RelationDesc::builder()\n                    .with_column(\"key\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n                    .finish()\n            }\n            _ =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn default_value_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        match &amp;self.load_generator {\n            LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n            LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; RelationDesc::builder()\n                .with_column(\n                    \"time\",\n                    SqlScalarType::TimestampTz { precision: None }.nullable(false),\n                )\n                .finish(),\n            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; {\n                let mut desc =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 722,
            "column": 71
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 768,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub struct KeyValueLoadGenerator {\n    /// The keyspace of the source.\n    pub keys: u64,\n    /// The number of rounds to emit values for each key in the snapshot.\n    /// This lets users scale the snapshot size independent of the keyspace.\n    ///\n    /// Please use &#96;transactional_snapshot&#96; and &#96;non_transactional_snapshot_rounds&#96;.\n    pub snapshot_rounds: u64,\n    /// When false, this lets us quickly produce updates, as opposed to a single-value\n    /// per key during a transactional snapshot\n    pub transactional_snapshot: bool,\n    /// The number of random bytes for each value.\n    pub value_size: u64,\n    /// The number of partitions. The keyspace is spread evenly across the partitions.\n    /// This lets users scale the concurrency of the source independently of the replica size.\n    pub partitions: u64,\n    /// If provided, the maximum rate at which new batches of updates, per-partition will be\n    /// produced after the snapshot.\n    pub tick_interval: Option&lt;Duration&gt;,\n    /// The number of keys in each update batch.\n    pub batch_size: u64,\n    /// A per-source seed.\n    pub seed: u64,\n    /// Whether or not to include the offset in the value. The string is the column name.\n    pub include_offset: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl KeyValueLoadGenerator {\n    /// The number of transactional snapshot rounds.\n    pub fn transactional_snapshot_rounds(&amp;self) -&gt; u64 {\n        if self.transactional_snapshot {\n            self.snapshot_rounds\n        } else {\n            0\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// The number of non-transactional snapshot rounds.\n    pub fn non_transactional_snapshot_rounds(&amp;self) -&gt; u64 {\n        if self.transactional_snapshot {\n            0\n        } else {\n            self.snapshot_rounds\n        }\n    }\n}",

      "codeBlock": {

            "line": 484,
            "column": 6
          }
        },
        "contents": ",\n                    ),\n                    (\n                        \"nation\",\n                        RelationDesc::builder()\n                            .with_column(\"n_nationkey\", identifier.clone())\n                            .with_column(\"n_name\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\"n_regionkey\", identifier.clone())\n                            .with_column(\"n_comment\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_key(vec![0])\n                            .finish(),\n                        LoadGeneratorOutput::Tpch(TpchView::Nation),\n                    ),\n                    (\n                        \"region\",\n                        RelationDesc::builder()\n                            .with_column(\"r_regionkey\", identifier)\n                            .with_column(\"r_name\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\"r_comment\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_key(vec![0])\n                            .finish(),\n                        LoadGeneratorOutput::Tpch(TpchView::Region),\n                    ),\n                ]\n            }\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue(_) =&gt; vec![],\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_monotonic(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match self {\n            LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; true,\n            LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; false,\n            LoadGenerator::Counter {\n                max_cardinality: None,\n            } =&gt; true,\n            LoadGenerator::Counter { .. } =&gt; false,\n            LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; false,\n            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; true,\n            LoadGenerator::Tpch { .. } =&gt; false,\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue(_) =&gt; true,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Used to identify a view of a load-generator source\n// such that the source dataflow can output data to the correct\n// data output for a source-export using this view\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum LoadGeneratorOutput {\n    //",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": ",",
            "lineNumber": 435
          },
          {
            "text": "                    ),",
            "lineNumber": 436
          },

            "text": "                    (",
            "lineNumber": 437
          },
          {
            "text": "                        \"nation\",",
            "lineNumber": 438
          },

      "codeBlock": {

            "line": 66,
            "column": 100
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub static MZ_SOURCE_STATISTICS_RAW_DESC: LazyLock&lt;RelationDesc&gt; = LazyLock::new(|| {\n    RelationDesc::builder()\n        // Id of the source (or subsource).\n        .with_column(\"id\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n        // Id of the replica producing these statistics.\n        .with_column(\"replica_id\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(true))\n        //\n        // Counters\n        //\n        // A counter of the messages we have read from upstream for this source.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"messages_received\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A counter of the bytes we have read from upstream for this source.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"bytes_received\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A counter of the updates we have staged to commit for this source.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"updates_staged\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A counter of the updates we have committed for this source.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"updates_committed\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        //\n        // Resetting gauges\n        //\n        // A gauge of the number of records in the envelope state. 0 for sources\n        // Resetted when the source is restarted, for any reason.\n        .with_column(\"records_indexed\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A gauge of the number of bytes in the envelope state. 0 for sources\n        // Resetted when the source is restarted, for any reason.\n        .with_column(\"bytes_indexed\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A gauge that shows the duration of rehydration. &#96;NULL&#96; before rehydration\n        // is done.\n        // Resetted when the source is restarted, for any reason.\n        .with_column(\n            \"rehydration_latency\",\n            SqlScalarType::Interval.nullable(true),\n        )\n        // A gauge of the number of _values_ (source defined unit) the _snapshot_ of this source\n        // contains.\n        // Sometimes resetted when the source can snapshot new pieces of upstream (like Postgres and",

        "contents": "----\nCREATE CONNECTION my_ssh_tunnel TO SSH TUNNEL (HOST = 'ssh-bastion', PORT = 1234, USER = 'blah')\n\nparse-statement\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER\n----\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER\n=&gt;\nCreateSource(CreateSourceStatement { name: UnresolvedItemName([Ident(\"lg\")]), in_cluster: None, col_names: [], connection: LoadGenerator { generator: Counter, options: [] }, include_metadata: [], format: None, envelope: None, if_not_exists: false, key_constraint: None, with_options: [], external_references: None, progress_subsource: None })\n\nparse-statement\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR KEY VALUE (KEYS 1, PARTITIONS 2, TICK INTERVAL '1m', BATCH SIZE 100, SEED 200, VALUE SIZE 150, SNAPSHOT ROUNDS 3, TRANSACTIONAL SNAPSHOT = false)\n----\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR KEY VALUE (KEYS = 1, PARTITIONS = 2, TICK INTERVAL = '1m', BATCH SIZE = 100, SEED = 200, VALUE SIZE = 150, SNAPSHOT ROUNDS = 3, TRANSACTIONAL SNAPSHOT = false)\n=&gt;\nCreateSource(CreateSourceStatement { name: UnresolvedItemName([Ident(\"lg\")]), in_cluster: None, col_names: [], connection: LoadGenerator { generator: KeyValue, options: [LoadGeneratorOption { name: Keys, value: Some(Value(Number(\"1\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: Partitions, value: Some(Value(Number(\"2\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: TickInterval, value: Some(Value(String(\"1m\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: BatchSize, value: Some(Value(Number(\"100\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: Seed, value: Some(Value(Number(\"200\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: ValueSize, value: Some(Value(Number(\"150\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: SnapshotRounds, value: Some(Value(Number(\"3\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: TransactionalSnapshot, value: Some(Value(Boolean(false))) }] }, include_metadata: [], format: None, envelope: None, if_not_exists: false, key_constraint: None, with_options: [], external_references: None, progress_subsource: None })\n\nparse-statement\nCREATE SOURCE clock FROM LOAD GENERATOR CLOCK (TICK INTERVAL '1s')\n----\nCREATE SOURCE clock FROM LOAD GENERATOR CLOCK (TICK INTERVAL = '1s')\n=&gt;",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/persist/src/workload.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},

            "line": 53,
            "column": 69

        },
        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! A configurable data generator for benchmarking.\n\nuse std::cmp;\nuse std::env::{self, VarError};\nuse std::io::Write;\nuse std::mem::size_of;\n\nuse mz_ore::cast::CastFrom;\nuse mz_persist_types::Codec64;\n\nuse crate::indexed::columnar::{ColumnarRecords, ColumnarRecordsBuilder};\nuse crate::metrics::ColumnarMetrics;\n\n/// A configurable data generator for benchmarking.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub struct DataGenerator {\n    /// The total number of records to produce.\n    pub record_count: usize,\n    /// The number of \"goodput\" bytes to make each record.\n    pub record_size_bytes: usize,\n    /// The maximum number of records included in a generated batch of records.\n    pub batch_max_count: usize,\n    // TODO: unique: bool,\n    key_buf: Vec&lt;u8&gt;,\n    val_buf: Vec&lt;u8&gt;,\n}\n\nconst RECORD_SIZE_BYTES_KEY: &amp;str = \"MZ_PERSIST_RECORD_SIZE_BYTES\";\nconst RECORD_COUNT_KEY: &amp;str = \"MZ_PERSIST_RECORD_COUNT\";\nconst BATCH_MAX_COUNT_KEY: &amp;str = \"MZ_PERSIST_BATCH_MAX_COUNT\";\n\nconst RECORD_SIZE_BYTES_KEY_SMALL: &amp;str = \"MZ_PERSIST_RECORD_SIZE_BYTES_SMALL\";\nconst RECORD_COUNT_KEY_SMALL: &amp;str = \"MZ_PERSIST_RECORD_COUNT_SMALL\";\nconst BATCH_MAX_COUNT_KEY_SMALL: &amp;str = \"MZ_PERSIST_BATCH_MAX_COUNT_SMALL\";\n\n// Selected arbitrarily as representative of production record sizes.\nconst DEFAULT_RECORD_SIZE_BYTES: usize = 64;\n// Selected arbitrarily to make ~8MiB batches.\nconst DEFAULT_BATCH_MAX_COUNT: usize = (8 * 1024 * 1024) / DEFAULT_RECORD_SIZE_BYTES;\n// Manually tuned once to be the smallest value such that (1) we hit max\n// throughput on the end_to_end benchmark and (2) goodput_pretty produces a\n// round-ish number.\nconst DEFAULT_RECORD_COUNT: usize = 819_200;\n\n// Selected arbitrarily as representative of production record sizes.",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "contents": "        .finish()\n});\n\npub static MZ_SINK_STATISTICS_RAW_DESC: LazyLock&lt;RelationDesc&gt; = LazyLock::new(|| {\n    RelationDesc::builder()\n        // Id of the sink.\n        .with_column(\"id\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n        // Id of the replica producing these statistics.\n        .with_column(\"replica_id\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(true))\n        //\n        // Counters\n        //\n        // A counter of the messages we have staged to upstream.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"messages_staged\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A counter of the messages we have committed.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"messages_committed\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A counter of the bytes we have staged to upstream.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"bytes_staged\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        // A counter of the bytes we have committed.\n        // Never resets.\n        .with_column(\"bytes_committed\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n        .finish()\n});\n\n// Types of statistics (counter and various types of gauges), that have different semantics\n// when sources/sinks are reset.\n\n/// A trait that defines the semantics storage statistics are able to have\npub trait StorageMetric {\n    /// Summarizes a set of measurements into a single representative value.\n    /// Typically this function is used to summarize the measurements collected from each worker.\n    fn summarize&lt;'a, I&gt;(values: I) -&gt; Self\n    where\n        I: IntoIterator&lt;Item = &amp;'a Self&gt;,\n        Self: Sized + 'a;\n\n    /// Incorporate this value with another.\n    fn incorporate(&amp;mut self, other: Self, field_name: &amp;'static str);\n}\n\n/// A counter that never resets.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq, Default)]\npub struct Counter(u64);\n\nimpl StorageMetric for Counter",

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I found it. From load_generator.rs lines 107-109:

LoadGenerator::Counter { .. } => RelationDesc::builder()
    .with_column("counter", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))
    .finish(),

So the COUNTER load generator has exactly one column: `counter` of type Int64 (bigint), NOT NULL.

When you do CREATE TABLE employees_from_src FROM SOURCE emp_src, the table gets the same schema as the source. So employees_from_src has one column: `counter` (bigint, not null).
</details></think>

---


`employees_from_src` gets its schema from the **LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER** source. That generator is defined to have a single column:

- **`counter`** — `bigint` (Int64), NOT NULL

So the schema of `employees_from_src` is:

| Column   | Type   | Nullable |
|----------|--------|----------|
| `counter` | bigint | NOT NULL |

There are no `employee`, `salary`, or `type` columns on this table. In the test, those names only appear in:

1. **`emp_view`** — a view that does `SELECT counter AS salary, 'full_time'::text AS type FROM employees_from_src` (so it exposes `salary` and `type`).
2. **`manual_exceptions`** — a separate table with `(employee text, salary int, type text)` that we insert into, using literals like `'Alice'` and `'Bob'` for `employee`.

So the “employee, salary, type” example is only in the **target** table and the **view’s** column names; the source-backed table `employees_from_src` is just the single column `counter`.

---

_**User**_

Ok so I understand it:

- we set up a source which is a single column counter
- Then we create a view from that source where we say the salary is the counter and the job type is full time
- We try to insert from that view into another table, which is invalid
- If we do the same thing with an actual base table, it is fine

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is summarizing their understanding. I will check if they have one small inaccuracy: the view is created from a *table* that was created FROM the source (employees_from_src), not directly from the source. So the chain is: view → table (employees_from_src) → source. The view selects from employees_from_src, not from the source directly. I'll confirm their understanding and just clarify that one detail so the "transitive" part is clear.
</details></think>

---


Yes, that’s the idea. One small detail to keep in mind:

- The view **doesn’t** select from the source directly. It selects from **`employees_from_src`**, which is the **table** created with `CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE emp_src`. So the chain is:  
  **view → table (source-export) → source.**

That’s why the error is about *transitive* dependencies: the view itself is fine, but its dependency chain eventually reaches a source (via the source-export table), and that’s what makes the INSERT invalid.

So in short:

1. Source = single column `counter`.
2. Table `employees_from_src` = that source’s data (same one column).
3. View = “salary” = counter, “type” = `'full_time'` from `employees_from_src`.
4. INSERT into another table from that view → **invalid** (selection transitively refers to a source).
5. Same pattern but from a normal base table (no source in the chain) → **valid**.

---

_**User**_

So we should also get an error if we try to select from the source directly to insert right? Since that cuts the transitivity out?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is asking: if we do INSERT INTO ... SELECT ... FROM source_directly (i.e. from the source itself, not from a table or view that depends on it), we should also get an error, right? Because we're selecting from the source directly - we're not going through a view or table, we're cutting out the "transitivity" and just referring to the source.

Yes - if you SELECT from a source directly in an INSERT INTO ... SELECT ..., the selection's dependencies include the source itself. So validate_read_dependencies would be called with the source's id, and in the match we have:

```rust
Source | Secret | Connection => false,
```

So when the dependency is a Source (directly), valid is false and we return InvalidTableMutationSelection. So yes, selecting directly from the source in INSERT INTO ... SELECT ... should also error.

And actually - we already have that in the test! I will check the test file:

From what I wrote earlier:
```
# Same error when selecting directly from source-export table
statement error invalid selection
INSERT INTO manual_exceptions (employee, salary, type)
SELECT 'Bob', counter, 'part_time' FROM employees_from_src LIMIT 1
```

That's from the source-export *table* (employees_from_src), not from the source (emp_src) itself. So we have:
1. INSERT from view (transitive: view → table → source) - error
2. INSERT from source-export table (direct dependency on source-export table, which is invalid) - error
3. We do NOT have: INSERT from source directly (SELECT ... FROM emp_src)

So the user is asking: what about SELECT ... FROM emp_src in the INSERT? That would also be invalid - we're selecting from the source directly. So yes, we should get the same error. And we could add that case to the test to make it complete. I will confirm: if the SELECT in INSERT INTO t SELECT ... FROM emp_src, then selection.depends_on() would include the source emp_src. validate_read_dependencies(catalog, source_id) would be called. The entry would be a Source, so we'd hit Source => false and return the error. Yes.

So the user is correct - we should also get an error when selecting from the source directly. And we could add that test case. I'll confirm and optionally add it to the test file.
</details></think>

---


Selecting directly from the source in an INSERT should also error: the selection would depend on the source, and the validator treats `Source` as invalid (`Source | Secret | Connection => false`).

Adding a test case that inserts by selecting directly from the source:

---


Yes. Selecting directly from the source in an INSERT is also invalid, and the validator treats it the same way: the selection depends on a `Source`, so it returns the same error.

I added a third failing case to the test that does `INSERT INTO ... SELECT ... FROM emp_src` (directly from the source). The test now covers:

1. **Via view** — transitive: view → source-export table → source  
2. **Via source-export table** — direct dependency on the table backed by the source  
3. **Via source** — direct dependency on the source (no transitivity)

---

_**User**_

ERROR:  column "counter" does not exist ?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is getting an error "column 'counter' does not exist" when running the test. This might be happening when selecting from the source directly - perhaps when you CREATE SOURCE ... FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, the source doesn't expose a column named "counter" in the same way, or the way you reference it in a SELECT is different.

I will check - when you create a source from LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, you get a source. When you do CREATE TABLE t FROM SOURCE s, the table gets the schema from the source (one column: counter). But when you SELECT from the source directly, maybe the source is a multi-output source or the column has a different name in that context?

Actually wait - LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER is a single-output source. So when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, you get a source that produces... I will check. In the load generator code, Counter has vec![] for views() - so it doesn't have sub-views like Auction does. So the source itself might expose the data directly. But the relation desc for Counter has column "counter". So the source should have a column named "counter".

Unless - when you create a source from LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER without "FOR TABLES", it might create something different. I will look at how single-output vs multi-output sources work. For COUNTER, views() returns vec![], so there are no sub-tables. So when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, what do you get? Maybe you get a source that you can't SELECT from directly - you have to create a table from it first? So SELECT * FROM emp_src might not work, or the column might be different?

I will search for how COUNTER source is queried - do you select from the source or from a table created from the source?
</details></think>

---


Checking how the COUNTER source is exposed when selected directly:

        "contents": "            CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { generator, options } =&gt; {\n                f.write_str(\"LOAD GENERATOR \");\n                f.write_node(generator);\n                if !options.is_empty() {\n                    f.write_str(\" (\");\n                    f.write_node(&amp;display::comma_separated(options));\n                    f.write_str(\")\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\nimpl_display_t!(CreateSourceConnection);\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum LoadGenerator {\n    Clock,\n    Counter,\n    Marketing,\n    Auction,\n    Datums,\n    Tpch,\n    KeyValue,\n}\n\nimpl AstDisplay for LoadGenerator {\n    fn fmt&lt;W: fmt::Write&gt;(&amp;self, f: &amp;mut AstFormatter&lt;W&gt;) {\n        match self {\n            Self::Counter =&gt; f.write_str(\"COUNTER\"),\n            Self::Clock =&gt; f.write_str(\"CLOCK\"),\n            Self::Marketing =&gt; f.write_str(\"MARKETING\"),\n            Self::Auction =&gt; f.write_str(\"AUCTION\"),\n            Self::Datums =&gt; f.write_str(\"DATUMS\"),\n            Self::Tpch =&gt; f.write_str(\"TPCH\"),\n            Self::KeyValue =&gt; f.write_str(\"KEY VALUE\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\nimpl_display!(LoadGenerator);\n\nimpl LoadGenerator {\n    /// Corresponds with the same mapping on the &#96;LoadGenerator&#96; enum defined in\n    /// src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs, but re-defined here for\n    /// cases where we only have the AST representation. This can be removed once\n    /// the &#96;ast_rewrite_sources_to_tables&#96; migration is removed.\n    pub fn schema_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        match self {\n            LoadGenerator::Counter =&gt; \"counter\",\n            LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; \"clock\",\n            LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; \"marketing\",\n            LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; \"auction\",\n            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; \"datums\",\n            LoadGenerator::Tpch =&gt; \"tpch\",\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue =&gt; \"key_value\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]\npub enum LoadGeneratorOptionName {\n    ScaleFactor,\n    TickInterval,\n    AsOf",
        "signatures": {},

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/environmentd/tests/sql.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 2095,
            "column": 15
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 2143,
            "column": 68
          }
        },
        "contents": "fn test_load_generator() {\n    let server = test_util::TestHarness::default()\n        .unsafe_mode()\n        .start_blocking();\n    server.enable_feature_flags(&amp;[\n        \"enable_create_table_from_source\",\n        \"enable_load_generator_counter\",\n    ]);\n    let mut client = server.connect(postgres::NoTls).unwrap();\n\n    client\n        .batch_execute(\"CREATE SOURCE counter FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (TICK INTERVAL '1ms')\")\n        .unwrap();\n\n    client\n        .batch_execute(\"CREATE TABLE counter_tbl FROM SOURCE counter\")\n        .unwrap();\n\n    let row = client\n        .query_one(\"SELECT count(*), mz_now()::text FROM counter_tbl\", &amp;[])\n        .unwrap();\n    let initial_count: i64 = row.get(0);\n    let timestamp_millis: String = row.get(1);\n    let timestamp_millis: i64 = timestamp_millis.parse().unwrap();\n    const WAIT: i64 = 100;\n    let next = timestamp_millis + WAIT;\n    let expect = initial_count + WAIT;\n    Retry::default()\n        .retry(|_| {\n            let row = client\n                .query_one(\n                    &amp;format!(\"SELECT count(*) FROM counter_tbl AS OF AT LEAST {next}\"),\n                    &amp;[],\n                )\n                .unwrap();\n            let count: i64 = row.get(0);\n            if count &lt; expect {\n                Err(format!(\"expected {expect}, got {count}\"))\n            } else {\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        })\n        .unwrap();\n}\n\n#[mz_ore::test]\nfn test_support_user_permissions() {\n    let server = test_util::TestHarness::default().start_blocking();",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 84,
            "column": 26
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 131,
            "column": 5
          }
        },
        "contents": "            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; {\n                let mut desc = RelationDesc::builder()\n                    .with_column(\"rowid\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false));\n                let typs = SqlScalarType::enumerate();\n                let mut names = BTreeSet::new();\n                for typ in typs {\n                    // Cut out variant information from the debug print.\n                    let mut name = format!(\"_{:?}\", typ)\n                        .split(' ')\n                        .next()\n                        .unwrap()\n                        .to_lowercase();\n                    // Incase we ever have multiple variants of the same type, create\n                    // unique names for them.\n                    while names.contains(&amp;name) {\n                        name.push('_');\n                    }\n                    names.insert(name.clone());\n                    desc = desc.with_column(name, typ.clone().nullable(true));\n                }\n                desc.finish()\n            }\n            LoadGenerator::Counter { .. } =&gt; RelationDesc::builder()\n                .with_column(\"counter\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))\n                .finish(),\n            LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n            LoadGenerator::Tpch { .. } =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue(KeyValueLoadGenerator { include_offset, .. }) =&gt; {\n                let mut desc = RelationDesc::builder()\n                    .with_column(\"partition\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n                    .with_column(\"value\", SqlScalarType::Bytes.nullable(false));\n\n                if let Some(offset_name) = include_offset.as_deref() {\n                    desc = desc.with_column(offset_name, SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false));\n                }\n                desc.finish()\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn timestamp_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC.clone()\n    }\n\n    fn connection_id(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;CatalogItemId&gt; {\n        None\n    }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 481,
            "column": 50
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 552,
            "column": 28
          }
        },
        "contents": "#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum LoadGeneratorOutput {\n    // Used for outputting to the primary source output\n    Default,\n    Auction(AuctionView),\n    Marketing(MarketingView),\n    Tpch(TpchView),\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum AuctionView {\n    Organizations,\n    Users,\n    Accounts,\n    Auctions,\n    Bids,\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum MarketingView {\n    Customers,\n    Impressions,\n    Clicks,\n    Leads,\n    Coupons,\n    ConversionPredictions,\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialOrd, Ord)]\npub enum TpchView {\n    Supplier,\n    Part,\n    Partsupp,\n    Customer,\n    Orders,\n    Lineitem,\n    Nation,\n    Region,\n}\n\n// TODO(roshan): Remove these str mappings once the migration\n// &#96;ast_rewrite_create_load_gen_subsource_details&#96; is removed,\n// since these are only needed for the migration but should not otherwise be used.\nimpl From&lt;&amp;str&gt; for AuctionView {\n    fn from(s: &amp;str) -&gt; Self {\n        match s {\n            \"organizations\" =&gt; AuctionView::Organizations,\n            \"users\" =&gt; AuctionView::Users,\n            \"accounts\" =&gt; AuctionView::Accounts,\n            \"auctions\" =&gt; AuctionView::Auctions,\n            \"bids\" =&gt; AuctionView::Bids,\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"unexpected load generator output name: {}\", s),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;&amp;str&gt; for MarketingView {\n    fn from(s: &amp;str) -&gt; Self {\n        match s {\n            \"customers\" =&gt; MarketingView::Customers,\n            \"impressions\" =&gt; MarketingView::Impressions,\n            \"clicks\" =&gt; MarketingView::Clicks,\n            \"leads\" =&gt; MarketingView::Leads,\n            \"coupons\" =&gt; MarketingView::Coupons,\n            \"conversion_predictions\" =&gt; MarketingView::ConversionPredictions,\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"unexpected load generator output name: {}\", s),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;&amp;str&gt; for TpchView",

        "contents": "    let (desc, data_source) = match progress_subsource {\n        Some(name) =&gt; {\n            let DeferredItemName::Named(name) = name else {\n                sql_bail!(\"[internal error] progress subsource must be named during purification\");\n            };\n            let ResolvedItemName::Item { id, .. } = name else {\n                sql_bail!(\"[internal error] invalid target id\");\n            };\n\n            let details = match external_connection {\n                GenericSourceConnection::Kafka(ref c) =&gt; {\n                    SourceExportDetails::Kafka(KafkaSourceExportDetails {\n                        metadata_columns: c.metadata_columns.clone(),\n                    })\n                }\n                GenericSourceConnection::LoadGenerator(ref c) =&gt; match c.load_generator {\n                    LoadGenerator::Auction\n                    | LoadGenerator::Marketing\n                    | LoadGenerator::Tpch { .. } =&gt; SourceExportDetails::None,\n                    LoadGenerator::Counter { .. }\n                    | LoadGenerator::Clock\n                    | LoadGenerator::Datums\n                    | LoadGenerator::KeyValue(_) =&gt; {\n                        SourceExportDetails::LoadGenerator(LoadGeneratorSourceExportDetails {\n                            output: LoadGeneratorOutput::Default,\n                        })\n                    }\n                },\n                GenericSourceConnection::Postgres(_)\n                | GenericSourceConnection::MySql(_)\n                | GenericSourceConnection::SqlServer(_) =&gt; SourceExportDetails::None,\n            };\n\n            let data_source =",

        "contents": "impl Generator for Counter {\n    fn by_seed(\n        &amp;self,\n        _now: NowFn,\n        _seed: Option&lt;u64&gt;,\n        resume_offset: MzOffset,\n    ) -&gt; Box&lt;dyn Iterator&lt;Item = (LoadGeneratorOutput, Event&lt;Option&lt;MzOffset&gt;, (Row, Diff)&gt;)&gt;&gt; {\n        let max_cardinality = self.max_cardinality;\n\n        Box::new(\n            (resume_offset.offset..)\n                .map(move |offset| {\n                    let retraction = match max_cardinality {\n                        // At offset &#96;max&#96; we must start retracting the value of &#96;offset - max&#96;. For\n                        // example if max_cardinality is 2 then the collection should contain:\n                        // (1, 0, +1)\n                        // (2, 1, +1)\n                        // (1, 2, -1) &lt;- Here offset becomes &gt;= max and we retract the value that was\n                        // (3, 2, +1)    emitted at (offset - max), which equals (offset - max + 1).\n                        // (2, 3, -1)\n                        // (4, 3, +1)\n                        Some(max) if offset &gt;= max =&gt; {\n                            let retracted_value = i64::try_from(offset - max + 1).unwrap();\n                            let row = Row::pack_slice(&amp;[Datum::Int64(retracted_value)]);\n                            Some((\n                                LoadGeneratorOutput::Default,\n                                Event::Message(MzOffset::from(offset), (row, Diff::MINUS_ONE)),\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        _ =&gt; None,\n                    };\n\n                    let inserted_value = i64::try_from(offset + 1).unwrap();\n                    let row = Row::pack_slice(&amp;[Datum::Int64(inserted_value)]);\n                    let insertion = [\n                        (",

        "contents": ")\n            }\n            LOAD =&gt; {\n                self.expect_keyword(GENERATOR)?;\n                let generator = match self.expect_one_of_keywords(&amp;[\n                    CLOCK, COUNTER, MARKETING, AUCTION, TPCH, DATUMS, KEY,\n                ])? {\n                    CLOCK =&gt; LoadGenerator::Clock,\n                    COUNTER =&gt; LoadGenerator::Counter,\n                    AUCTION =&gt; LoadGenerator::Auction,\n                    TPCH =&gt; LoadGenerator::Tpch,\n                    DATUMS =&gt; LoadGenerator::Datums,\n                    MARKETING =&gt; LoadGenerator::Marketing,\n                    KEY =&gt; {\n                        self.expect_keyword(VALUE)?;\n                        LoadGenerator::KeyValue\n                    }\n                    _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n                };\n                let options = if self.consume_token(&amp;Token::LParen) {\n                    let options =\n                        self.parse_comma_separated(Parser::parse_load_generator_option)?;\n                    self.expect_token(&amp;Token::RParen)?;\n                    options\n                } else {\n                    vec![]\n                };\n                Ok(CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { generator, options })\n            }\n            _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_pg_connection_option(&amp;mut self) -&gt;",

        "contents": " {\n            let (desc, output) = match purified_export.details {\n                PurifiedExportDetails::LoadGenerator { table, output } =&gt; (table, output),\n                _ =&gt; unreachable!(\"purified export details must be load generator\"),\n            };\n            // We only determine the table description for multi-output load generator sources here,\n            // whereas single-output load generators will have their relation description\n            // determined during statment planning as envelope and format options may affect their\n            // schema.\n            if let Some(desc) = desc {\n                let (gen_columns, gen_constraints) = scx.relation_desc_into_table_defs(&amp;desc)?;\n                match columns {\n                    TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_) =&gt; unreachable!(),\n                    TableFromSourceColumns::NotSpecified =&gt; {\n                        *columns = TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(gen_columns);\n                        *constraints = gen_constraints;\n                    }\n                    TableFromSourceColumns::Named(_) =&gt; {\n                        sql_bail!(\"columns cannot be named for multi-output load generator sources\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            let details = SourceExportStatementDetails::LoadGenerator { output };\n            with_options.push(TableFromSourceOption {\n                name: TableFromSourceOptionName::Details,\n                value: Some(WithOptionValue::Value(Value::String(hex::encode(\n                    details.into_proto().encode_to_vec(),\n                )))),\n            })\n        }\n        PurifiedExportDetails::Kafka {} =&gt; {\n            // NOTE: Kafka tables have their 'schemas' purified into the statement inside the",

        "contents": "impl SourceConnection for LoadGeneratorSourceConnection {\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        \"load-generator\"\n    }\n\n    fn external_reference(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;&amp;str&gt; {\n        None\n    }\n\n    fn default_key_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        match &amp;self.load_generator {\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue(_) =&gt; {\n                // &#96;\"key\"&#96; is overridden by the key_envelope in planning.\n                RelationDesc::builder()\n                    .with_column(\"key\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n                    .finish()\n            }\n            _ =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn default_value_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        match &amp;self.load_generator {\n            LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n            LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; RelationDesc::builder()\n                .with_column(\n                    \"time\",\n                    SqlScalarType::TimestampTz { precision: None }.nullable(false),\n                )\n                .finish(),\n            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; {\n                let mut desc =",

        "contents": "        GenericSourceConnection::LoadGenerator(load_gen_connection) =&gt; {\n            let reference_client = SourceReferenceClient::LoadGenerator {\n                generator: &amp;load_gen_connection.load_generator,\n            };\n            retrieved_source_references = reference_client.get_source_references().await?;\n\n            let requested_exports = retrieved_source_references\n                .requested_source_exports(requested_references.as_ref(), &amp;unresolved_source_name)?;\n            // There should be exactly one source_export returned\n            let export = requested_exports.into_element();\n            PurifiedSourceExport {\n                external_reference: export.external_reference,\n                details: PurifiedExportDetails::LoadGenerator {\n                    table: export\n                        .meta\n                        .load_generator_desc()\n                        .expect(\"is loadgen\")\n                        .clone(),\n                    output: export\n                        .meta\n                        .load_generator_output()\n                        .expect(\"is loadgen\")\n                        .clone(),\n                },\n            }\n        }\n        GenericSourceConnection::Kafka(kafka_conn) =&gt; {\n            let reference_client = SourceReferenceClient::Kafka",

        "contents": "        let (new_progress_name, new_progress_stmt, new_source_name, new_source_stmt) =\n            match connection {\n                connection @ (CreateSourceConnection::Postgres { .. }\n                | CreateSourceConnection::MySql { .. }\n                | CreateSourceConnection::SqlServer { .. }\n                | CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator {\n                    generator:\n                        LoadGenerator::Tpch | LoadGenerator::Auction | LoadGenerator::Marketing,\n                    ..\n                }) =&gt; {\n                    // Assert the expected state of the source\n                    assert_eq!(col_names, &amp;[]);\n                    assert_eq!(key_constraint, None);\n                    assert_eq!(format, None);\n                    assert_eq!(envelope, None);\n                    assert_eq!(include_metadata, &amp;[]);\n                    assert_eq!(external_references, None);\n\n                    // This is a dummy replacement statement for the source object of multi-output\n                    // sources. It is describing the query &#96;TABLE source_name&#96;. This ensures that\n                    // whoever was used to run select queries against the &#96;source_name&#96; + \"_progress\"\n                    // object still gets the same data after the migration. This switch does\n                    // changes the schema of the object with &#96;source_item.id&#96; but because we're turning\n                    // it into a view, which is not durable, it's ok. We'll never open a persist shard\n                    // for this global id anymore.\n                    let dummy_source_stmt = Statement::CreateView(CreateViewStatement {\n                        if_exists: IfExistsBehavior::Error,",
