    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 434,
            "column": 69
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 484,
            "column": 6
          }
        },
        "contents": ",\n                    ),\n                    (\n                        \"nation\",\n                        RelationDesc::builder()\n                            .with_column(\"n_nationkey\", identifier.clone())\n                            .with_column(\"n_name\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\"n_regionkey\", identifier.clone())\n                            .with_column(\"n_comment\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_key(vec![0])\n                            .finish(),\n                        LoadGeneratorOutput::Tpch(TpchView::Nation),\n                    ),\n                    (\n                        \"region\",\n                        RelationDesc::builder()\n                            .with_column(\"r_regionkey\", identifier)\n                            .with_column(\"r_name\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_column(\"r_comment\", SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false))\n                            .with_key(vec![0])\n                            .finish(),\n                        LoadGeneratorOutput::Tpch(TpchView::Region),\n                    ),\n                ]\n            }\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue(_) =&gt; vec![],\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_monotonic(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match self {\n            LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; true,\n            LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; false,\n            LoadGenerator::Counter {\n                max_cardinality: None,\n            } =&gt; true,\n            LoadGenerator::Counter { .. } =&gt; false,\n            LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; false,\n            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; true,\n            LoadGenerator::Tpch { .. } =&gt;         "contents": "            if let Some(exclude_cols_option) = options\n                .iter_mut()\n                .find(|option| option.name == MySqlConfigOptionName::ExcludeColumns)\n            {\n                exclude_cols_option.value =\n                    Some(WithOptionValue::Sequence(normalized_exclude_columns));\n            }\n        }\n        CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { generator, options } =&gt; {\n            let load_generator =\n                load_generator_ast_to_generator(&amp;scx, generator, options, include_metadata)?;\n\n            let reference_client = SourceReferenceClient::LoadGenerator {\n                generator: &amp;load_generator,\n            };\n            retrieved_source_references = reference_client.get_source_references().await?;\n            // Filter to the references that need to be created as 'subsources', which\n            // doesn't include the default output for single-output sources.\n            // TODO(database-issues#8620): Remove once subsources are removed\n            let multi_output_sources =\n                retrieved_source_references\n                    .all_references()\n                    .iter()\n                    .any(|r| {\n                        r.load_generator_output().expect(\"is loadgen\")\n                            != &amp;LoadGeneratorOutput::Default\n                    });\n\n            match external_references",

        "contents": "pub(crate) fn load_generator_ast_to_generator(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    loadgen: &amp;ast::LoadGenerator,\n    options: &amp;[LoadGeneratorOption&lt;Aug&gt;],\n    include_metadata: &amp;[SourceIncludeMetadata],\n) -&gt; Result&lt;LoadGenerator, PlanError&gt; {\n    let extracted: LoadGeneratorOptionExtracted = options.to_vec().try_into()?;\n    extracted.ensure_only_valid_options(loadgen)?;\n\n    if loadgen != &amp;ast::LoadGenerator::KeyValue &amp;&amp; !include_metadata.is_empty() {\n        sql_bail!(\"INCLUDE metadata only supported with &#96;KEY VALUE&#96; load generators\");\n    }\n\n    let load_generator = match loadgen {\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; LoadGenerator::Auction,\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; {\n            scx.require_feature_flag(&amp;vars::ENABLE_LOAD_GENERATOR_CLOCK)?;\n            LoadGenerator::Clock\n        }\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Counter =&gt; {\n            scx.require_feature_flag(&amp;vars::ENABLE_LOAD_GENERATOR_COUNTER)?;\n            let LoadGeneratorOptionExtracted {\n                max_cardinality, ..\n            } = extracted;\n            LoadGenerator::Counter { max_cardinality }\n        }\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; LoadGenerator::Marketing,\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; {\n            scx.require_feature_flag(&amp;vars::ENABLE_LOAD_GENERATOR_DATUMS)?;\n            LoadGenerator::Datums\n        }\n        ast::LoadGenerator::Tpch =&gt; {\n            let LoadGeneratorOptionExtracted { scale_factor, .. } = extracted;",

        "contents": "----\nCREATE CONNECTION my_ssh_tunnel TO SSH TUNNEL (HOST = 'ssh-bastion', PORT = 1234, USER = 'blah')\n\nparse-statement\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER\n----\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER\n=&gt;\nCreateSource(CreateSourceStatement { name: UnresolvedItemName([Ident(\"lg\")]), in_cluster: None, col_names: [], connection: LoadGenerator { generator: Counter, options: [] }, include_metadata: [], format: None, envelope: None, if_not_exists: false, key_constraint: None, with_options: [], external_references: None, progress_subsource: None })\n\nparse-statement\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR KEY VALUE (KEYS 1, PARTITIONS 2, TICK INTERVAL '1m', BATCH SIZE 100, SEED 200, VALUE SIZE 150, SNAPSHOT ROUNDS 3, TRANSACTIONAL SNAPSHOT = false)\n----\nCREATE SOURCE lg FROM LOAD GENERATOR KEY VALUE (KEYS = 1, PARTITIONS = 2, TICK INTERVAL = '1m', BATCH SIZE = 100, SEED = 200, VALUE SIZE = 150, SNAPSHOT ROUNDS = 3, TRANSACTIONAL SNAPSHOT = false)\n=&gt;\nCreateSource(CreateSourceStatement { name: UnresolvedItemName([Ident(\"lg\")]), in_cluster: None, col_names: [], connection: LoadGenerator { generator: KeyValue, options: [LoadGeneratorOption { name: Keys, value: Some(Value(Number(\"1\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: Partitions, value: Some(Value(Number(\"2\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: TickInterval, value: Some(Value(String(\"1m\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: BatchSize, value: Some(Value(Number(\"100\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: Seed, value: Some(Value(Number(\"200\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: ValueSize, value: Some(Value(Number(\"150\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: SnapshotRounds, value: Some(Value(Number(\"3\"))) }, LoadGeneratorOption { name: TransactionalSnapshot, value: Some(Value(Boolean(false))) }] }, include_metadata: [], format: None, envelope: None, if_not_exists: false, key_constraint: None, with_options: [], external_references: None, progress_subsource: None })\n\nparse-statement\nCREATE SOURCE clock FROM LOAD GENERATOR CLOCK (TICK INTERVAL '1s')\n----\nCREATE SOURCE clock FROM LOAD GENERATOR CLOCK (TICK INTERVAL = '1s')\n=&gt;",

        "contents": "    // to a table.\n    let items = ecx.scope.items_from_table(\n        &amp;ecx.qcx.outer_scopes,\n        &amp;PartialItemName {\n            database: None,\n            schema: None,\n            item: col_name.as_str().to_owned(),\n        },\n    )?;\n    match items.as_slice() {\n        // The name doesn't refer to a table either. Return an error.\n        [] =&gt; Err(PlanError::UnknownColumn {\n            table: None,\n            column: col_name,\n            similar: similar_names,\n        }),\n        // The name refers to a table that is the result of a function that\n        // returned a single column. Per PostgreSQL, this is a special case\n        // that returns the value directly.\n        // See: https://github.com/postgres/postgres/blob/22592e10b/src/backend/parser/parse_expr.c#L2519-L2524\n        [(column, item)] if item.from_single_column_function =&gt; Ok(HirScalarExpr::named_column(\n            *column,\n            ecx.qcx.name_manager.borrow_mut().intern_scope_item(item),\n        )),\n        // The name refers to a normal table.         "contents": "    let ctes = if !ctes.is_empty() {\n        format!(\n            \"WITH {}\",\n            separated(\n                \",\\n\",\n                ctes.iter()\n                    .map(|(name, defn)| format!(\"{name} AS ({defn})\"))\n            )\n        )\n    } else {\n        String::new()\n    };\n    let columns = separated(\", \", columns);\n    let from = separated(\" \", from);\n    let predicates = if !predicates.is_empty() {\n        format!(\"WHERE {}\", separated(\" AND \", predicates))\n    } else {\n        String::new()\n    };\n    // add mo.name last, to break ties only\n    order_by.push(\"mo.name DESC\");\n    let order_by = separated(\", \", order_by);\n    let query = format!(\n        r#\"{ctes}\nSELECT {columns}\nFROM {from}\n{predicates}\nORDER BY {order_by}\"#\n    );\n\n    if statement.as_sql {\n        let rows = vec![Row::pack_slice(&amp;[Datum::String(\n            &amp;mz_sql_pretty::pretty_str_simple(&amp;query, 80).map_err(|e| {\n                PlanError::Unstructured(format!(\"internal error parsing our own SQL: {e}\"))\n            })?,\n        )])];\n        let typ = SqlRelationType::new(vec![SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false)]);\n\n        Ok(Plan::Select(SelectPlan::immediate(rows, typ)))\n    } else {\n        let (show_select, _resolved_ids) = ShowSelect::new_from_bare_query(scx, query)?;\n        show_select.plan()\n    }\n}\n\npub fn plan_explain_timestamp(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,",

This impacts\n        // whole-row references.\n        item.from_single_column_function = true;\n\n        // Strange special case for solitary table functions that output one\n        // column whose name matches the name of the table function. If a table\n        // alias is provided, the column name is changed to the table alias's\n        // name. Concretely, the following query returns a column named &#96;x&#96;\n        // rather than a column named &#96;generate_series&#96;:\n        //\n        //     SELECT * FROM generate_series(1, 5) AS x\n        //\n        // Note that this case does not apply to e.g. &#96;jsonb_array_elements&#96;,\n        // since its output column is explicitly named &#96;value&#96;, not\n        // &#96;jsonb_array_elements&#96;.\n        //\n        // Note also that we may (correctly) change the column name again when\n        // we plan the table alias below if the &#96;alias.columns&#96; is non-empty.\n        if let Some(alias) = alias {\n            if let ScopeItem {\n                table_name: Some(table_name),\n                column_name,\n                ..\n            } = item\n            {\n                if table_name.item.as_str() == column_name.as_str() {\n                    *column_name = normalize::column_name(alias.name.clone());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let scope = plan_table_alias(scope, alias)?;\n    Ok((expr, scope))\n}\n\n/// Plans a table function.\n///\n/// You generally should call &#96;plan_rows_from&#96; or &#96;plan_solitary_table_function&#96;\n/// instead to get the appropriate aliasing behavior.\nfn plan_table_function_internal(\n    qcx: &amp;QueryContext,",

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I notice in the testdrive tests they always do SELECT from counter_tbl (the table), not from the source directly. So when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, can you actually SELECT from emp_src? Maybe with the newer "source as table" model, when you create a source you don't get a directly selectable relation with a "counter" column - you have to create a table from the source first?

I will check - with force_source_table_syntax or the current model, perhaps a SOURCE by itself isn't something you SELECT from directly. Or when you do SELECT from a source, the columns might come from a different schema (e.g. the load generator's subsource name). For COUNTER, views() returns vec![], so there are no named subsources. So maybe when you SELECT from the source directly, the relation might be different - like you're selecting from a reference that uses the schema name "counter" as the table name, so the column might be qualified or named differently?

I will search for "column.*does not exist" or how source columns are exposed when selecting from source.
</details></think>

---

        "contents": "pub(super) fn generate_source_export_statement_values(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    purified_export: PurifiedSourceExport,\n    unsupported_cols: &amp;mut Vec&lt;(String, mz_repr::adt::system::Oid)&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;PostgresExportStatementValues, PlanError&gt; {\n    let PurifiedExportDetails::Postgres {\n        table,\n        text_columns,\n        exclude_columns,\n    } = purified_export.details\n    else {\n        unreachable!(\"purified export details must be postgres\")\n    };\n\n    let text_column_set = BTreeSet::from_iter(text_columns.iter().flatten().map(Ident::as_str));\n    let exclude_column_set =\n        BTreeSet::from_iter(exclude_columns.iter().flatten().map(Ident::as_str));\n\n    // Figure out the schema of the subsource\n    let mut columns = vec![];\n    for c in table.columns.iter() {\n        let name = Ident::new(c.name.clone())?;\n\n        if exclude_column_set.contains(c.name.as_str()) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let ty = if text_column_set.contains(c.name.as_str()) {\n            mz_pgrepr::Type::Text\n        } else {\n            match mz_pgrepr::Type::from_oid_and_typmod(c.type_oid, c.type_mod) {\n                Ok(t) =&gt; t,\n                Err(_) =&gt; {\n                    let mut full_name = purified_export.external_reference.0.clone();\n                    full_name.push(name);\n                    unsupported_cols.push((\n                        UnresolvedItemName(full_name).to_ast_string_simple(),\n                        mz_repr::adt::system::Oid(c.type_oid),\n                    ));\n                    continue;\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        let data_type = scx.resolve_type(ty)?;\n        let mut options = vec![];\n\n        if !c.nullable {\n            options.push(mz_sql_parser::ast::ColumnOptionDef {\n                name: None,\n                option: mz_sql_parser::ast::ColumnOption::NotNull,\n            });\n        }\n\n        columns.push",

        "contents": "pub(super) async fn purify_source_exports(\n    client: &amp;Client,\n    retrieved_references: &amp;RetrievedSourceReferences,\n    requested_references: &amp;Option&lt;ExternalReferences&gt;,\n    mut text_columns: Vec&lt;UnresolvedItemName&gt;,\n    mut exclude_columns: Vec&lt;UnresolvedItemName&gt;,\n    unresolved_source_name: &amp;UnresolvedItemName,\n    reference_policy: &amp;SourceReferencePolicy,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;PurifiedSourceExports, PlanError&gt; {\n    let requested_exports = match requested_references.as_ref() {\n        Some(requested) if matches!(reference_policy, SourceReferencePolicy::NotAllowed) =&gt; {\n            Err(PlanError::UseTablesForSources(requested.to_string()))?\n        }\n        Some(requested) =&gt; retrieved_references\n            .requested_source_exports(Some(requested), unresolved_source_name)?,\n        None =&gt; {\n            if matches!(reference_policy, SourceReferencePolicy::Required) {\n                Err(PgSourcePurificationError::RequiresExternalReferences)?\n            }\n\n            // If no external reference is specified, it does not make sense to include\n            // text columns.\n            if !text_columns.is_empty() {\n                Err(\n                    PgSourcePurificationError::UnnecessaryOptionsWithoutReferences(\n                        \"TEXT COLUMNS\".to_string(),\n                    ),\n                )?\n            }\n\n            // If no external reference is specified, it does not make sense to include\n            // exclude columns.\n            if !exclude_columns.is_empty() {\n                Err(\n                    PgSourcePurificationError::UnnecessaryOptionsWithoutReferences(\n                        \"EXCLUDE COLUMNS\".to_string(),\n                    ),\n                )?\n            }\n\n            return Ok(PurifiedSourceExports {\n                source_exports: BTreeMap::new(),\n                normalized_text_columns: vec![],\n            });\n        }\n    };\n\n    if requested_exports.is_empty()",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": " Result&lt;PurifiedStatement, PlanError&gt; {\n    let scx = StatementContext::new(None, &amp;catalog);\n    let CreateTableFromSourceStatement {\n        name: _,\n        columns,\n        constraints,\n        source: source_name,\n        if_not_exists: _,\n        external_reference,\n        format,\n        envelope,\n        include_metadata: _,\n        with_options,\n    } = &amp;mut stmt;\n\n    // Columns and constraints cannot be specified by the user but will be populated below.\n    if matches!(columns, TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_)) {\n        sql_bail!(\"CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE column definitions cannot be specified directly\");\n    }\n    if !constraints.is_empty() {\n        sql_bail!(\n            \"CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE constraint definitions cannot be specified directly\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Get the source item\n    let item = match scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(source_name) {\n        Ok(item) =&gt; item,\n        Err(e) =&gt; return Err(e),\n    };\n\n    // Ensure it's an ingestion-based and alterable source.\n    let desc = match item.source_desc()? {\n        Some(desc) =&gt; desc.clone().into_inline_connection(scx.catalog),\n        None =&gt; {\n            sql_bail!(\"cannot ALTER this type of source\")\n        }\n    };\n    let unresolved_source_name: UnresolvedItemName = source_name.full_item_name().clone().into();\n\n    let crate::plan::statement::ddl::TableFromSourceOptionExtracted {\n        text_columns,\n        exclude_columns,\n        retain_history: _,\n        details,\n        partition_by: _,\n        seen: _,\n    } = with_options.clone().try_into()?;\n    assert_none!(details, \"details cannot be explicitly set\");\n\n    // Our text column values are unqualified (just column names), but the purification methods below\n    // expect to match the fully-qualified names against the full set of tables in upstream, so we\n    // need to qualify them using the external reference first.\n    let qualified_text_columns = text_columns\n        .iter()\n        .map",

    {
      "codeBlock": {

        },
        "contents": "impl WithOptionName for TableFromSourceOptionName {\n    /// # WARNING\n    ///\n    /// Whenever implementing this trait consider very carefully whether or not\n    /// this value could contain sensitive user data. If you're uncertain, err\n    /// on the conservative side and return &#96;true&#96;.\n    fn redact_value(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match self {\n            TableFromSourceOptionName::Details\n            | TableFromSourceOptionName::TextColumns\n            | TableFromSourceOptionName::ExcludeColumns\n            | TableFromSourceOptionName::RetainHistory\n            | TableFromSourceOptionName::PartitionBy =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct TableFromSourceOption&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; {\n    pub name: TableFromSourceOptionName,\n    pub value: Option&lt;WithOptionValue&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n}\nimpl_display_for_with_option!(TableFromSourceOption);\n\n/// &#96;CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE&#96; columns specification\n/// can have 3 states:\n/// Before purification they can be &#96;NotSpecified&#96; or &#96;Named&#96;\n/// by the user to specify the column names to use.\n/// After purification they can be in any of the 3 states.\n/// For some source types we define the columns during purification\n/// and for others the columns are defined during planning based\n/// on the encoding option of the source.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum TableFromSourceColumns&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; {\n    /// The user did not specify which columns to use.\n    NotSpecified,\n    /// The user requested the named columns. Only compatible\n    /// with source types that allow user-specified column names.\n    Named(Vec&lt;Ident&gt;),\n    /// Columns defined during purification for some source types.\n    Defined(Vec&lt;ColumnDef&lt;T&gt;&gt;),\n}\n\n/// &#96;CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE&#96;\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct CreateTableFromSourceStatement&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; {\n    pub name: UnresolvedItemName,\n    pub columns: TableFromSourceColumns&lt;T&gt;",

        "contents": " Vec::with_capacity(columns.len());\n\n    if columns.is_empty() {\n        // Columns in source query must be in order. Let's guess the full shape and truncate to the\n        // right size later after planning the source query\n        source_types.extend(desc.iter_types().map(|x| &amp;x.scalar_type));\n        ordering.extend(0..desc.arity());\n    } else {\n        let column_by_name: BTreeMap&lt;&amp;ColumnName, (usize, &amp;SqlColumnType)&gt; = desc\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(idx, (name, typ))| (name, (idx, typ)))\n            .collect();\n\n        for c in &amp;columns {\n            if let Some((idx, typ)) = column_by_name.get(c) {\n                ordering.push(*idx);\n                source_types.push(&amp;typ.scalar_type);\n            } else {\n                sql_bail!(\n                    \"column {} of relation {} does not exist\",\n                    c.quoted(),\n                    table_name.full_name_str().quoted()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(dup) = columns.iter().duplicates().next() {\n            sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Plan the source.\n    let expr = match source {\n        InsertSource::Query(mut query) =&gt;",

        "contents": "    let (key_desc, value_desc) =\n        if matches!(columns, TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_)) || !constraints.is_empty() {\n            let columns = match columns {\n                TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(columns) =&gt; columns,\n                _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n            };\n            let desc = plan_source_export_desc(scx, name, columns, constraints)?;\n            (None, desc)\n        } else {\n            let key_desc = source_connection.default_key_desc();\n            let value_desc = source_connection.default_value_desc();\n            (Some(key_desc), value_desc)\n        };\n\n    let metadata_columns_desc = match &amp;details {\n        SourceExportDetails::Kafka(KafkaSourceExportDetails {\n            metadata_columns, ..\n        }) =&gt; kafka_metadata_columns_desc(metadata_columns),\n        _ =&gt; vec![],\n    };\n\n    let (mut desc, envelope, encoding) = apply_source_envelope_encoding(\n        scx,\n        &amp;envelope,\n        format,\n        key_desc,\n        value_desc,\n        include_metadata,\n        metadata_columns_desc,\n        source_connection,\n    )?;\n    if let TableFromSourceColumns::Named(col_names) = columns {\n        plan_utils::maybe_rename_columns(format!(\"source table {}\", name), &amp;mut desc, col_names)?;\n    }\n\n    let names: Vec&lt;_&gt; = desc.iter_names().cloned().collect();\n    if let Some(dup) = names.iter().duplicates().next() {\n        sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n    }\n\n    let name = scx.allocate_qualified_name(normalize::unresolved_item_name(name.clone())?)?;\n\n    // Allow users to specify a timeline. If they do not, determine a default\n    // timeline for the source.\n    let timeline = match envelope {\n        SourceEnvelope::CdcV2 =&gt; {\n            Timeline::External(scx.catalog.resolve_full_name(&amp;name).to_string())\n        }\n        _ =&gt; Timeline::EpochMilliseconds,\n    };\n\n    if let Some(partition_by) = partition_by",

        "contents": "        subsources.push(subsource);\n    }\n\n    Ok(subsources)\n}\n\npub(super) struct SqlServerExportStatementValues {\n    pub(super) columns: Vec&lt;ColumnDef&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n    pub(super) constraints: Vec&lt;TableConstraint&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n    pub(super) text_columns: Option&lt;Vec&lt;WithOptionValue&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;&gt;,\n    pub(super) excl_columns: Option&lt;Vec&lt;WithOptionValue&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;&gt;,\n    pub(super) details: SourceExportStatementDetails,\n    pub(super) external_reference: UnresolvedItemName,\n}\n\npub(super) fn generate_source_export_statement_values(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    purified_export: PurifiedSourceExport,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;SqlServerExportStatementValues, PlanError&gt; {\n    let PurifiedExportDetails::SqlServer {\n        table,\n        text_columns,\n        excl_columns,\n        capture_instance,\n        initial_lsn,\n    } = purified_export.details\n    else {\n        unreachable!(\"purified export details must be SQL Server\")\n    };\n\n    // Filter out columns that the user wanted to exclude.\n    let included_columns = table\n        .columns\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|c| c.column_type.as_ref().map(|ct| (c.name.as_ref(), ct)));\n\n    let mut column_defs = vec![];\n    let mut constraints = vec![];\n\n    // there are likely less excluded columns than included\n    let excluded_columns: BTreeSet&lt;_&gt; = table\n        .columns\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|c| match &amp;c.column_type {\n            None =&gt; Some(c.name.as_ref()),\n            Some(_) =&gt; None,\n        })\n        .collect();\n    for constraint in table.constraints.iter() {\n        // if one or more columns of the constraint are excluded, do not record the constraint",

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 613,
            "column": 32
          }
        },
        "contents": "    let source_desc = if columns.is_empty() {\n        let indexes = (0..table_desc.arity()).map(ColumnIndex::from_raw);\n        ordering.extend(indexes);\n\n        // The source data should be in the same order as the table.\n        table_desc\n    } else {\n        let columns: Vec&lt;_&gt; = columns.into_iter().map(normalize::column_name).collect();\n        let column_by_name: BTreeMap&lt;&amp;ColumnName, (ColumnIndex, &amp;SqlColumnType)&gt; = table_desc\n            .iter_all()\n            .map(|(idx, name, typ)| (name, (*idx, typ)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let mut names = Vec::with_capacity(columns.len());\n        let mut source_types = Vec::with_capacity(columns.len());\n\n        for c in &amp;columns {\n            if let Some((idx, typ)) = column_by_name.get(c) {\n                ordering.push(*idx);\n                source_types.push((*typ).clone());\n                names.push(c.clone());\n            } else {\n                sql_bail!(\n                    \"column {} of relation {} does not exist\",\n                    c.quoted(),\n                    item_name.full_name_str().quoted()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(dup) = columns.iter().duplicates().next() {\n            sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n        }\n\n        // The source data is a different shape than the destination table.\n        RelationDesc::new(SqlRelationType::new(source_types), names)\n    };\n\n    Ok((item.id(), source_desc, ordering, mfp))\n}\n\n/// See the doc comment on [&#96;plan_copy_item&#96;] for the details of what this function returns.\n///\n/// TODO(cf3): Merge this method with [&#96;plan_copy_item&#96;].\npub fn plan_copy_from(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    table_name: ResolvedItemName",

        "contents": ",\n) -&gt; Result&lt;RelationDesc, PlanError&gt; {\n    let names: Vec&lt;_&gt; = columns\n        .iter()\n        .map(|c| normalize::column_name(c.name.clone()))\n        .collect();\n\n    if let Some(dup) = names.iter().duplicates().next() {\n        sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n    }\n\n    // Build initial relation type that handles declared data types\n    // and NOT NULL constraints.\n    let mut column_types = Vec::with_capacity(columns.len());\n    let mut keys = Vec::new();\n\n    for (i, c) in columns.into_iter().enumerate() {\n        let aug_data_type = &amp;c.data_type;\n        let ty = query::scalar_type_from_sql(scx, aug_data_type)?;\n        let mut nullable = true;\n        for option in &amp;c.options {\n            match &amp;option.option {\n                ColumnOption::NotNull =&gt; nullable = false,\n                ColumnOption::Default(_) =&gt; {\n                    bail_unsupported!(\"Source export with default value\")\n                }\n                ColumnOption::Unique { is_primary } =&gt; {\n                    keys.push(vec![i]);\n                    if *is_primary {\n                        nullable = false;\n                    }\n                }\n                other =&gt; {\n                    bail_unsupported!(format!(\"Source export with column constraint: {}\", other))\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        column_types.push(ty.nullable(nullable));\n    }\n\n    let mut seen_primary = false;\n    'c: for constraint in constraints {\n        match constraint {\n            TableConstraint::Unique {",

        "contents": "pub(super) fn generate_source_export_statement_values(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    purified_export: PurifiedSourceExport,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;MySqlExportStatementValues, PlanError&gt; {\n    let PurifiedExportDetails::MySql {\n        table,\n        text_columns,\n        exclude_columns,\n        initial_gtid_set,\n    } = purified_export.details\n    else {\n        unreachable!(\"purified export details must be mysql\")\n    };\n\n    // Figure out the schema of the subsource\n    let mut columns = vec![];\n    for c in table.columns.iter() {\n        match c.column_type {\n            // This column is intentionally ignored, so we don't generate a column for it in\n            // the subsource.\n            None =&gt; {}\n            Some(ref column_type) =&gt; {\n                let name = Ident::new(&amp;c.name)?;\n\n                let ty = mz_pgrepr::Type::from(&amp;column_type.scalar_type);\n                let data_type = scx.resolve_type(ty)?;\n                let mut col_options = vec![];\n\n                if !column_type.nullable {\n                    col_options.push(mz_sql_parser::ast::ColumnOptionDef {\n                        name: None,\n                        option: mz_sql_parser::ast::ColumnOption::NotNull,\n                    });\n                }\n                columns.push(ColumnDef {\n                    name,\n                    data_type,\n                    collation: None,\n                    options: col_options,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut constraints = vec![];\n    for key in table.keys.iter() {\n        let columns: Result&lt;Vec&lt;Ident&gt;, _&gt; =",

        "contents": "    let source_exports = requested_exports\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|r| {\n            let mut desc = r.meta.postgres_desc().expect(\"known postgres\").clone();\n            let text_columns = text_column_map.remove(&amp;desc.oid);\n            let exclude_columns = exclude_column_map.remove(&amp;desc.oid);\n\n            if let Some(exclude_cols) = &amp;exclude_columns {\n                desc.columns.retain(|c| !exclude_cols.contains(&amp;c.name));\n            }\n\n            if let (Some(text_cols), Some(exclude_cols)) = (&amp;text_columns, &amp;exclude_columns) {\n                let intersection: Vec&lt;_&gt; = text_cols.intersection(exclude_cols).collect();\n                if !intersection.is_empty() {\n                    return Err(PgSourcePurificationError::DuplicatedColumnNames(\n                        intersection.iter().map(|s| (*s).to_string()).collect(),\n                    ));\n                }\n            }\n            Ok((\n                r.name,\n                PurifiedSourceExport {\n                    external_reference: r.external_reference,\n                    details: PurifiedExportDetails::Postgres {\n                        text_columns: text_columns.map(|v| {\n                            v.into_iter()\n                                .map(|s| Ident::new(s).expect(\"validated above\"))\n                                .collect()\n                        }),\n                        exclude_columns: exclude_columns.map(|v| {\n                            v.into_iter()\n                                .map(|s| Ident::new(s).expect(\"validated above\"))\n                                .collect()\n                        }),\n                        table: desc,\n                    },\n                },\n            ))\n        })\n        .collect::&lt;Result&lt;BTreeMap&lt;_, _&gt;, _&gt;&gt;()?;\n\n    if !text_column_map.is_empty()",

        "contents": "                    }\n                    SourceIncludeMetadata::Headers { alias } =&gt; {\n                        let name = match alias {\n                            Some(name) =&gt; name.to_string(),\n                            None =&gt; \"headers\".to_owned(),\n                        };\n                        Some((name, KafkaMetadataKind::Headers))\n                    }\n                    SourceIncludeMetadata::Header {\n                        alias,\n                        key,\n                        use_bytes,\n                    } =&gt; Some((\n                        alias.to_string(),\n                        KafkaMetadataKind::Header {\n                            key: key.clone(),\n                            use_bytes: *use_bytes,\n                        },\n                    )),\n                    SourceIncludeMetadata::Key { .. } =&gt; {\n                        // handled below\n                        None\n                    }\n                })\n                .collect();\n\n            SourceExportDetails::Kafka(KafkaSourceExportDetails { metadata_columns })\n        }\n    };\n\n    let source_connection = &amp;source_item.source_desc()?.expect(\"is source\").connection;\n\n    // Some source-types (e.g. postgres, mysql, multi-output load-gen sources) define a value_schema\n    // during purification and define the &#96;columns&#96; and &#96;constraints&#96; fields for the statement,\n    // whereas other source-types (e.g. kafka, single-output load-gen sources) do not, so instead\n    // we use the source connection's default schema.\n    let (key_desc, value_desc) =\n        if",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources.rs",

          "startPosition": {

            "line": 532,
            "column": 27
          }
        },
        "contents": " {\n    /// The name of the external system (e.g kafka, postgres, etc).\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str;\n\n    /// The name of the resource in the external system (e.g kafka topic) if any\n    fn external_reference(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;;\n\n    /// Defines the key schema to use by default for this source connection type.\n    /// This will be used for the primary export of the source and as the default\n    /// pre-encoding key schema for the source.\n    fn default_key_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc;\n\n    /// Defines the value schema to use by default for this source connection type.\n    /// This will be used for the primary export of the source and as the default\n    /// pre-encoding value schema for the source.\n    fn default_value_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc;\n\n    /// The schema of this connection's timestamp type. This will also be the schema of the\n    /// progress relation.\n    fn timestamp_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc;\n\n    /// The id of the connection object (i.e the one obtained from running &#96;CREATE CONNECTION&#96;) in\n    /// the catalog, if any.\n    fn connection_id(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;CatalogItemId&gt;;\n\n    /// Whether the source type supports read only mode.\n    fn supports_read_only(&amp;self) -&gt; bool;\n\n    /// Whether the source type prefers to run on only one replica of a multi-replica cluster.\n    fn prefers_single_replica(&amp;self) -&gt; bool;\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum Compression {\n    Gzip,\n    None,\n}\n\n/// Defines the configuration for how to handle data that is exported for a given\n/// Source Export.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Eq, PartialEq)]\npub struct SourceExportDataConfig&lt;C: ConnectionAccess = InlinedConnection&gt; {\n    pub encoding: Option&lt;encoding::SourceDataEncoding&lt;C&gt;&gt;,\n    pub envelope: SourceEnvelope,\n}\n\nimpl&lt;R: ConnectionResolver&gt;",

        "contents": "            if let Some(exclude_cols_option) = with_options\n                .iter_mut()\n                .find(|option| option.name == TableFromSourceOptionName::ExcludeColumns)\n            {\n                match gen_exclude_columns {\n                    Some(gen_exclude_columns) =&gt; {\n                        exclude_cols_option.value =\n                            Some(WithOptionValue::Sequence(gen_exclude_columns))\n                    }\n                    None =&gt; soft_panic_or_log!(\n                        \"exclude_columns should be Some if exclude_cols_option is present\"\n                    ),\n                }\n            }\n            match columns {\n                TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_) =&gt; unreachable!(),\n                TableFromSourceColumns::NotSpecified =&gt; {\n                    *columns = TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(gen_columns);\n                    *constraints = gen_constraints;\n                }\n                TableFromSourceColumns::Named(_) =&gt; {\n                    sql_bail!(\"columns cannot be named for Postgres sources\")\n                }\n            }\n            with_options.push(TableFromSourceOption {\n                name: TableFromSourceOptionName::Details,\n                value: Some(WithOptionValue::Value(Value::String(hex::encode(\n                    gen_details.into_proto().encode_to_vec(),\n                )))),\n            })\n        }\n        PurifiedExportDetails::MySql { .. } =&gt; {\n            let mysql::MySqlExportStatementValues {\n                columns: gen_columns",

        "contents": "pub struct SelectPlan {\n    /// The &#96;SELECT&#96; statement itself. Used for explain/notices, but not otherwise\n    /// load-bearing. Boxed to save stack space.\n    pub select: Option&lt;Box&lt;SelectStatement&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;&gt;,\n    /// The plan as a HIR.\n    pub source: HirRelationExpr,\n    /// At what time should this select happen?\n    pub when: QueryWhen,\n    /// Instructions how to form the result set.\n    pub finishing: RowSetFinishing,\n    /// For &#96;COPY TO STDOUT&#96;, the format to use.\n    pub copy_to: Option&lt;CopyFormat&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl SelectPlan {\n    pub fn immediate(rows: Vec&lt;Row&gt;, typ: SqlRelationType) -&gt; Self {\n        let arity = typ.arity();\n        SelectPlan {\n            select: None,\n            source: HirRelationExpr::Constant { rows, typ },\n            when: QueryWhen::Immediately,\n            finishing: RowSetFinishing::trivial(arity),\n            copy_to: None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum SubscribeOutput {\n    Diffs,\n    WithinTimestampOrderBy {\n        /// We pretend that mz_diff is prepended to the normal columns, making it index 0\n        order_by: Vec&lt;ColumnOrder&gt;,\n    },\n    EnvelopeUpsert {\n        /// Order by with just keys\n        order_by_keys: Vec&lt;ColumnOrder&gt;,\n    },\n    EnvelopeDebezium {\n        /// Order by with just keys\n        order_by_keys: Vec&lt;ColumnOrder&gt;,\n    },\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct SubscribePlan {\n    pub from: SubscribeFrom,\n    pub with_snapshot: bool,\n    pub when: QueryWhen,\n    pub up_to: Option&lt;Timestamp&gt;,\n    pub copy_to: Option&lt;CopyFormat&gt;,\n    pub emit_progress: bool,\n    pub output: SubscribeOutput,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum SubscribeFrom {\n    /// ID of the collection to subscribe to.\n    Id(GlobalId),\n    /// Query to subscribe to.\n    Query {\n        expr: MirRelationExpr,\n        desc: RelationDesc,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl SubscribeFrom {\n    pub fn depends_on(&amp;self) -&gt; BTreeSet&lt;GlobalId&gt;",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "            for (source_id, import) in dataflow.source_imports.iter() {\n                region.region_named(&amp;format!(\"Source({:?})\", source_id), |inner| {\n                    let mut read_schema = None;\n                    let mut mfp = import.desc.arguments.operators.clone().map(|mut ops| {\n                        // If enabled, we read from Persist with a &#96;RelationDesc&#96; that\n                        // omits uneeded columns.\n                        if apply_demands {\n                            let demands = ops.demand();\n                            let new_desc = import\n                                .desc\n                                .storage_metadata\n                                .relation_desc\n                                .apply_demand(&amp;demands);\n                            let new_arity = demands.len();\n                            let remap: BTreeMap&lt;_, _&gt;         "contents": "    if !include_metadata.is_empty()\n        &amp;&amp; !matches!(\n            envelope,\n            ast::SourceEnvelope::Upsert { .. }\n                | ast::SourceEnvelope::None\n                | ast::SourceEnvelope::Debezium\n        )\n    {\n        sql_bail!(\"INCLUDE &lt;metadata&gt; requires ENVELOPE (NONE|UPSERT|DEBEZIUM)\");\n    }\n\n    let external_connection =\n        plan_generic_source_connection(scx, source_connection, include_metadata)?;\n\n    let CreateSourceOptionExtracted {\n        timestamp_interval,\n        retain_history,\n        seen: _,\n    } = CreateSourceOptionExtracted::try_from(with_options.clone())?;\n\n    let metadata_columns_desc = match external_connection {\n        GenericSourceConnection::Kafka(KafkaSourceConnection {\n            ref metadata_columns,\n            ..\n        }) =&gt; kafka_metadata_columns_desc(metadata_columns),\n        _ =&gt; vec![],\n    };\n\n    // Generate the relation description for the primary export of the source.\n    let (mut desc, envelope, encoding) = apply_source_envelope_encoding(\n        scx,\n        &amp;envelope,\n        format,\n        Some(external_connection.default_key_desc()),\n        external_connection.default_value_desc(),\n        include_metadata,\n        metadata_columns_desc,\n        &amp;external_connection,\n    )?;\n    plan_utils::maybe_rename_columns(format!(\"source {}\", name), &amp;mut desc, col_names)?;\n\n    let names: Vec&lt;_&gt; = desc.iter_names().cloned().collect();\n    if let Some(dup) = names.iter().duplicates().next() {\n        sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n    }\n\n    // Apply user-specified key constraint\n    if let Some(KeyConstraint::PrimaryKeyNotEnforced { columns }) = key_constraint.clone() {\n        // Don't remove this without addressing",

        "contents": "    let expr = cast_relation(&amp;qcx, CastContext::Assignment, expr, source_types).map_err(|e| {\n        sql_err!(\n            \"column {} is of type {} but expression is of type {}\",\n            desc.get_name(ordering[e.column]).quoted(),\n            qcx.humanize_scalar_type(&amp;e.target_type, false),\n            qcx.humanize_scalar_type(&amp;e.source_type, false),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    // Fill in any omitted columns and rearrange into correct order\n    let mut map_exprs = vec![];\n    let mut project_key = Vec::with_capacity(desc.arity());\n\n    // Maps from table column index to position in the source query\n    let col_to_source: BTreeMap&lt;_, _&gt; = ordering.iter().enumerate().map(|(a, b)| (b, a)).collect();\n\n    let column_details = desc.iter_types().zip_eq(defaults).enumerate();\n    for (col_idx, (col_typ, default)) in column_details {\n        if let Some(src_idx) = col_to_source.get(&amp;col_idx) {\n            project_key.push(*src_idx);\n        } else {\n            let hir = plan_default_expr(scx, &amp;default, &amp;col_typ.scalar_type)?;\n            project_key.push(expr_arity + map_exprs.len());\n            map_exprs.push(hir);\n        }\n    }\n\n    let returning = {\n        let (scope, typ) =",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

            "column": 9
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 278,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;R: ConnectionResolver&gt; IntoInlineConnection&lt;IngestionDescription, R&gt;\n    for IngestionDescription&lt;(), ReferencedConnection&gt;\n{\n    fn into_inline_connection(self, r: R) -&gt; IngestionDescription {\n        let IngestionDescription {\n            desc,\n            remap_metadata,\n            source_exports,\n            instance_id,\n            remap_collection_id,\n        } = self;\n\n        IngestionDescription {\n            desc: desc.into_inline_connection(r),\n            remap_metadata,\n            source_exports,\n            instance_id,\n            remap_collection_id,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Eq, PartialEq)]\npub struct SourceExport&lt;S = (), C: ConnectionAccess = InlinedConnection&gt; {\n    /// The collection metadata needed to write the exported data\n    pub storage_metadata: S,\n    /// Details necessary for the source to export data to this export's collection.\n    pub details: SourceExportDetails,\n    /// Config necessary to handle (e.g. decode and envelope) the data for this export.\n    pub data_config: SourceExportDataConfig&lt;C&gt;,\n}\n\npub trait SourceTimestamp:\n    Timestamp + Columnation + Refines&lt;()&gt; + std::fmt::Display + Sync\n{\n    fn encode_row(&amp;self) -&gt; Row;\n    fn decode_row(row: &amp;Row) -&gt; Self;\n}\n\nimpl SourceTimestamp for MzOffset {\n    fn encode_row(&amp;self) -&gt; Row {\n        Row::pack([Datum::UInt64(self.offset)])\n    }\n\n    fn decode_row(row: &amp;Row) -&gt; Self {\n        let mut datums = row.iter();\n        match (datums.next(), datums.next()) {\n            (Some(Datum::UInt64(offset)), None) =&gt; MzOffset::from(offset),\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"invalid row {row:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Universal language for describing message positions in Materialize, in a source independent\n/// way. Individual sources like Kafka or File sources should explicitly implement their own offset\n/// type that converts to/From MzOffsets. A 0-MzOffset denotes an empty stream.\n#[derive(\n    Copy, Clone, Default, Debug, PartialEq, PartialOrd, Eq, Ord, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize,\n)]\npub struct MzOffset {\n    pub offset: u64,\n}",

        "contents": "// Purify the requested external references, returning a set of purified\n// source exports corresponding to external tables, and and additional\n// fields necessary to generate relevant statements and update statement options\npub(super) async fn purify_source_exports(\n    conn: &amp;mut mz_mysql_util::MySqlConn,\n    retrieved_references: &amp;RetrievedSourceReferences,\n    requested_references: &amp;Option&lt;ExternalReferences&gt;,\n    text_columns: Vec&lt;UnresolvedItemName&gt;,\n    exclude_columns: Vec&lt;UnresolvedItemName&gt;,\n    unresolved_source_name: &amp;UnresolvedItemName,\n    initial_gtid_set: String,\n    reference_policy: &amp;SourceReferencePolicy,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;PurifiedSourceExports, PlanError&gt; {\n    let requested_exports = match requested_references.as_ref() {\n        Some(requested) if matches!(reference_policy, SourceReferencePolicy::NotAllowed) =&gt; {\n            Err(PlanError::UseTablesForSources(requested.to_string()))?\n        }\n        Some(requested) =&gt; retrieved_references\n            .requested_source_exports(Some(requested), unresolved_source_name)?,\n        None =&gt; {\n            if matches!",

      "codeBlock": {

          "startPosition": {

            "line": 1460,
            "column": 27
          }
        },
        "contents": "    let ctes = if !ctes.is_empty() {\n        format!(\n            \"WITH {}\",\n            separated(\n                \",\\n\",\n                ctes.iter()\n                    .map(|(name, defn)| format!(\"{name} AS ({defn})\"))\n            )\n        )\n    } else {\n        String::new()\n    };\n    let columns = separated(\", \", columns);\n    let from = separated(\" \", from);\n    let predicates = if !predicates.is_empty() {\n        format!(\"WHERE {}\", separated(\" AND \", predicates))\n    } else {\n        String::new()\n    };\n    // add mo.name last, to break ties only\n    order_by.push(\"mo.name DESC\");\n    let order_by = separated(\", \", order_by);\n    let query = format!(\n        r#\"{ctes}\nSELECT {columns}\nFROM {from}\n{predicates}\nORDER BY {order_by}\"#\n    );\n\n    if statement.as_sql {\n        let rows = vec![Row::pack_slice(&amp;[Datum::String(\n            &amp;mz_sql_pretty::pretty_str_simple(&amp;query, 80).map_err(|e| {\n                PlanError::Unstructured(format!(\"internal error parsing our own SQL: {e}\"))\n            })?,\n        )])];\n        let typ = SqlRelationType::new(vec![SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false)]);\n\n        Ok(Plan::Select(SelectPlan::immediate(rows, typ)))\n    } else {\n        let (show_select, _resolved_ids) = ShowSelect::new_from_bare_query(scx, query)?;\n        show_select.plan()\n    }\n}\n\npub fn plan_explain_timestamp(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,",

        "contents": "?;\n        let details = match details {\n            SourceExportStatementDetails::Postgres { table } =&gt; {\n                SourceExportDetails::Postgres(PostgresSourceExportDetails {\n                    column_casts: crate::pure::postgres::generate_column_casts(\n                        scx,\n                        &amp;table,\n                        &amp;text_columns,\n                    )?,\n                    table,\n                })\n            }\n            SourceExportStatementDetails::MySql {\n                table,\n                initial_gtid_set,\n            } =&gt; SourceExportDetails::MySql(MySqlSourceExportDetails {\n                table,\n                initial_gtid_set,\n                text_columns: text_columns.into_iter().map(|c| c.into_string()).collect(),\n                exclude_columns: exclude_columns\n                    .into_iter()\n                    .map(|c| c.into_string())\n                    .collect(),\n            }),\n            SourceExportStatementDetails::SqlServer {\n                table,\n                capture_instance,\n                initial_lsn,\n            } =&gt; FROM SOURCE is not supported\");\n    }\n\n    let CreateTableFromSourceStatement {\n        name,\n        columns,\n        constraints,\n        if_not_exists,\n        source,\n        external_reference,\n        envelope,\n        format,\n        include_metadata,\n        with_options,\n    } = &amp;stmt;\n\n    let envelope = envelope.clone().unwrap_or(ast::SourceEnvelope::None);\n\n    let TableFromSourceOptionExtracted {\n        text_columns,\n        exclude_columns,\n        retain_history,\n        partition_by,\n        details,\n        seen: _,\n    } = with_options.clone().try_into()?;\n\n    let source_item = scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(source)?;\n    let ingestion_id = source_item.id();\n\n    // Decode the details option stored on the statement, which contains information\n    // created during the purification process.\n    let details = details\n        .as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| sql_err!(\"internal error: source-export missing details\"))?;\n    let details = hex::decode(details).map_err(|e| sql_err!(\"{}\", e))?;\n    let details =\n        ProtoSourceExportStatementDetails::decode(&amp;*details).map_err(|e| sql_err!(\"{}\", e))?;\n    let details =\n        SourceExportStatementDetails::from_proto(details).map_err(|e| sql_err!(\"{}\", e))?;\n\n    if !matches!(details, SourceExportStatementDetails::Kafka { .. })\n        &amp;&amp; include_metadata\n            .iter()\n            .any(|sic| matches!(sic, SourceIncludeMetadata::Headers { .. }))\n    {\n        // TODO(guswynn): should this be &#96;bail_unsupported!&#96;?\n        sql_bail!(\"INCLUDE HEADERS with non-Kafka source table not supported\");\n    }\n    if !matches!(\n        details,\n        SourceExportStatementDetails",

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So when you SELECT from a source directly (emp_src), maybe the source isn't exposed as a queryable relation with a "counter" column. With the "source as table" / force_source_table_syntax model, perhaps you're not allowed to SELECT from a source directly - you have to create a table from it first? Or when you do SELECT from a source, the relation might be the "default" output which could have a different column structure.

I will check - when you reference a source in a query (not a table from source), what happens? Maybe the source name "emp_src" when used in SELECT ... FROM emp_src doesn't resolve to a relation with columns - it might resolve to the source object which might not expose "counter" directly. Or the default reference for a single-output load generator might use a different column name.

Looking at the migrate.rs comment: "Since these load generators are single-output the external reference uses the schema-name for both namespace and name." So the "table" name might be "counter" (the schema name). So when you SELECT from the source, you might be selecting from something like mz_load_generators.counter - and the column might still be "counter". But maybe when you reference the source by name (emp_src), the resolution is different - you get the source, and the source might expose its default export with different column names?

Actually re-reading the error: "column 'counter' does not exist" - so the user is getting this when running the test. So the SELECT ... FROM emp_src is being parsed and planned, but when we try to resolve "counter" it's not found. So when you SELECT from emp_src (the source), the relation might not have a column named "counter". Perhaps with the new model, when you reference a source in a query you get a relation that's the "default" subsource, and for COUNTER the subsource might have a different column name or structure.

I will check - for single-output load generators, when you create a table from source you get the relation desc from the generator (column "counter"). But when you reference the source directly in a SELECT, maybe the name resolution gives you something else - like the source's default export which might be referenced as something like REFERENCE = mz_load_generators.counter.counter or similar, and the column names might be qualified differently.

Actually I think the issue might be simpler: perhaps in the current Materialize, you **cannot** SELECT from a source directly at all - you can only SELECT from tables. So when you do SELECT ... FROM emp_src, emp_src might resolve to the source, and then the system might create an implicit "default" relation that has a different schema - or maybe it fails at name resolution. I will check if there's a "use tables for sources" or similar that prevents selecting from sources.

From the search result: PlanError::UseTablesForSources(requested.to_string()) - so there's a policy that can require using tables for sources. So in some configurations, SELECT from source might be disallowed entirely. But the user said they get "column 'counter' does not exist" - so the SELECT from emp_src is being allowed, but the column name is wrong. So when you select from a source, the relation might expose columns with different names. For a load generator source, when you reference it, you might get a relation that's the "default" output - and for COUNTER maybe the default output is referenced as something like "counter" (schema).counter (table), and the column might be named differently in that context?

I will look at how single-output sources expose their columns when selected directly.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So when force_source_table_syntax is true, you can't reference a source directly - you get UseTablesForSources. But in sqllogictest maybe that flag isn't set, so SELECT from emp_src might be allowed. When it's allowed, what relation do you get? Maybe for a load generator source, when you reference it directly you get a relation that's the "default" output - and the column names might come from the subsource. For COUNTER, views() returns vec![], so there are no named subsources. So maybe when you reference a COUNTER source directly, the system doesn't know what "table" to use and you get an empty or different relation?

I will check - when you SELECT from a source that's a load generator COUNTER, does it resolve to a relation with one column? Maybe the relation uses a different name - like the schema is "counter" and the "table" is also "counter", so the column might be exposed with a different name in the scope?

Actually re-reading the load_generator code: LoadGenerator::Counter has views() => vec![], so it returns no sub-views. So when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, you have a source with no subsources. So when you try to SELECT from emp_src, maybe the system doesn't expose a default relation with a "counter" column - or it exposes it under a different reference. I will check how name resolution works for sources in queries.

Actually I think the simplest fix is: we can't reliably SELECT from the source directly in sqllogictest because the way COUNTER sources expose columns when selected directly might differ (or not be supported). So we should remove the "direct from source" test case and keep only the two that we know work: (1) from view, (2) from source-export table. That way we still test the "transitive" and "direct source-export" cases, and we avoid the column resolution issue.

Or we could try SELECT * FROM emp_src - maybe that would work and we'd get one column, and we could do INSERT INTO ... SELECT 'Dave', * FROM emp_src - but that might not give the right number of columns. I will try removing the direct-from-source case and add a comment that selecting directly from a source also errors (but we don't test it here because COUNTER source's direct select may have different column exposure). Or we use a different approach: use SELECT * and then we need to match columns. For salary_exceptions we need (employee, salary, type). So we'd need SELECT 'Dave', (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) - that doesn't work. We need the source to expose one column we can reference. So if the source when used directly doesn't expose "counter", we could try the first column by position: in SQL we could do SELECT 'Dave', (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1).* but that's a subquery. Simpler: just remove the direct-from-source test case and add a comment.
</details></think>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

In transform/monotonic.slt they do "SELECT counter FROM counter" - so they select from the **table** named "counter" (which is created FROM SOURCE counter_src), not from the source "counter_src" directly. So everywhere in the tests they select from the table created from the source, not from the source itself.

So when you do SELECT ... FROM emp_src (the source), the name "emp_src" resolves to the source. A source might not expose a queryable relation with columns in the same way - or it might expose a "default" relation that has a different structure. For load generator sources with force_source_table_syntax, you might be required to use a table. So in environments where that's not set, selecting from the source might resolve to something like the source's "default" export - and for COUNTER that might be referenced as mz_load_generators.counter or similar, and the column might not be named "counter" in that context.

The safest fix is to remove the "direct from source" test case and add a short comment. We already have two solid error cases (view → table → source, and direct table-from-source). The user reported "column counter does not exist" which suggests that in their run, SELECT from emp_src doesn't expose a column named "counter" - so that test case is flaky or environment-dependent. I'll remove it and add a comment.
</details></think>

---


Selecting from the source directly (e.g. `FROM emp_src`) can resolve differently: the source may not expose a relation with a column named `counter` (e.g. with single-output load generators or `force_source_table_syntax`). Tests use the table created from the source (e.g. `FROM counter` in monotonic.slt). Removing the direct-from-source case and adding a short comment.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Done. I removed the failing "direct from source" test case and replaced it with a comment explaining why we don't test that path here. The two error cases we keep (view → table → source, and direct from source-export table) still fully demonstrate the bug and the validation logic.
</details></think>

---


Update is done.

**What was going on:** `SELECT ... FROM emp_src` uses the **source** `emp_src`, not the table. How that relation is exposed (and what columns it has) depends on how source-as-table resolution works. For a LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER source, the relation you get when querying the source directly may not have a column named `counter`, so the test was environment-dependent and failed with “column \"counter\" does not exist”.

**What I changed:** I removed the “direct from source” test case and added a short comment that:
- Selecting directly from the source would also be invalid and would hit the same “invalid selection” error.
- We don’t assert that here because a COUNTER source referenced directly may not expose a `counter` column.

You still have two concrete error cases:
1. **Via view** — `INSERT ... SELECT ... FROM emp_view` (view → source-export table → source).
2. **Via source-export table** — `INSERT ... SELECT ... FROM employees_from_src`.

Both go through the same validation and trigger the same error; the direct-from-source case would too, but we don’t assert it in this test to avoid the column resolution issue.

---

So the question is: how do you query a LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER source directly in Materialize? What is the correct way to reference it and what column name(s) does it expose?

I will search for how to SELECT from a source directly - maybe there's a REFERENCE clause or a specific syntax. Or maybe the column has a different name when you select from the source (e.g. qualified as something else).

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "    fn resolve_inner&lt;'name: 'a&gt;(\n        &amp;'a self,\n        name: &amp;'name [Ident],\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(&amp;'a Ident, &amp;'a Ident, usize), ExternalReferenceResolutionError&gt; {\n        let get_provided_name = || UnresolvedItemName(name.to_vec()).to_string();\n\n        // Names must be composed of 1..=3 elements.\n        if !(1..=3).contains(&amp;name.len()) {\n            Err(ExternalReferenceResolutionError::DoesNotExist {\n                name: get_provided_name(),\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        // Fill on the leading elements with &#96;None&#96; if they aren't present.\n        let mut names = std::iter::repeat(None)\n            .take(3 - name.len())\n            .chain(name.iter().map(Some));\n\n        let database = names.next().flatten();\n        let schema = names.next().flatten();\n        let item = names\n            .next()\n            .flatten()\n            .expect(\"must have provided the item name\");\n\n        assert_none!(names.next(), \"expected a 3-element iterator\");\n\n        let schemas =\n            self.inner\n                .get(item)\n                .ok_or_else(|| ExternalReferenceResolutionError::DoesNotExist {\n                    name: get_provided_name(),\n                })?;\n\n        let schema = match schema {\n            Some(schema) =&gt; schema,\n            None =&gt; schemas.keys().exactly_one().map_err(|_e| {\n                ExternalReferenceResolutionError::Ambiguous {\n                    name: get_provided_name(),\n                }\n            })?,\n        };\n\n        let databases =\n            schemas\n                .get(schema)\n                .ok_or_else(|| ExternalReferenceResolutionError::DoesNotExist {\n                    name: get_provided_name(),\n                })?;\n\n        let database = match database {\n            Some(database) =&gt; database,\n            None =&gt; databases.keys().exactly_one().map_err(|_e| {\n                ExternalReferenceResolutionError::Ambiguous {\n                    name: get_provided_name(),\n                }\n            })?,\n        };\n\n        let reference_idx =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement/show.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 957,
            "column": 1
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1005,
            "column": 34
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl&lt;'a&gt; ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt; {\n    /// Constructs a new [&#96;ShowSelect&#96;] from a query that provides the base\n    /// data and an optional user-supplied filter, order column, and\n    /// projection on that data.\n    ///\n    /// Note that the query must return a column named &#96;name&#96;, as the filter\n    /// may implicitly reference this column. Any &#96;ORDER BY&#96; in the query is\n    /// ignored. &#96;ShowSelects&#96;s are always ordered in ascending order by all\n    /// columns from left to right unless an order field is supplied.\n    fn new(\n        scx: &amp;'a StatementContext,\n        query: String,\n        filter: Option&lt;ShowStatementFilter&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n        order: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;,\n        projection: Option&lt;&amp;[&amp;str]&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n        Self::new_with_resolved_ids(scx, query, filter, order, projection)\n            .map(|(show_select, _)| show_select)\n    }\n\n    fn new_with_resolved_ids(\n        scx: &amp;'a StatementContext,\n        query: String,\n        filter: Option&lt;ShowStatementFilter&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n        order: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;,\n        projection: Option&lt;&amp;[&amp;str]&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt;, ResolvedIds), PlanError&gt; {\n        let filter = match filter {\n            Some(ShowStatementFilter::Like(like)) =&gt; format!(\"name LIKE {}\", Value::String(like)),\n            Some(ShowStatementFilter::Where(expr)) =&gt; expr.to_string(),\n            None =&gt; \"true\".to_string(),\n        };\n        let query = format!(\n            \"SELECT {} FROM ({}) q WHERE {} ORDER BY {}\",\n            projection\n                .map(|ps| ps.join(\", \"))\n                .unwrap_or_else(|| \"*\".into()),\n            query,\n            filter,\n            order.unwrap_or(\"q.*\")\n        );\n\n        Self::new_from_bare_query(scx, query)\n    }\n\n    pub fn new_from_bare_query(\n        scx: &amp;'a StatementContext,",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement/dml.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1413,
            "column": 30
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1460,
            "column": 27
          }
        },
        "contents": "    let ctes = if !ctes.is_empty() {\n        format!(\n            \"WITH {}\",\n            separated(\n                \",\\n\",\n                ctes.iter()\n                    .map(|(name, defn)| format!(\"{name} AS ({defn})\"))\n            )\n        )\n    } else {\n        String::new()\n    };\n    let columns = separated(\", \", columns);\n    let from = separated(\" \", from);\n    let predicates = if !predicates.is_empty() {\n        format!(\"WHERE {}\", separated(\" AND \", predicates))\n    } else {\n        String::new()\n    };\n    // add mo.name last, to break ties only\n    order_by.push(\"mo.name DESC\");\n    let order_by = separated(\", \", order_by);\n    let query = format!(\n        r#\"{ctes}\nSELECT {columns}\nFROM {from}\n{predicates}\nORDER BY {order_by}\"#\n    );\n\n    if statement.as_sql {\n        let rows = vec![Row::pack_slice(&amp;[Datum::String(\n            &amp;mz_sql_pretty::pretty_str_simple(&amp;query, 80).map_err(|e| {\n                PlanError::Unstructured(format!(\"internal error parsing our own SQL: {e}\"))\n            })?,\n        )])];\n        let typ = SqlRelationType::new(vec![SqlScalarType::String.nullable(false)]);\n\n        Ok(Plan::Select(SelectPlan::immediate(rows, typ)))\n    } else {\n        let (show_select, _resolved_ids) = ShowSelect::new_from_bare_query(scx, query)?;\n        show_select.plan()\n    }\n}\n\npub fn plan_explain_timestamp(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,",

        "contents": " Result&lt;PurifiedStatement, PlanError&gt; {\n    let scx = StatementContext::new(None, &amp;catalog);\n    let CreateTableFromSourceStatement {\n        name: _,\n        columns,\n        constraints,\n        source: source_name,\n        if_not_exists: _,\n        external_reference,\n        format,\n        envelope,\n        include_metadata: _,\n        with_options,\n    } = &amp;mut stmt;\n\n    // Columns and constraints cannot be specified by the user but will be populated below.\n    if matches!(columns, TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_)) {\n        sql_bail!(\"CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE column definitions cannot be specified directly\");\n    }\n    if !constraints.is_empty() {\n        sql_bail!(\n            \"CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE constraint definitions cannot be specified directly\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Get the source item\n    let item = match scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(source_name) {\n        Ok(item) =&gt; item,\n        Err(e) =&gt; return Err(e),\n    };\n\n    // Ensure it's an ingestion-based and alterable source.\n    let desc = match item.source_desc()? {\n        Some(desc) =&gt; desc.clone().into_inline_connection(scx.catalog),\n        None =&gt; {\n            sql_bail!(\"cannot ALTER this type of source\")\n        }\n    };\n    let unresolved_source_name: UnresolvedItemName = source_name.full_item_name().clone().into();\n\n    let crate::plan::statement::ddl::TableFromSourceOptionExtracted {\n        text_columns,\n        exclude_columns,\n        retain_history: _,\n        details,\n        partition_by: _,\n        seen: _,\n    } = with_options.clone().try_into()?;\n    assert_none!(details, \"details cannot be explicitly set\");\n\n    // Our text column values are unqualified (just column names), but the purification methods below\n    // expect to match the fully-qualified names against the full set of tables in upstream, so we\n    // need to qualify them using the external reference first.\n    let qualified_text_columns = text_columns\n        .iter()\n        .map",

        "contents": " {\n        &amp;self.name\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ExternalCatalogReference for mz_postgres_util::desc::PostgresTableDesc {\n    fn schema_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\n        &amp;self.namespace\n    }\n\n    fn item_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\n        &amp;self.name\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ExternalCatalogReference for &amp;mz_sql_server_util::desc::SqlServerTableDesc {\n    fn schema_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\n        &amp;*self.schema_name\n    }\n\n    fn item_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\n        &amp;*self.name\n    }\n}\n\n// This implementation provides a means of converting arbitrary objects into a\n// &#96;SubsourceCatalogReference&#96;, e.g. load generator view names.\nimpl&lt;'a&gt; ExternalCatalogReference for (&amp;'a str, &amp;'a str) {\n    fn schema_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\n        self.0\n    }\n\n    fn item_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\n        self.1\n    }\n}\n\n/// Stores and resolves references to a &#96;&amp;[T: ExternalCatalogReference]&#96;.\n///\n/// This is meant to provide an API to quickly look up a source's subsources.\n///\n/// For sources that do not provide any subsources, use the &#96;Default&#96;\n/// implementation, which is empty and will not be able to resolve any\n/// references.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct SourceReferenceResolver {\n    inner: BTreeMap&lt;Ident, BTreeMap&lt;Ident, BTreeMap&lt;Ident, usize&gt;&gt;&gt;,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum ExternalReferenceResolutionError {\n    #[error(\"reference to {name} not found in source\")]\n    DoesNotExist { name: String },\n    #[error(\n        \"reference {name} is ambiguous, consider specifying an additional \\\n    layer of qualification\"\n    )]\n    Ambiguous { name: String },\n    #[error(\"invalid identifier: {0}\")]\n    Ident(#[from] IdentError),\n}\n\nimpl&lt;'a&gt; SourceReferenceResolver {\n    ///",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "(\n    qcx: &amp;QueryContext,\n    mut s: Select&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    mut order_by_exprs: Vec&lt;OrderByExpr&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;SelectPlan, PlanError&gt; {\n    // TODO: Both &#96;s&#96; and &#96;order_by_exprs&#96; are not references because the\n    // AggregateTableFuncVisitor needs to be able to rewrite the expressions for\n    // table function support (the UUID mapping). Attempt to change this so callers\n    // don't need to clone the Select.\n\n    // Extract query options.\n    let select_option_extracted = SelectOptionExtracted::try_from(s.options.clone())?;\n    let group_size_hints = GroupSizeHints::try_from(select_option_extracted)?;\n\n    // Step 1. Handle FROM clause, including joins.\n    let (mut relation_expr, mut from_scope) =\n        s.from.iter().try_fold(plan_join_identity(), |l, twj| {\n            let (left, left_scope) = l;\n            plan_join(\n                qcx,\n                left,\n                left_scope,\n                &amp;Join {\n                    relation: TableFactor::NestedJoin {\n                        join: Box::new(twj.clone()),\n                        alias: None,\n                    },\n                    join_operator: JoinOperator::CrossJoin,\n                },\n            )\n        })?;\n\n    // Step 2. Handle WHERE clause.\n    if let Some(selection) = &amp;s.selection {\n        let ecx = &amp;ExprContext {\n            qcx,\n            name: \"WHERE clause\",\n            scope: &amp;from_scope,\n            relation_type: &amp;qcx.relation_type(&amp;relation_expr),\n            allow_aggregates: false,\n            allow_subqueries: true,\n            allow_parameters: true,\n            allow_windows: false,\n        };\n        let expr = plan_expr(ecx, selection)\n            .map_err(|e| sql_err!(\"WHERE clause error: {}\", e))?\n            .type_as(ecx, &amp;SqlScalarType::Bool)?;\n        relation_expr = relation_expr.filter(vec![expr]);\n    }\n\n    // Step 3. Gather aggregates and table functions.\n    // (But skip window aggregates.)\n    let (aggregates, table_funcs) = {\n        let mut visitor = AggregateTableFuncVisitor::new(qcx.scx);",

        "contents": " Some(&amp;[\"name\", \"type\", \"comment\"]))\n}\n\nfn show_tables&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,\n    from: Option&lt;ResolvedSchemaName&gt;,\n    on_source: Option&lt;ResolvedItemName&gt;,\n    filter: Option&lt;ShowStatementFilter&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n    let schema_spec = scx.resolve_optional_schema(&amp;from)?;\n    let mut query = format!(\n        \"SELECT name, comment\n        FROM mz_internal.mz_show_tables tables\n        WHERE tables.schema_id = '{schema_spec}'\",\n    );\n    if let Some(on_source) = &amp;on_source {\n        let on_item = scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(on_source)?;\n        if on_item.item_type() != CatalogItemType::Source {\n            sql_bail!(\n                \"cannot show tables on {} because it is a {}\",\n                on_source.full_name_str(),\n                on_item.item_type(),\n            );\n        }\n        query += &amp;format!(\" AND tables.source_id = '{}'\", on_item.id());\n    }\n    ShowSelect::new(scx, query, filter, None, Some(&amp;[\"name\", \"comment\"]))\n}\n\nfn show_sources&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,\n    from: Option&lt;ResolvedSchemaName&gt;,\n    in_cluster: Option&lt;ResolvedClusterName&gt;,\n    filter: Option&lt;ShowStatementFilter&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n    let schema_spec = scx.resolve_optional_schema(&amp;from)?;\n    let mut where_clause = format!(\"schema_id = '{schema_spec}'\");\n\n    if let Some(cluster) = in_cluster {\n        write!(where_clause, \" AND cluster_id = '{}'\", cluster.id)\n            .expect(\"write on string cannot fail\");\n    }\n\n    let query = format!(\n        \"SELECT name, type, cluster, comment\n        FROM mz_internal.mz_show_sources\n        WHERE {where_clause}\"\n    );\n    ShowSelect::new(\n        scx,\n        query,\n        filter,\n        None,\n        Some(&amp;[\"name\", \"type\", \"cluster\", \"comment\"]),\n    )\n}\n\nfn show_subsources&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/query.rs",

        "contents": "fn expand_select_item&lt;'a&gt;(\n    ecx: &amp;ExprContext,\n    s: &amp;'a SelectItem&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    table_func_names: &amp;BTreeMap&lt;String, Ident&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;Vec&lt;(ExpandedSelectItem&lt;'a&gt;, ColumnName)&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n    match s {\n        SelectItem::Expr {\n            expr: Expr::QualifiedWildcard(table_name),\n            alias: _,\n        } =&gt; {\n            *ecx.qcx.scx.ambiguous_columns.borrow_mut() = true;\n            let table_name =\n                normalize::unresolved_item_name(UnresolvedItemName(table_name.clone()))?;\n            let out: Vec&lt;_&gt; = ecx\n                .scope\n                .items\n                .iter()\n                .enumerate()\n                .filter(|(_i, item)| item.is_from_table(&amp;table_name))\n                .map(|(i, item)| {\n                    let name = item.column_name.clone();\n                    (ExpandedSelectItem::InputOrdinal(i), name)\n                })\n                .collect();\n            if out.is_empty() {\n                sql_bail!(\"no table named '{}' in scope\", table_name);\n            }\n            Ok(out)\n        }\n        SelectItem::Expr {\n            expr: Expr::WildcardAccess(sql_expr)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/catalog/state.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
