          "endPosition": {
            "line": 2104,
            "column": 52
          }
        },
        "contents": "    pub fn resolve(\n        &amp;self,\n        get_schema_entries: fn(&amp;Schema) -&gt; &amp;BTreeMap&lt;String, CatalogItemId&gt;,\n        current_database: Option&lt;&amp;DatabaseId&gt;,\n        search_path: &amp;Vec&lt;(ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier, SchemaSpecifier)&gt;,\n        name: &amp;PartialItemName,\n        conn_id: &amp;ConnectionId,\n        err_gen: fn(String) -&gt; SqlCatalogError,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;CatalogEntry, SqlCatalogError&gt; {\n        // If a schema name was specified, just try to find the item in that\n        // schema. If no schema was specified, try to find the item in the connection's\n        // temporary schema. If the item is not found, try to find the item in every\n        // schema in the search path.\n        let schemas = match &amp;name.schema {\n            Some(schema_name) =&gt; {\n                match self.resolve_schema(\n                    current_database,\n                    name.database.as_deref(),\n                    schema_name,\n                    conn_id,\n                ) {\n                    Ok(schema) =&gt; vec![(schema.name.database.clone(), schema.id.clone())],\n                    Err(e) =&gt; return Err(e),\n                }\n            }\n            None =&gt; match self\n                .try_get_schema(\n                    &amp;ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier::Ambient,\n                    &amp;SchemaSpecifier::Temporary,\n                    conn_id,\n                )\n                .and_then(|schema| schema.items.get(&amp;name.item))\n            {\n                Some(id) =&gt; return Ok(self.get_entry(id)),\n                None =&gt; search_path.to_vec(),\n            },\n        };\n\n        for (database_spec, schema_spec) in &amp;schemas",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/pure/references.rs",
        "range": {

            "line": 516,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "        filtered\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(reference, alias)| {\n                let name = match alias {\n                    Some(alias_name) =&gt; {\n                        let partial = crate::normalize::unresolved_item_name(alias_name.clone())?;\n                        match partial.schema {\n                            Some(_) =&gt; alias_name.clone(),\n                            // In cases when a prefix is not provided for the aliased name\n                            // fallback to using the schema of the source with the given name\n                            None =&gt; super::source_export_name_gen(source_name, &amp;partial.item)?,\n                        }\n                    }\n                    None =&gt; {\n                        // Just use the item name for this reference and ensure it's created in\n                        // the current schema or the source's schema if provided, not mirroring\n                        // the schema of the reference.\n                        super::source_export_name_gen(source_name, reference.name())?\n                    }\n                };\n\n                Ok(RequestedSourceExport {\n                    external_reference: reference.external_reference()?,\n                    name,\n                    meta: reference,\n                })\n            })\n            .collect::&lt;Result&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;, PlanError&gt;&gt;()\n    }\n\n    pub(super) fn resolve_name(&amp;self, name: &amp;[Ident]) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;ReferenceMetadata, PlanError&gt; {\n        let idx = self.resolver.resolve_idx(name)?;\n        Ok(&amp;self.references[idx])\n    }\n\n    pub(super) fn all_references(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;Vec&lt;ReferenceMetadata&gt; {\n        &amp;self.references\n    }\n}",
        "signatures": {},

          {
            "text": "    pub(super) fn resolve_name(&amp;self, name: &amp;[Ident]) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;ReferenceMetadata, PlanError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 509

            "text": "        let idx = self.resolver.resolve_idx(name)?;",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement/show.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 500,

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 550,
            "column": 34
          }
        },
        "contents": "fn show_subsources&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,\n    from_schema: Option&lt;ResolvedSchemaName&gt;,\n    on_source: Option&lt;ResolvedItemName&gt;,\n    filter: Option&lt;ShowStatementFilter&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n    let mut query_filter = Vec::new();\n\n    if on_source.is_none() &amp;&amp; from_schema.is_none() {\n        query_filter.push(\"subsources.id NOT LIKE 's%'\".into());\n        let schema_spec = scx.resolve_active_schema().map(|spec| spec.clone())?;\n        query_filter.push(format!(\"subsources.schema_id = '{schema_spec}'\"));\n    }\n\n    if let Some(on_source) = &amp;on_source {\n        let on_item = scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(on_source)?;\n        if on_item.item_type() != CatalogItemType::Source {\n            sql_bail!(\n                \"cannot show subsources on {} because it is a {}\",\n                on_source.full_name_str(),\n                on_item.item_type(),\n            );\n        }\n        query_filter.push(format!(\"sources.id = '{}'\", on_item.id()));\n    }\n\n    if let Some(schema) = from_schema {\n        let schema_spec = schema.schema_spec();\n        query_filter.push(format!(\"subsources.schema_id = '{schema_spec}'\"));\n    }\n\n    // TODO(database-issues#8322): this looks in both directions for subsources as long as\n    // progress collections still exist\n    let query = format!(\n        \"SELECT DISTINCT\n            subsources.name AS name,\n            subsources.type AS type\n        FROM\n            mz_sources AS subsources\n            JOIN mz_internal.mz_object_dependencies deps ON (subsources.id = deps.object_id OR subsources.id = deps.referenced_object_id)\n            JOIN mz_sources AS sources ON (sources.id = deps.object_id OR sources.id = deps.referenced_object_id)\n        WHERE (subsources.type = 'subsource' OR subsources.type = 'progress') AND {}\",\n        itertools::join(query_filter, \" AND \"),\n    );\n    ShowSelect::new(scx, query, filter, None, None)\n}\n\nfn show_views&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,",

        "contents": "    let (key_desc, value_desc) =\n        if matches!(columns, TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_)) || !constraints.is_empty() {\n            let columns = match columns {\n                TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(columns) =&gt; columns,\n                _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n            };\n            let desc = plan_source_export_desc(scx, name, columns, constraints)?;\n            (None, desc)\n        } else {\n            let key_desc = source_connection.default_key_desc();\n            let value_desc = source_connection.default_value_desc();\n            (Some(key_desc), value_desc)\n        };\n\n    let metadata_columns_desc = match &amp;details {\n        SourceExportDetails::Kafka(KafkaSourceExportDetails {\n            metadata_columns, ..\n        }) =&gt; kafka_metadata_columns_desc(metadata_columns),\n        _ =&gt; vec![],\n    };\n\n    let (mut desc, envelope, encoding) = apply_source_envelope_encoding(\n        scx,\n        &amp;envelope,\n        format,\n        key_desc,\n        value_desc,\n        include_metadata,\n        metadata_columns_desc,\n        source_connection,\n    )?;\n    if let TableFromSourceColumns::Named(col_names) = columns {\n        plan_utils::maybe_rename_columns(format!(\"source table {}\", name), &amp;mut desc, col_names)?;\n    }\n\n    let names: Vec&lt;_&gt; = desc.iter_names().cloned().collect();\n    if let Some(dup) = names.iter().duplicates().next() {\n        sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n    }\n\n    let name = scx.allocate_qualified_name(normalize::unresolved_item_name(name.clone())?)?;\n\n    // Allow users to specify a timeline. If they do not, determine a default\n    // timeline for the source.\n    let timeline = match envelope {\n        SourceEnvelope::CdcV2 =&gt; {\n            Timeline::External(scx.catalog.resolve_full_name(&amp;name).to_string())\n        }\n        _ =&gt; Timeline::EpochMilliseconds,\n    };\n\n    if let Some(partition_by) = partition_by",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/query.rs",
        "range": {

            "line": 347,

          }

        "contents": " Vec::with_capacity(columns.len());\n\n    if columns.is_empty() {\n        // Columns in source query must be in order. Let's guess the full shape and truncate to the\n        // right size later after planning the source query\n        source_types.extend(desc.iter_types().map(|x| &amp;x.scalar_type));\n        ordering.extend(0..desc.arity());\n    } else {\n        let column_by_name: BTreeMap&lt;&amp;ColumnName, (usize, &amp;SqlColumnType)&gt; = desc\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(idx, (name, typ))| (name, (idx, typ)))\n            .collect();\n\n        for c in &amp;columns {\n            if let Some((idx, typ)) = column_by_name.get(c) {\n                ordering.push(*idx);\n                source_types.push(&amp;typ.scalar_type);\n            } else {\n                sql_bail!(\n                    \"column {} of relation {} does not exist\",\n                    c.quoted(),\n                    table_name.full_name_str().quoted()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(dup) = columns.iter().duplicates().next() {\n            sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Plan the source.\n    let expr = match source {\n        InsertSource::Query(mut query) =&gt;",

            "line": 613,
            "column": 32
          }
        },
        "contents": "    let source_desc = if columns.is_empty() {\n        let indexes = (0..table_desc.arity()).map(ColumnIndex::from_raw);\n        ordering.extend(indexes);\n\n        // The source data should be in the same order as the table.\n        table_desc\n    } else {\n        let columns: Vec&lt;_&gt; = columns.into_iter().map(normalize::column_name).collect();\n        let column_by_name: BTreeMap&lt;&amp;ColumnName, (ColumnIndex, &amp;SqlColumnType)&gt; = table_desc\n            .iter_all()\n            .map(|(idx, name, typ)| (name, (*idx, typ)))\n            .collect();\n\n        let mut names = Vec::with_capacity(columns.len());\n        let mut source_types = Vec::with_capacity(columns.len());\n\n        for c in &amp;columns {\n            if let Some((idx, typ)) = column_by_name.get(c) {\n                ordering.push(*idx);\n                source_types.push((*typ).clone());\n                names.push(c.clone());\n            } else {\n                sql_bail!(\n                    \"column {} of relation {} does not exist\",\n                    c.quoted(),\n                    item_name.full_name_str().quoted()\n                );\n            }\n        }\n        if let Some(dup) = columns.iter().duplicates().next() {\n            sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n        }\n\n        // The source data is a different shape than the destination table.\n        RelationDesc::new(SqlRelationType::new(source_types), names)\n    };\n\n    Ok((item.id(), source_desc, ordering, mfp))\n}\n\n/// See the doc comment on [&#96;plan_copy_item&#96;] for the details of what this function returns.\n///\n/// TODO(cf3): Merge this method with [&#96;plan_copy_item&#96;].\npub fn plan_copy_from(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    table_name: ResolvedItemName",

        "contents": "pub struct CreateSourceStatement&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; {\n    pub name: UnresolvedItemName,\n    pub in_cluster: Option&lt;T::ClusterName&gt;,\n    pub col_names: Vec&lt;Ident&gt;,\n    pub connection: CreateSourceConnection&lt;T&gt;,\n    pub include_metadata: Vec&lt;SourceIncludeMetadata&gt;,\n    pub format: Option&lt;FormatSpecifier&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n    pub envelope: Option&lt;SourceEnvelope&gt;,\n    pub if_not_exists: bool,\n    pub key_constraint: Option&lt;KeyConstraint&gt;,\n    pub with_options: Vec&lt;CreateSourceOption&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n    pub external_references: Option&lt;ExternalReferences&gt;,\n    pub progress_subsource: Option&lt;DeferredItemName&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; AstDisplay for CreateSourceStatement&lt;T&gt; {\n    fn fmt&lt;W: fmt::Write&gt;(&amp;self, f: &amp;mut AstFormatter&lt;W&gt;) {\n        f.write_str(\"CREATE SOURCE \");\n        if self.if_not_exists {\n            f.write_str(\"IF NOT EXISTS \");\n        }\n        f.write_node(&amp;self.name);\n        if !self.col_names.is_empty() {\n            f.write_str(\" (\");\n            f.write_node(&amp;display::comma_separated(&amp;self.col_names));\n            if self.key_constraint.is_some() {\n                f.write_str(\", \");\n                f.write_node(self.key_constraint.as_ref().unwrap());\n            }\n            f.write_str(\")\");\n        } else if self.key_constraint.is_some() {\n            f.write_str(\" (\");\n            f.write_node(self.key_constraint.as_ref().unwrap());\n            f.write_str(\")\")\n        }\n        if let Some(cluster) = &amp;self.in_cluster {\n            f.write_str(\" IN CLUSTER \");\n            f.write_node(cluster);\n        }\n        f.write_str(\" FROM \");\n        f.write_node(&amp;self.connection);\n        if let Some(format) = &amp;self.format {\n            f.write_str(\" \");\n            f.write_node(format);\n        }\n        if !self.include_metadata.is_empty() {\n            f.write_str(\" INCLUDE \");\n            f.write_node(&amp;display::comma_separated(&amp;self.include_metadata));\n        }\n\n        if let Some(envelope) = &amp;self.envelope",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement.rs",

          }

        "contents": "    pub fn get_schema(\n        &amp;self,\n        database_spec: &amp;ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier,\n        schema_spec: &amp;SchemaSpecifier,\n    ) -&gt; &amp;dyn CatalogSchema {\n        self.catalog.get_schema(database_spec, schema_spec)\n    }\n\n    pub fn resolve_item(&amp;self, name: RawItemName) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;dyn CatalogItem, PlanError&gt; {\n        match name {\n            RawItemName::Name(name) =&gt; {\n                let name = normalize::unresolved_item_name(name)?;\n                Ok(self.catalog.resolve_item(&amp;name)?)\n            }\n            RawItemName::Id(id, _, _) =&gt; {\n                let gid = id.parse()?;\n                Ok(self.catalog.get_item(&amp;gid))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_item(&amp;self, id: &amp;CatalogItemId) -&gt; &amp;dyn CatalogItem {\n        self.catalog.get_item(id)\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_item_by_resolved_name(\n        &amp;self,\n        name: &amp;ResolvedItemName,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;Box&lt;dyn CatalogCollectionItem + '_&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n        match name {\n            ResolvedItemName::Item { id, version, .. } =&gt; {\n                Ok(self.get_item(id).at_version(*version))\n            }\n            ResolvedItemName::Cte { .. } =&gt; sql_bail!(\"non-user item\"),\n            ResolvedItemName::ContinualTask { .. } =&gt; sql_bail!(\"non-user item\"),\n            ResolvedItemName::Error =&gt; unreachable!(\"should have been caught in name resolution\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn get_column_by_resolved_name(\n        &amp;self,\n        name: &amp;ColumnName&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(Box&lt;dyn CatalogCollectionItem + '_&gt;, usize), PlanError&gt; {\n        match (&amp;name.relation, &amp;name.column) {\n            (\n                ResolvedItemName::Item { id, version, .. },\n                ResolvedColumnReference::Column { index, .. },\n            ) =&gt; {\n                let item = self.get_item(id).at_version(*version);\n                Ok((item, *index))\n            }\n            _ =&gt; unreachable!(\n                \"get_column_by_resolved_name errors should have been caught in name resolution\"\n            ),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn resolve_function",

        "contents": "    // (But skip window aggregates.)\n    let (aggregates, table_funcs) = {\n        let mut visitor = AggregateTableFuncVisitor::new(qcx.scx);\n        visitor.visit_select_mut(&amp;mut s);\n        for o in order_by_exprs.iter_mut() {\n            visitor.visit_order_by_expr_mut(o);\n        }\n        visitor.into_result()?\n    };\n    let mut table_func_names: BTreeMap&lt;String, Ident&gt; = BTreeMap::new();\n    if !table_funcs.is_empty() {\n        let (expr, scope) = plan_scalar_table_funcs(\n            qcx,\n            table_funcs,\n            &amp;mut table_func_names,\n            &amp;relation_expr,\n            &amp;from_scope,\n        )?;\n        relation_expr = relation_expr.join(expr, HirScalarExpr::literal_true(), JoinKind::Inner);\n        from_scope = from_scope.product(scope)?;\n    }\n\n    // Step 4. Expand SELECT clause.\n    let projection = {\n        let ecx = &amp;ExprContext {\n            qcx,\n            name: \"SELECT clause\",\n            scope: &amp;from_scope,\n            relation_type: &amp;qcx.relation_type(&amp;relation_expr),\n            allow_aggregates: true,\n            allow_subqueries: true,\n            allow_parameters: true,\n            allow_windows: true,\n        };\n        let mut out = vec![];\n        for si in &amp;s.projection {\n            if *si == SelectItem::Wildcard &amp;&amp; s.from.is_empty() {\n                sql_bail!(\"SELECT * with no tables specified is not valid\");\n            }\n            out.extend(expand_select_item(ecx, si, &amp;table_func_names)?);\n        }\n        out\n    };\n\n    // Step 5. Handle GROUP BY clause.\n    // This will also plan the aggregates gathered in Step 3.\n    // See an overview of how aggregates are planned in the doc comment at the top of the file.\n    let (mut group_scope, select_all_mapping) = {\n        // Compute GROUP BY expressions.",

        "contents": "        let propagated_with_options: Vec&lt;_&gt; = source_stmt\n            .with_options\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|opt| match opt.name {\n                CreateSourceOptionName::TimestampInterval =&gt; None,\n                CreateSourceOptionName::RetainHistory =&gt; Some(CreateSubsourceOption {\n                    name: CreateSubsourceOptionName::RetainHistory,\n                    value: opt.value.clone(),\n                }),\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        // 2. Then plan the main source.\n        let source_plan = match self.plan_statement(\n            ctx.session(),\n            Statement::CreateSource(source_stmt),\n            &amp;params,\n            &amp;resolved_ids,\n        )? {\n            Plan::CreateSource(plan) =&gt; plan,\n            p =&gt; unreachable!(\"s must be CreateSourcePlan but got {:?}\", p),\n        };\n\n        let id_ts = self.get_catalog_write_ts().await;\n        let (item_id, global_id) = self.catalog().allocate_user_id(id_ts).await?;\n\n        let source_full_name = self.catalog().resolve_full_name(&amp;source_plan.name, None);\n        let of_source = ResolvedItemName::Item {\n            id: item_id,\n            qualifiers: source_plan.name.qualifiers.clone(),\n            full_name: source_full_name,\n            print_id: true,\n            version: RelationVersionSelector::Latest,\n        };\n\n        // Generate subsource statements\n        let conn_catalog = self.catalog().for_system_session();\n        let pcx = plan::PlanContext::zero();\n        let scx = StatementContext::new(Some(&amp;pcx), &amp;conn_catalog);\n\n        let mut subsource_stmts = generate_subsource_statements(&amp;scx, of_source, subsources)?;\n\n        for subsource_stmt in subsource_stmts.iter_mut() {\n            subsource_stmt\n                .with_options\n                .extend(propagated_with_options.iter().cloned())\n        }\n\n        create_source_plans.push(CreateSourcePlanBundle",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1208,
            "column": 1
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1260,
            "column": 23
          }
        },
        "contents": "impl&lt;'a&gt; SourceReferenceResolver {\n    /// Constructs a new &#96;SourceReferenceResolver&#96; from a slice of &#96;T:\n    /// SubsourceCatalogReference&#96;.\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    /// - If any &#96;&amp;str&#96; provided cannot be taken to an [&#96;Ident&#96;].\n    pub fn new&lt;T: ExternalCatalogReference&gt;(\n        database: &amp;str,\n        referenceable_items: &amp;'a [T],\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;SourceReferenceResolver, ExternalReferenceResolutionError&gt; {\n        // An index from table name -&gt; schema name -&gt; database name -&gt; index in\n        // &#96;referenceable_items&#96;.\n        let mut inner = BTreeMap::new();\n\n        let database = Ident::new(database)?;\n\n        for (reference_idx, item) in referenceable_items.iter().enumerate() {\n            let item_name = Ident::new(item.item_name())?;\n            let schema_name = Ident::new(item.schema_name())?;\n\n            inner\n                .entry(item_name)\n                .or_insert_with(BTreeMap::new)\n                .entry(schema_name)\n                .or_insert_with(BTreeMap::new)\n                .entry(database.clone())\n                .or_insert(reference_idx);\n        }\n\n        Ok(SourceReferenceResolver { inner })\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the canonical reference and index from which it originated in\n    /// the &#96;referenceable_items&#96; provided to [&#96;Self::new&#96;].\n    ///\n    /// # Args\n    /// - &#96;name&#96; is &#96;&amp;[Ident]&#96; to let users provide the inner element of\n    ///   [&#96;UnresolvedItemName&#96;].\n    /// - &#96;canonicalize_to_width&#96; limits the number of elements in the returned\n    ///   [&#96;UnresolvedItemName&#96;];this is useful if the source type requires\n    ///   contriving database and schema names that a subsource should not\n    ///   persist as its reference.\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    /// - If &#96;name&#96; does not resolve to an item in &#96;self.inner&#96;.\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    /// - If &#96;canonicalize_to_width&#96;&#96; is not in &#96;1..=3&#96;.\n    pub fn resolve(\n        &amp;self,\n        name: &amp;[Ident],",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/catalog/state.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1915,
            "column": 64
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1975,
            "column": 5
          }
        },
        "contents": ",\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn config(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;mz_sql::catalog::CatalogConfig {\n        &amp;self.config\n    }\n\n    pub fn resolve_database(&amp;self, database_name: &amp;str) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;Database, SqlCatalogError&gt; {\n        match self.database_by_name.get(database_name) {\n            Some(id) =&gt; Ok(&amp;self.database_by_id[id]),\n            None =&gt; Err(SqlCatalogError::UnknownDatabase(database_name.into())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn resolve_schema(\n        &amp;self,\n        current_database: Option&lt;&amp;DatabaseId&gt;,\n        database_name: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;,\n        schema_name: &amp;str,\n        conn_id: &amp;ConnectionId,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;Schema, SqlCatalogError&gt; {\n        let database_spec = match database_name {\n            // If a database is explicitly specified, validate it. Note that we\n            // intentionally do not validate &#96;current_database&#96; to permit\n            // querying &#96;mz_catalog&#96; with an invalid session database, e.g., so\n            // that you can run &#96;SHOW DATABASES&#96; to *find* a valid database.\n            Some(database) =&gt; Some(ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier::Id(\n                self.resolve_database(database)?.id().clone(),\n            )),\n            None =&gt; current_database.map(|id| ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier::Id(id.clone())),\n        };\n\n        // First try to find the schema in the named database.\n        if let Some(database_spec) = database_spec {\n            if let Ok(schema) =\n                self.resolve_schema_in_database(&amp;database_spec, schema_name, conn_id)\n            {\n                return Ok(schema);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Then fall back to the ambient database.\n        if let Ok(schema) = self.resolve_schema_in_database(\n            &amp;ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier::Ambient,\n            schema_name,\n            conn_id,\n        ) {\n            return Ok(schema);\n        }\n\n        Err(SqlCatalogError::UnknownSchema(schema_name.into()))\n    }\n\n    /// Optimized lookup for a system schema.\n    ///\n    /// Panics if the system schema doesn't exist in the catalog.\n    pub fn resolve_system_schema(&amp;self, name: &amp;'static str) -&gt; SchemaId {\n        self.ambient_schemas_by_name[name]\n    }",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": ",",
            "lineNumber": 1916
          },
          {
            "text": "            },",

          },
          {
            "text": "        }",

          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 1919
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1920
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub fn config(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;mz_sql::catalog::CatalogConfig {",
            "lineNumber": 1921
          },
          {
            "text": "        &amp;self.config",
            "lineNumber": 1922
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 1923
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1924
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub fn resolve_database(&amp;self, database_name: &amp;str) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;Database, SqlCatalogError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 1925
          },
          {
            "text": "        match self.database_by_name.get(database_name) {",
            "lineNumber": 1926
          },
          {
            "text": "            Some(id) =&gt; Ok(&amp;self.database_by_id[id]),",
            "lineNumber": 1927
          },
          {
            "text": "            None =&gt; Err(SqlCatalogError::UnknownDatabase(database_name.into())),",
            "lineNumber": 1928
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 1929
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 1930
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1931
          },
          {
            "text": "    pub fn resolve_schema(",
            "lineNumber": 1932
          },
          {
            "text": "        &amp;self,",

          },
          {
            "text": "        current_database: Option&lt;&amp;DatabaseId&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 1934
          },
          {
            "text": "        database_name: Option&lt;&amp;str&gt;,",
            "lineNumber": 1935
          },
          {
            "text": "        schema_name: &amp;str,",
            "lineNumber": 1936
          },
          {
            "text": "        conn_id: &amp;ConnectionId,",
            "lineNumber": 1937
          },
          {
            "text": "    ) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;Schema, SqlCatalogError&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 1938
          },
          {
            "text": "        let database_spec = match database_name {",
            "lineNumber": 1939
          },
          {
            "text": "            // If a database is explicitly specified, validate it. Note that we",
            "lineNumber": 1940
          },
          {
            "text": "            // intentionally do not validate &#96;current_database&#96; to permit",
            "lineNumber": 1941
          },
          {
            "text": "            // querying &#96;mz_catalog&#96; with an invalid session database, e.g., so",
            "lineNumber": 1942
          },
          {
            "text": "            // that you can run &#96;SHOW DATABASES&#96; to *find* a valid database.",
            "lineNumber": 1943
          },
          {
            "text": "            Some(database) =&gt; Some(ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier::Id(",
            "lineNumber": 1944
          },
          {
            "text": "                self.resolve_database(database)?.id().clone(),",
            "lineNumber": 1945
          },
          {
            "text": "            )),",
            "lineNumber": 1946
          },
          {
            "text": "            None =&gt; current_database.map(|id| ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier::Id(id.clone())),",
            "lineNumber": 1947
          },
          {
            "text": "        };",
            "lineNumber": 1948
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1949
          },
          {
            "text": "        // First try to find the schema in the named database.",
            "lineNumber": 1950
          },
          {
            "text": "        if let Some(database_spec) = database_spec {",
            "lineNumber": 1951
          },
          {
            "text": "            if let Ok(schema) =",
            "lineNumber": 1952
          },
          {
            "text": "                self.resolve_schema_in_database(&amp;database_spec, schema_name, conn_id)",
            "lineNumber": 1953
          },
          {
            "text": "            {",
            "lineNumber": 1954
          },
          {
            "text": "                return Ok(schema);",
            "lineNumber": 1955
          },
          {
            "text": "            }",
            "lineNumber": 1956
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 1957
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1958
          },
          {
            "text": "        // Then fall back to the ambient database.",
            "lineNumber": 1959
          },
          {
            "text": "        if let Ok(schema) = self.resolve_schema_in_database(",
            "lineNumber": 1960
          },

            "lineNumber": 1961

          },

            "lineNumber": 1964
          },

          },
          {
            "text": "        }",
            "lineNumber": 1966
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1967
          },
          {
            "text": "        Err(SqlCatalogError::UnknownSchema(schema_name.into()))",
            "lineNumber": 1968
          },
          {
            "text": "    }",
            "lineNumber": 1969
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1970
          },
          {
            "text": "    /// Optimized lookup for a system schema.",
            "lineNumber": 1971

            "lineNumber": 1974

          {

        "contents": " {\n        let raw_name = match normalize::unresolved_item_name(raw_name) {\n            Ok(raw_name) =&gt; raw_name,\n            Err(e) =&gt; {\n                if self.status.is_ok() {\n                    self.status = Err(e);\n                }\n                return ResolvedItemName::Error;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let mut r: Result&lt;&amp;dyn CatalogItem, CatalogError&gt; =\n            Err(CatalogError::UnknownItem(raw_name.to_string()));\n\n        if r.is_err() &amp;&amp; config.types {\n            r = self.catalog.resolve_type(&amp;raw_name);\n        }\n\n        if r.is_err() &amp;&amp; config.functions {\n            r = self.catalog.resolve_function(&amp;raw_name);\n        }\n\n        if r.is_err() &amp;&amp; config.relations {\n            // Check if unqualified name refers to a CTE.\n            //\n            // Note that this is done in non-function contexts as CTEs\n            // are treated as relations.\n            if raw_name.database.is_none() &amp;&amp; raw_name.schema.is_none() {\n                let norm_name = normalize::ident(Ident::new_unchecked(&amp;raw_name.item));\n                if let Some(id) = self.ctes.get(&amp;norm_name) {\n                    return ResolvedItemName::Cte {\n                        id: *id,\n                        name: norm_name,\n                    };\n                }\n            }\n            if let Some((ct_name, ct_id)) = self.continual_task.as_ref() {\n                if *ct_name == raw_name {\n                    return ResolvedItemName::ContinualTask {\n                        id: *ct_id,\n                        name: raw_name,\n                    };\n                }\n            }\n            r = self.catalog.resolve_item(&amp;raw_name);\n        };\n\n        match r {\n            Ok(item) =&gt;",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources.rs",

          "startPosition": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1309,
            "column": 28
          }
        },
        "contents": "    pub fn resolve(\n        &amp;self,\n        name: &amp;[Ident],\n        canonicalize_to_width: usize,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(UnresolvedItemName, usize), ExternalReferenceResolutionError&gt; {\n        let (db, schema, idx) = self.resolve_inner(name)?;\n\n        let item = name.last().expect(\"must have provided at least 1 element\");\n\n        let canonical_name = match canonicalize_to_width {\n            1 =&gt; vec![item.clone()],\n            2 =&gt; vec![schema.clone(), item.clone()],\n            3 =&gt; vec![db.clone(), schema.clone(), item.clone()],\n            o =&gt; panic!(\"canonicalize_to_width values must be 1..=3, but got {}\", o),\n        };\n\n        Ok((UnresolvedItemName(canonical_name), idx))\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the index from which it originated in the &#96;referenceable_items&#96;\n    /// provided to [&#96;Self::new&#96;].\n    ///\n    /// # Args\n    /// &#96;name&#96; is &#96;&amp;[Ident]&#96; to let users provide the inner element of\n    /// [&#96;UnresolvedItemName&#96;].\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    /// - If &#96;name&#96; does not resolve to an item in &#96;self.inner&#96;.\n    pub fn resolve_idx(&amp;self, name: &amp;[Ident]) -&gt; Result&lt;usize, ExternalReferenceResolutionError&gt; {\n        let (_db, _schema, idx) = self.resolve_inner(name)?;\n        Ok(idx)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns the index from which it originated in the &#96;referenceable_items&#96;\n    /// provided to [&#96;Self::new&#96;].\n    ///\n    /// # Args\n    /// &#96;name&#96; is &#96;&amp;[Ident]&#96; to let users provide the inner element of\n    /// [&#96;UnresolvedItemName&#96;].\n    ///\n    /// # Return\n    /// Returns a tuple whose elements are:\n    /// 1. The \"database\"- or top-level namespace of the reference.\n    /// 2. The \"schema\"- or second-level namespace of the reference.\n    /// 3. The index to find the item in &#96;referenceable_items&#96; argument provided\n    ///    to &#96;SourceReferenceResolver::new&#96;.\n    ///\n    /// # Errors\n    /// - If &#96;name&#96; does not resolve to an item in &#96;self.inner&#96;.\n    fn resolve_inner&lt;'name: 'a&gt;(\n        &amp;'a self,\n        name: &amp;'name [Ident]",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    pub fn resolve(",
            "lineNumber": 1259

          },

            "text": "            2 =&gt; vec![schema.clone(), item.clone()],",

            "lineNumber": 1277

          {
            "text": "    /// Returns the index from which it originated in the &#96;referenceable_items&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 1278

            "lineNumber": 1279

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/names.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1540,
            "column": 93
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1593,
            "column": 44
          }
        },
        "contents": "        let version = match version {\n            // If there isn't a version specified, and this item supports versioning, track the\n            // latest.\n            None =&gt; match item.latest_version() {\n                // Only track the version of the referenced object, if the feature is enabled.\n                Some(v) if alter_table_enabled =&gt; RelationVersionSelector::Specific(v),\n                _ =&gt; RelationVersionSelector::Latest,\n            },\n            // Note: Return the specific version if one is specified, even if the feature is off.\n            Some(v) =&gt; {\n                let specified_version = RelationVersion::from(v);\n                match item.latest_version() {\n                    Some(latest) if latest &gt;= specified_version =&gt; {\n                        RelationVersionSelector::Specific(specified_version)\n                    }\n                    _ =&gt; {\n                        if self.status.is_ok() {\n                            self.status = Err(PlanError::InvalidVersion {\n                                name: item.name().item.clone(),\n                                version: v.to_string(),\n                            })\n                        }\n                        return ResolvedItemName::Error;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        };\n        let item = item.at_version(version);\n        self.ids\n            .entry(item.id())\n            .or_default()\n            .insert(item.global_id());\n\n        let full_name = match normalize::full_name(raw_name) {\n            Ok(full_name) =&gt; full_name,\n            Err(e) =&gt; {\n                if self.status.is_ok() {\n                    self.status = Err(e);\n                }\n                return ResolvedItemName::Error;\n            }\n        };\n        ResolvedItemName::Item {\n            id,\n            qualifiers: item.name().qualifiers.clone(),\n            full_name,\n            print_id: true,\n            version,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;'a&gt; Fold&lt;Raw, Aug&gt; for NameResolver&lt;'a&gt;",
        "signatures": {},

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So when you reference an item in a query, it goes through relation_desc() - and for a Source, the catalog must return a relation description. The issue might be that when you reference a *source* (not a table-from-source), the name resolution might resolve to something different. I will check - for a load generator COUNTER source, when you do SELECT * FROM emp_src, what does the catalog return? Maybe sources don't have relation_desc() and that's why we get weird behavior, or maybe they do but the columns come from the "default" export.

Actually re-reading the user's error: "column counter does not exist" - so the query is parsing and planning, but when resolving "counter" it's not found. So the relation from emp_src might have different column names. I will check how load generator sources expose their relation when the source is referenced in a query - maybe the columns are qualified with the schema name, e.g. "counter" (schema).something?

Looking at the load generator: for Counter, views() returns vec![], so there are no named subsources. So when you reference the source in a query, how does it work? Maybe you have to use REFERENCE? I will search for how to reference a load generator source's output in a query.
</details></think>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I see - in source-tables.td they do CREATE TABLE counter_1 FROM SOURCE counter (REFERENCE "counter"). So for a COUNTER source, the REFERENCE is "counter" - that's the name of the "table" or output within the load generator. So when you want to query the source directly, maybe you need to use the qualified form: emp_src with REFERENCE, or something like mz_load_generators.counter? I will check - can you SELECT from a source using a REFERENCE or subsource name?

Actually re-reading the test - when they CREATE TABLE counter_1 FROM SOURCE counter (REFERENCE "counter"), they're creating a *table* from the source with that reference. So the table gets its schema from that reference. To SELECT from the source *directly* (without going through a table), we need to know how Materialize exposes that. Maybe you do: SELECT * FROM emp_src (REFERENCE "counter") or SELECT * FROM mz_load_generators.counter? I will search for selecting from a source with reference or from mz_load_generators.

        "contents": "(\n        &amp;self,\n        object: ResolvedItemName,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;(HirRelationExpr, Scope), PlanError&gt; {\n        match object {\n            ResolvedItemName::Item {\n                id,\n                full_name,\n                version,\n                ..\n            } =&gt; {\n                let item = self.scx.get_item(&amp;id).at_version(version);\n                let desc = match item.relation_desc() {\n                    Some(desc) =&gt; desc.clone(),\n                    None =&gt; {\n                        return Err(PlanError::InvalidDependency {\n                            name: full_name.to_string(),\n                            item_type: item.item_type().to_string(),\n                        });\n                    }\n                };\n                let expr = HirRelationExpr::Get {\n                    id: Id::Global(item.global_id()),\n                    typ: desc.typ().clone(),\n                };\n\n                let name = full_name.into();\n                let scope = Scope::from_source(Some(name), desc.iter_names().cloned());\n\n                Ok((expr, scope))\n            }\n            ResolvedItemName::Cte { id, name } =&gt; {\n                let name = name.into();\n                let cte = self.ctes.get(&amp;id).unwrap();\n                let expr = HirRelationExpr::Get {\n                    id: Id::Local(id),\n                    typ: cte.desc.typ().clone(),\n                };\n\n                let scope = Scope::from_source(Some(name), cte.desc.iter_names());\n\n                Ok((expr, scope))\n            }\n            ResolvedItemName::ContinualTask { id, name } =&gt; {\n                let cte = self.ctes.get(&amp;id).unwrap();\n                let expr = HirRelationExpr::Get {\n                    id: Id::Local(id),\n                    typ: cte.desc.typ().clone(),\n                };\n\n                let scope = Scope::from_source(Some(name), cte.desc.iter_names());\n\n                Ok((expr, scope))\n            }\n            ResolvedItemName::Error =&gt; unreachable!(\"should have been caught in name resolution\"),\n        }\n    }",

        "contents": " Result&lt;PurifiedStatement, PlanError&gt; {\n    let scx = StatementContext::new(None, &amp;catalog);\n    let CreateTableFromSourceStatement {\n        name: _,\n        columns,\n        constraints,\n        source: source_name,\n        if_not_exists: _,\n        external_reference,\n        format,\n        envelope,\n        include_metadata: _,\n        with_options,\n    } = &amp;mut stmt;\n\n    // Columns and constraints cannot be specified by the user but will be populated below.\n    if matches!(columns, TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_)) {\n        sql_bail!(\"CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE column definitions cannot be specified directly\");\n    }\n    if !constraints.is_empty() {\n        sql_bail!(\n            \"CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE constraint definitions cannot be specified directly\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Get the source item\n    let item = match scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(source_name) {\n        Ok(item) =&gt; item,\n        Err(e) =&gt; return Err(e),\n    };\n\n    // Ensure it's an ingestion-based and alterable source.\n    let desc = match item.source_desc()? {\n        Some(desc) =&gt; desc.clone().into_inline_connection(scx.catalog),\n        None =&gt; {\n            sql_bail!(\"cannot ALTER this type of source\")\n        }\n    };\n    let unresolved_source_name: UnresolvedItemName = source_name.full_item_name().clone().into();\n\n    let crate::plan::statement::ddl::TableFromSourceOptionExtracted {\n        text_columns,\n        exclude_columns,\n        retain_history: _,\n        details,\n        partition_by: _,\n        seen: _,\n    } = with_options.clone().try_into()?;\n    assert_none!(details, \"details cannot be explicitly set\");\n\n    // Our text column values are unqualified (just column names), but the purification methods below\n    // expect to match the fully-qualified names against the full set of tables in upstream, so we\n    // need to qualify them using the external reference first.\n    let qualified_text_columns = text_columns\n        .iter()\n        .map",

        "contents": "    let (key_desc, value_desc) =\n        if matches!(columns, TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(_)) || !constraints.is_empty() {\n            let columns = match columns {\n                TableFromSourceColumns::Defined(columns) =&gt; columns,\n                _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n            };\n            let desc = plan_source_export_desc(scx, name, columns, constraints)?;\n            (None, desc)\n        } else {\n            let key_desc = source_connection.default_key_desc();\n            let value_desc = source_connection.default_value_desc();\n            (Some(key_desc), value_desc)\n        };\n\n    let metadata_columns_desc = match &amp;details {\n        SourceExportDetails::Kafka(KafkaSourceExportDetails {\n            metadata_columns, ..\n        }) =&gt; kafka_metadata_columns_desc(metadata_columns),\n        _ =&gt; vec![],\n    };\n\n    let (mut desc, envelope, encoding) = apply_source_envelope_encoding(\n        scx,\n        &amp;envelope,\n        format,\n        key_desc,\n        value_desc,\n        include_metadata,\n        metadata_columns_desc,\n        source_connection,\n    )?;\n    if let TableFromSourceColumns::Named(col_names) = columns {\n        plan_utils::maybe_rename_columns(format!(\"source table {}\", name), &amp;mut desc, col_names)?;\n    }\n\n    let names: Vec&lt;_&gt; = desc.iter_names().cloned().collect();\n    if let Some(dup) = names.iter().duplicates().next() {\n        sql_bail!(\"column {} specified more than once\", dup.quoted());\n    }\n\n    let name = scx.allocate_qualified_name(normalize::unresolved_item_name(name.clone())?)?;\n\n    // Allow users to specify a timeline. If they do not, determine a default\n    // timeline for the source.\n    let timeline = match envelope {\n        SourceEnvelope::CdcV2 =&gt; {\n            Timeline::External(scx.catalog.resolve_full_name(&amp;name).to_string())\n        }\n        _ =&gt; Timeline::EpochMilliseconds,\n    };\n\n    if let Some(partition_by) = partition_by",

            "text": "    if let TableFromSourceColumns::Named(col_names) = columns {",

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1944
          },
          {

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 1949
          },
          {

          {
            "lineNumber": 1951
          },

            "text": "    // Allow users to specify a timeline. If they do not, determine a default",

        "contents": "        filtered\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|(reference, alias)| {\n                let name = match alias {\n                    Some(alias_name) =&gt; {\n                        let partial = crate::normalize::unresolved_item_name(alias_name.clone())?;\n                        match partial.schema {\n                            Some(_) =&gt; alias_name.clone(),\n                            // In cases when a prefix is not provided for the aliased name\n                            // fallback to using the schema of the source with the given name\n                            None =&gt; super::source_export_name_gen(source_name, &amp;partial.item)?,\n                        }\n                    }\n                    None =&gt; {\n                        // Just use the item name for this reference and ensure it's created in\n                        // the current schema or the source's schema if provided, not mirroring\n                        // the schema of the reference.\n                        super::source_export_name_gen(source_name, reference.name())?\n                    }\n                };\n\n                Ok(RequestedSourceExport {\n                    external_reference: reference.external_reference()?,\n                    name,\n                    meta: reference,\n                })\n            })\n            .collect::&lt;Result&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;, PlanError&gt;&gt;()\n    }\n\n    pub(super) fn resolve_name(&amp;self, name: &amp;[Ident]) -&gt; Result&lt;&amp;ReferenceMetadata, PlanError&gt; {\n        let idx = self.resolver.resolve_idx(name)?;\n        Ok(&amp;self.references[idx])\n    }\n\n    pub(super) fn all_references(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;Vec&lt;ReferenceMetadata&gt; {\n        &amp;self.references\n    }\n}",

        "contents": "}\n\nfn plan_table_factor(\n    qcx: &amp;QueryContext,\n    table_factor: &amp;TableFactor&lt;Aug&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(HirRelationExpr, Scope), PlanError&gt; {\n    match table_factor {\n        TableFactor::Table { name, alias } =&gt; {\n            let (expr, scope) = qcx.resolve_table_name(name.clone())?;\n            let scope = plan_table_alias(scope, alias.as_ref())?;\n            Ok((expr, scope))\n        }\n\n        TableFactor::Function {\n            function,\n            alias,\n            with_ordinality,\n        } =&gt; plan_solitary_table_function(qcx, function, alias.as_ref(), *with_ordinality),\n\n        TableFactor::RowsFrom {\n            functions,\n            alias,\n            with_ordinality,\n        } =&gt; plan_rows_from(qcx, functions, alias.as_ref(), *with_ordinality),\n\n        TableFactor::Derived {\n            lateral,\n            subquery,\n            alias,\n        } =&gt; {\n            let mut qcx = (*qcx).clone();\n            if !lateral {\n                // Since this derived table was not marked as &#96;LATERAL&#96;,\n                // make elements in outer scopes invisible until we reach the\n                // next lateral barrier.\n                for scope in &amp;mut qcx.outer_scopes {\n                    if scope.lateral_barrier {\n                        break;\n                    }\n                    scope.items.clear();\n                }\n            }\n            qcx.outer_scopes[0].lateral_barrier = true;\n            let (expr, scope) = plan_nested_query(&amp;mut qcx, subquery)?;\n            let scope = plan_table_alias(scope, alias.as_ref())?;\n            Ok((expr, scope))\n        }\n\n        TableFactor::NestedJoin { join, alias } =&gt; {\n            let (expr, scope) = plan_table_with_joins(qcx, join)?;\n            let scope = plan_table_alias(scope, alias.as_ref())?;\n            Ok((expr, scope))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Plans a &#96;ROWS FROM&#96; expression.\n///\n/// &#96;ROWS FROM&#96; concatenates table functions into a single table, filling in\n/// &#96;NULL&#96;s in places where one table function has fewer rows than another. We\n/// can achieve this by augmenting each table function with a row number, doing",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/pure/references.rs",

          "startPosition": {
            "line": 142,

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 196
          }
        },
        "contents": "        match self {\n            ReferenceMetadata::Postgres { table, database } =&gt; {\n                Ok(UnresolvedItemName::qualified(&amp;[\n                    Ident::new(database)?,\n                    Ident::new(&amp;table.namespace)?,\n                    Ident::new(&amp;table.name)?,\n                ]))\n            }\n            ReferenceMetadata::MySql(table) =&gt; Ok(UnresolvedItemName::qualified(&amp;[\n                Ident::new(&amp;table.schema_name)?,\n                Ident::new(&amp;table.name)?,\n            ])),\n            ReferenceMetadata::SqlServer {\n                table,\n                database,\n                capture_instance: _,\n            } =&gt; Ok(UnresolvedItemName::qualified(&amp;[\n                Ident::new(database.as_ref())?,\n                Ident::new(table.schema_name.as_ref())?,\n                Ident::new(table.name.as_ref())?,\n            ])),\n            ReferenceMetadata::Kafka(topic) =&gt; {\n                Ok(UnresolvedItemName::qualified(&amp;[Ident::new(topic)?]))\n            }\n            ReferenceMetadata::LoadGenerator {\n                name, namespace, ..\n            } =&gt; {\n                let name = FullItemName {\n                    database: RawDatabaseSpecifier::Name(\n                        mz_storage_types::sources::load_generator::LOAD_GENERATOR_DATABASE_NAME\n                            .to_owned(),\n                    ),\n                    schema: namespace.to_string(),\n                    item: name.to_string(),\n                };\n                Ok(UnresolvedItemName::from(name))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// A set of resolved source references.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub(super) struct RetrievedSourceReferences {\n    updated_at: u64,\n    references: Vec&lt;ReferenceMetadata&gt;,\n    resolver: SourceReferenceResolver,\n}\n\n/// The name of the fake database that we use for non-Postgres sources\n/// to fit the model of a 3-layer catalog used to resolve references\n/// in the &#96;SourceReferenceResolver&#96;. This isn't actually stored in\n/// the catalog since the &#96;ReferenceMetadata::external_reference&#96;",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement/ddl.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1732,
            "column": 2
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1790,
            "column": 36
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub fn plan_create_table_from_source(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,\n    stmt: CreateTableFromSourceStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;Plan, PlanError&gt; {\n    if !scx.catalog.system_vars().enable_create_table_from_source() {\n        sql_bail!(\"CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE is not supported\");\n    }\n\n    let CreateTableFromSourceStatement {\n        name,\n        columns,\n        constraints,\n        if_not_exists,\n        source,\n        external_reference,\n        envelope,\n        format,\n        include_metadata,\n        with_options,\n    } = &amp;stmt;\n\n    let envelope = envelope.clone().unwrap_or(ast::SourceEnvelope::None);\n\n    let TableFromSourceOptionExtracted {\n        text_columns,\n        exclude_columns,\n        retain_history,\n        partition_by,\n        details,\n        seen: _,\n    } = with_options.clone().try_into()?;\n\n    let source_item = scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(source)?;\n    let ingestion_id = source_item.id();\n\n    // Decode the details option stored on the statement, which contains information\n    // created during the purification process.\n    let details = details\n        .as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| sql_err!(\"internal error: source-export missing details\"))?;\n    let details = hex::decode(details).map_err(|e| sql_err!(\"{}\", e))?;\n    let details =\n        ProtoSourceExportStatementDetails::decode(&amp;*details).map_err(|e| sql_err!(\"{}\", e))?;\n    let details =\n        SourceExportStatementDetails::from_proto(details).map_err(|e| sql_err!(\"{}\", e))?;\n\n    if !matches!(details, SourceExportStatementDetails::Kafka { .. })\n        &amp;&amp; include_metadata\n            .iter()\n            .any(|sic| matches!(sic, SourceIncludeMetadata::Headers { .. }))\n    {\n        // TODO(guswynn): should this be &#96;bail_unsupported!&#96;?\n        sql_bail!(\"INCLUDE HEADERS with non-Kafka source table not supported\");\n    }\n    if !matches!(\n        details,\n        SourceExportStatementDetails",

        "contents": "    fn fold_table_factor(\n        &amp;mut self,\n        node: mz_sql_parser::ast::TableFactor&lt;Raw&gt;,\n    ) -&gt; mz_sql_parser::ast::TableFactor&lt;Aug&gt; {\n        use mz_sql_parser::ast::TableFactor::*;\n        match node {\n            Table { name, alias } =&gt; Table {\n                name: self.fold_item_name(name),\n                alias: alias.map(|alias| self.fold_table_alias(alias)),\n            },\n            Function {\n                function,\n                alias,\n                with_ordinality,\n            } =&gt; {\n                match &amp;function.name {\n                    RawItemName::Name(name) =&gt; {\n                        if *name == UnresolvedItemName::unqualified(ident!(\"values\"))\n                            &amp;&amp; self.status.is_ok()\n                        {\n                            self.status = Err(PlanError::FromValueRequiresParen);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    _ =&gt; {}\n                }\n\n                Function {\n                    function: self.fold_function(function),\n                    alias: alias.map(|alias| self.fold_table_alias(alias)),\n                    with_ordinality,\n                }\n            }\n            RowsFrom {\n                functions,\n                alias,\n                with_ordinality,\n            } =&gt; RowsFrom {\n                functions: functions\n                    .into_iter()\n                    .map(|f| self.fold_function(f))\n                    .collect(),\n                alias: alias.map(|alias| self.fold_table_alias(alias)),\n                with_ordinality,\n            },\n            Derived {\n                lateral,\n                subquery,\n                alias,\n            } =&gt; Derived {\n                lateral,\n                subquery: Box::new(self.fold_query(*subquery)),\n                alias: alias.map(|alias| self.fold_table_alias(alias)),\n            },\n            NestedJoin { join, alias } =&gt; NestedJoin {\n                join: Box::new(self.fold_table_with_joins(*join))",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/scope.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 41
          }
        },
        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! Handles SQL's scoping rules.\n//!\n//! A scope spans a single SQL &#96;Query&#96;. Nested subqueries create new scopes.\n//! Names are resolved against the innermost scope first.\n//! * If a match is found, it is returned.\n//! * If no matches are found, the name is resolved against the parent scope.\n//! * If multiple matches are found, the name is ambiguous and we return an\n//!   error to the user.\n//!\n//! Matching rules:\n//! * &#96;bar&#96; will match any column in the scope named &#96;bar&#96;\n//! * &#96;foo.bar&#96; will match any column in the scope named &#96;bar&#96; that originated\n//!    from a table named &#96;foo&#96;.\n//! * Table aliases such as &#96;foo AS quux&#96; replace the old table name.\n//! * Functions create unnamed columns, which can be named with columns aliases\n//!   &#96;(bar + 1) as more_bar&#96;.\n//!\n//! Additionally, most databases fold some form of CSE into name resolution so\n//! that eg &#96;SELECT sum(x) FROM foo GROUP BY sum(x)&#96; would be treated something\n//! like &#96;SELECT \"sum(x)\" FROM foo GROUP BY sum(x) AS \"sum(x)\"&#96; rather than\n//! failing to resolve &#96;x&#96;. We handle this by including the underlying\n//! &#96;sql_parser::ast::Expr&#96; in cases where this is possible.\n//!\n//! Many SQL expressions do strange and arbitrary things to scopes. Rather than\n//! try to capture them all here, we just expose the internals of &#96;Scope&#96; and\n//! handle it in the appropriate place in &#96;super::query&#96;.\n//!\n//! NOTE(benesch): The above approach of exposing scope's internals to the\n//! entire planner has not aged well. SQL scopes are now full of undocumented\n//! assumptions and requirements, since various subcomponents of the planner\n//! shove data into scope items to communicate with subcomponents a mile away.\n//! I've tried to refactor this code several times to no avail. It works better",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.",
            "lineNumber": 1
          },
          {
            "text": "//",

          },
          {
            "text": "// included in the LICENSE file.",
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "//",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "text": "// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with",
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Handles SQL's scoping rules.",
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "text": "//! A scope spans a single SQL &#96;Query&#96;. Nested subqueries create new scopes.",
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Names are resolved against the innermost scope first.",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "//! * If a match is found, it is returned.",
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "text": "//! * If no matches are found, the name is resolved against the parent scope.",
            "lineNumber": 15
          },
          {
            "text": "//! * If multiple matches are found, the name is ambiguous and we return an",
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "//!   error to the user.",
            "lineNumber": 17
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 18
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Matching rules:",
            "lineNumber": 19
          },
          {
            "text": "//! * &#96;bar&#96; will match any column in the scope named &#96;bar&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "text": "//! * &#96;foo.bar&#96; will match any column in the scope named &#96;bar&#96; that originated",
            "lineNumber": 21
          },
          {
            "text": "//!    from a table named &#96;foo&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "//! * Table aliases such as &#96;foo AS quux&#96; replace the old table name.",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "//! * Functions create unnamed columns, which can be named with columns aliases",
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "text": "//!   &#96;(bar + 1) as more_bar&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 26
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Additionally, most databases fold some form of CSE into name resolution so",
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "text": "//! that eg &#96;SELECT sum(x) FROM foo GROUP BY sum(x)&#96; would be treated something",
            "lineNumber": 28
          },
          {
            "text": "//! like &#96;SELECT \"sum(x)\" FROM foo GROUP BY sum(x) AS \"sum(x)\"&#96; rather than",
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "text": "//! failing to resolve &#96;x&#96;. We handle this by including the underlying",
            "lineNumber": 30
          },
          {
            "text": "//! &#96;sql_parser::ast::Expr&#96; in cases where this is possible.",
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "text": "//! Many SQL expressions do strange and arbitrary things to scopes. Rather than",
            "lineNumber": 33

          {
            "text": "//! try to capture them all here, we just expose the internals of &#96;Scope&#96; and",
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "text": "//! handle it in the appropriate place in &#96;super::query&#96;.",
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "text": "//!",

          {
            "text": "//! NOTE(benesch): The above approach of exposing scope's internals to the",
            "lineNumber": 37

            "text": "//! entire planner has not aged well. SQL scopes are now full of undocumented",
            "lineNumber": 38

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/pure.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 751,
            "column": 79
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 800,
            "column": 36
          }
        },
        "contents": "    // auto-generated subsources are created or not created by this source statement.\n    let reference_policy = if scx.catalog.system_vars().enable_create_table_from_source()\n        &amp;&amp; scx.catalog.system_vars().force_source_table_syntax()\n    {\n        SourceReferencePolicy::NotAllowed\n    } else if scx.catalog.system_vars().enable_create_table_from_source() {\n        SourceReferencePolicy::Optional\n    } else {\n        SourceReferencePolicy::Required\n    };\n\n    let mut format_options = SourceFormatOptions::Default;\n\n    let retrieved_source_references: RetrievedSourceReferences;\n\n    match source_connection {\n        CreateSourceConnection::Kafka {\n            connection,\n            options: base_with_options,\n            ..\n        } =&gt; {\n            if let Some(external_references) = external_references {\n                Err(KafkaSourcePurificationError::ReferencedSubsources(\n                    external_references.clone(),\n                ))?;\n            }\n\n            let connection = {\n                let item = scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(connection)?;\n                // Get Kafka connection\n                match item.connection()? {\n                    Connection::Kafka(connection) =&gt; {\n                        connection.clone().into_inline_connection(&amp;catalog)\n                    }\n                    _ =&gt; Err(KafkaSourcePurificationError::NotKafkaConnection(\n                        scx.catalog.resolve_full_name(item.name()),\n                    ))?,\n                }\n            };\n\n            let extracted_options: KafkaSourceConfigOptionExtracted =\n                base_with_options.clone().try_into()?;\n\n            let topic = extracted_options\n                .topic\n                .ok_or(KafkaSourcePurificationError::ConnectionMissingTopic)?;\n\n            let consumer = connection\n                .create_with_context",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    // auto-generated subsources are created or not created by this source statement.",
            "lineNumber": 753
          },
          {
            "text": "    let reference_policy = if scx.catalog.system_vars().enable_create_table_from_source()",
            "lineNumber": 754
          },
          {
            "text": "        &amp;&amp; scx.catalog.system_vars().force_source_table_syntax()",
            "lineNumber": 755
          },
          {
            "text": "    {",
            "lineNumber": 756
          },
          {
            "text": "        SourceReferencePolicy::NotAllowed",
            "lineNumber": 757
          },

        "contents": " {\n        let raw_name = match normalize::unresolved_item_name(raw_name) {\n            Ok(raw_name) =&gt; raw_name,\n            Err(e) =&gt; {\n                if self.status.is_ok() {\n                    self.status = Err(e);\n                }\n                return ResolvedItemName::Error;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let mut r: Result&lt;&amp;dyn CatalogItem, CatalogError&gt; =\n            Err(CatalogError::UnknownItem(raw_name.to_string()));\n\n        if r.is_err() &amp;&amp; config.types {\n            r = self.catalog.resolve_type(&amp;raw_name);\n        }\n\n        if r.is_err() &amp;&amp; config.functions {\n            r = self.catalog.resolve_function(&amp;raw_name);\n        }\n\n        if r.is_err() &amp;&amp; config.relations {\n            // Check if unqualified name refers to a CTE.\n            //\n            // Note that this is done in non-function contexts as CTEs\n            // are treated as relations.\n            if raw_name.database.is_none() &amp;&amp; raw_name.schema.is_none() {\n                let norm_name = normalize::ident(Ident::new_unchecked(&amp;raw_name.item));\n                if let Some(id) = self.ctes.get(&amp;norm_name) {\n                    return ResolvedItemName::Cte {\n                        id: *id,\n                        name: norm_name,\n                    };\n                }\n            }\n            if let Some((ct_name, ct_id)) = self.continual_task.as_ref() {\n                if *ct_name == raw_name {\n                    return ResolvedItemName::ContinualTask {\n                        id: *ct_id,\n                        name: raw_name,\n                    };\n                }\n            }\n            r = self.catalog.resolve_item(&amp;raw_name);\n        };\n\n        match r {\n            Ok(item) =&gt;",

        "contents": "/// in the &#96;SourceReferenceResolver&#96;. This isn't actually stored in\n/// the catalog since the &#96;ReferenceMetadata::external_reference&#96;\n/// method only includes the database name for Postgres sources.\npub(crate) static DATABASE_FAKE_NAME: &amp;str = \"database\";\n\nimpl&lt;'a&gt; SourceReferenceClient&lt;'a&gt; {\n    /// Get all available source references from the upstream system\n    /// and return a &#96;RetrievedSourceReferences&#96; object that can be used\n    /// to resolve user-specified source references and create &#96;SourceReferences&#96;\n    /// for storage in the catalog.\n    pub(super) async fn get_source_references(\n        mut self,\n    ) -&gt; Result&lt;RetrievedSourceReferences, PlanError&gt; {\n        let references = match self {\n            SourceReferenceClient::Postgres {\n                client,\n                publication,\n                database,\n            } =&gt; {\n                let tables = mz_postgres_util::publication_info(client, publication, None).await?;\n\n                if tables.is_empty() {\n                    Err(PgSourcePurificationError::EmptyPublication(\n                        publication.to_string(),\n                    ))?;\n                }\n\n                tables\n                    .into_iter()\n                    .map(|(_oid, desc)| ReferenceMetadata::Postgres {\n                        table: desc,\n                        database: database.to_string(),\n                    })\n                    .collect()\n            }\n            SourceReferenceClient::MySql {\n                ref mut conn,",

          }
        },
        "contents": "pub enum PurifiedStatement {\n    PurifiedCreateSource {\n        // The progress subsource, if we are offloading progress info to a separate relation\n        create_progress_subsource_stmt: Option&lt;CreateSubsourceStatement&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n        create_source_stmt: CreateSourceStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n        // Map of subsource names to external details\n        subsources: BTreeMap&lt;UnresolvedItemName, PurifiedSourceExport&gt;,\n        /// All the available upstream references that can be added as tables\n        /// to this primary source.\n        available_source_references: SourceReferences,\n    },\n    PurifiedAlterSource {\n        alter_source_stmt: AlterSourceStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    },\n    PurifiedAlterSourceAddSubsources {\n        // This just saves us an annoying catalog lookup\n        source_name: ResolvedItemName,\n        /// Options that we will need the values of to update the source's\n        /// definition.\n        options: Vec&lt;AlterSourceAddSubsourceOption&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n        // Map of subsource names to external details\n        subsources: BTreeMap&lt;UnresolvedItemName, PurifiedSourceExport&gt;,\n    },\n    PurifiedAlterSourceRefreshReferences {\n        source_name: ResolvedItemName,\n        /// The updated available upstream references for the primary source.\n        available_source_references: SourceReferences,\n    },\n    PurifiedCreateSink(CreateSinkStatement&lt;Aug&gt;),\n    PurifiedCreateTableFromSource {\n        stmt: CreateTableFromSourceStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    },\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub struct PurifiedSourceExport {\n    pub external_reference: UnresolvedItemName,\n    pub details: PurifiedExportDetails,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum PurifiedExportDetails {\n    MySql {\n        table: MySqlTableDesc,\n        text_columns: Option&lt;Vec&lt;Ident&gt;&gt;",
        "signatures": {},

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/names.rs",

          "startPosition": {

            "line": 1282,
            "column": 46
          }
        },
        "contents": "/// well as the inner kind of object that is represented, e.g. [&#96;CatalogItemId&#96;] is used to\n/// identify both Tables and Views. No other kind of ID encapsulates all of this, hence this new\n/// \"*Id\" type.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Ord, PartialOrd, Hash, Serialize)]\npub enum CommentObjectId {\n    Table(CatalogItemId),\n    View(CatalogItemId),\n    MaterializedView(CatalogItemId),\n    Source(CatalogItemId),\n    Sink(CatalogItemId),\n    Index(CatalogItemId),\n    Func(CatalogItemId),\n    Connection(CatalogItemId),\n    Type(CatalogItemId),\n    Secret(CatalogItemId),\n    ContinualTask(CatalogItemId),\n    Role(RoleId),\n    Database(DatabaseId),\n    Schema((ResolvedDatabaseSpecifier, SchemaSpecifier)),\n    Cluster(ClusterId),\n    ClusterReplica((ClusterId, ReplicaId)),\n    NetworkPolicy(NetworkPolicyId),\n}\n\n/// Whether to resolve an name in the types namespace, the functions namespace,\n/// or the relations namespace. It is possible to resolve name in multiple\n/// namespaces, in which case types are preferred to functions are preferred to\n/// relations.\n// NOTE(benesch,sploiselle): The fact that some names are looked up in multiple\n// namespaces is a bit dubious, and stems from the fact that we don't\n// automatically create types for relations (see database-issues#7142). It's possible that we\n// don't allow names to be looked up in multiple namespaces (i.e., this becomes\n// &#96;enum ItemResolutionNamespace&#96;), but it's also possible that the design of\n// the &#96;DOC ON TYPE&#96; option means we're forever stuck with this complexity.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]\nstruct ItemResolutionConfig {\n    types: bool,\n    functions: bool,\n    relations: bool,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct NameResolver&lt;'a&gt; {\n    catalog: &amp;'a dyn SessionCatalog,\n    ctes: BTreeMap&lt;String, LocalId&gt;,\n    continual_task: Option&lt;(PartialItemName, LocalId)&gt;,\n    status: Result&lt;(), PlanError&gt;,\n    ids: BTreeMap&lt;CatalogItemId, BTreeSet&lt;GlobalId&gt;&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl&lt;'a&gt; NameResolver&lt;'a&gt; {\n    fn new(catalog: &amp;'a dyn SessionCatalog) -&gt;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "/// well as the inner kind of object that is represented, e.g. [&#96;CatalogItemId&#96;] is used to",

        "contents": "    let qualified_text_columns = text_columns\n        .iter()\n        .map(|col| {\n            UnresolvedItemName(\n                external_reference\n                    .as_ref()\n                    .map(|er| er.0.iter().chain_one(col).map(|i| i.clone()).collect())\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|| vec![col.clone()]),\n            )\n        })\n        .collect_vec();\n    let qualified_exclude_columns = exclude_columns\n        .iter()\n        .map(|col| {\n            UnresolvedItemName(\n                external_reference\n                    .as_ref()\n                    .map(|er| er.0.iter().chain_one(col).map(|i| i.clone()).collect())\n                    .unwrap_or_else(|| vec![col.clone()]),\n            )\n        })\n        .collect_vec();\n\n    // Should be overriden below if a source-specific format is required.\n    let mut format_options = SourceFormatOptions::Default;\n\n    let retrieved_source_references: RetrievedSourceReferences;\n\n    let requested_references = external_reference.as_ref().map(|ref_name| {\n        ExternalReferences::SubsetTables(vec![ExternalReferenceExport {\n            reference: ref_name.clone(),\n            alias: None,\n        }])\n    });\n\n    // Run purification work specific to each source type: resolve the external reference to\n    // a fully qualified name and obtain the appropriate details for the source-export statement\n    let purified_export = match desc.connection {\n        GenericSourceConnection::Postgres(pg_source_connection) =&gt;",
