            "line": 235,
            "column": 44
          }
        },
        "contents": "    match create_stmt {\n        Statement::CreateIndex(CreateIndexStatement { on_name, .. }) =&gt; {\n            maybe_update_item_name(on_name.name_mut());\n        }\n        Statement::CreateSink(CreateSinkStatement { from, .. }) =&gt; {\n            maybe_update_item_name(from.name_mut());\n        }\n        Statement::CreateView(CreateViewStatement {\n            definition: ViewDefinition { query, .. },\n            ..\n        }) =&gt; {\n            rewrite_query(from_name, to_item_name, query)?;\n        }\n        Statement::CreateMaterializedView(CreateMaterializedViewStatement {\n            replacement_for,\n            query,\n            ..\n        }) =&gt; {\n            if let Some(target) = replacement_for {\n                maybe_update_item_name(target.name_mut());\n            }\n            rewrite_query(from_name, to_item_name, query)?;\n        }\n        Statement::CreateSource(_)\n        | Statement::CreateSubsource(_)\n        | Statement::CreateTable(_)\n        | Statement::CreateTableFromSource(_)\n        | Statement::CreateSecret(_)\n        | Statement::CreateConnection(_)\n        | Statement::CreateWebhookSource(_) =&gt; {}\n        item =&gt; {\n            unreachable!(\"Internal error: only catalog items need to update item refs {item:?}\")\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Rewrites &#96;query&#96;'s references of &#96;from&#96; to &#96;to&#96; or errors if too ambiguous.\nfn rewrite_query(from: FullItemName, to: String, query: &amp;mut Query&lt;Raw&gt;) -&gt; Result&lt;(), String&gt; {\n    let from_ident = Ident::new_unchecked(from.item.clone());\n    let to_ident = Ident::new_unchecked(to);",

        "contents": "impl RetrievedSourceReferences {\n    /// Convert the resolved source references into a &#96;SourceReferences&#96; object\n    /// for storage in the catalog.\n    pub(super) fn available_source_references(self) -&gt; SourceReferences {\n        SourceReferences {\n            updated_at: self.updated_at,\n            references: self\n                .references\n                .into_iter()\n                .map(|reference| match reference {\n                    ReferenceMetadata::Postgres { table, .. } =&gt; SourceReference {\n                        name: table.name,\n                        namespace: Some(table.namespace),\n                        columns: table.columns.into_iter().map(|c| c.name).collect(),\n                    },\n                    ReferenceMetadata::MySql(table) =&gt; SourceReference {\n                        name: table.name,\n                        namespace: Some(table.schema_name),\n                        columns: table\n                            .columns\n                            .into_iter()\n                            .map(|column| column.name())\n                            .collect(),\n                    },\n                    ReferenceMetadata::SqlServer { table, .. } =&gt; SourceReference {\n                        name: table.name.to_string(),\n                        namespace: Some(table.schema_name.to_string()),\n                        columns: table\n                            .columns\n                            .into_iter()\n                            .map(|c| c.name.to_string())\n                            .collect(),\n                    },\n                    ReferenceMetadata::Kafka(topic) =&gt; SourceReference {\n                        name: topic,\n                        namespace: None,\n                        columns: vec![],\n                    },\n                    ReferenceMetadata::LoadGenerator {\n                        name,\n                        desc,",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "    let tables = requested_exports\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|requested_export| {\n            let table = requested_export.meta.mysql_table().expect(\"is mysql\");\n            let table_ref = table.table_ref();\n            // we are cloning the BTreeSet&lt;&amp;str&gt; so we can avoid a borrow on &#96;table&#96; here\n            let text_cols = text_cols_map.get(&amp;table_ref).map(|s| s.clone());\n            let exclude_columns = exclude_columns_map.get(&amp;table_ref).map(|s| s.clone());\n            let parsed_table = table\n                .clone()\n                .to_desc(text_cols.as_ref(), exclude_columns.as_ref())\n                .map_err(|err| match err {\n                    mz_mysql_util::MySqlError::UnsupportedDataTypes { columns } =&gt; {\n                        PlanError::from(MySqlSourcePurificationError::UnrecognizedTypes {\n                            cols: columns\n                                .into_iter()\n                                .map(|c| (c.qualified_table_name, c.column_name, c.column_type))\n                                .collect(),\n                        })\n                    }\n                    mz_mysql_util::MySqlError::DuplicatedColumnNames {\n                        qualified_table_name,\n                        columns,\n                    } =&gt; PlanError::from(MySqlSourcePurificationError::DuplicatedColumnNames(\n                        qualified_table_name,\n                        columns,\n                    )),\n                    _ =&gt; err.into(),\n                })?;\n            Ok(requested_export.change_meta(parsed_table))\n        })\n        .collect::&lt;Result&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;, PlanError&gt;&gt;()?;\n\n    if tables.is_empty() {\n        Err(MySqlSourcePurificationError::EmptyDatabase)?;\n    }\n\n    validate_requested_references_privileges(tables.iter().map(|t| &amp;t.external_reference), conn)\n        .await?;\n\n    let reference_resolver = SourceReferenceResolver::new(\n        MYSQL_DATABASE_FAKE_NAME,\n        &amp;tables.iter().map(|r| &amp;r.meta).collect::&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;&gt;(),\n    )?;\n\n    // Normalize column options and remove unused column references.",

      "codeBlock": {

            "column": 25
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub enum ResolvedItemName {\n    Item {\n        id: CatalogItemId,\n        qualifiers: ItemQualifiers,\n        full_name: FullItemName,\n        // Whether this object, when printed out, should use [id AS name] syntax. We\n        // want this for things like tables and sources, but not for things like\n        // types.\n        print_id: bool,\n        version: RelationVersionSelector,\n    },\n    Cte {\n        id: LocalId,\n        name: String,\n    },\n    ContinualTask {\n        id: LocalId,\n        name: PartialItemName,\n    },\n    Error,\n}\n\nimpl ResolvedItemName {\n    pub fn full_name_str(&amp;self) -&gt; String {\n        match self {\n            ResolvedItemName::Item { full_name, .. } =&gt; full_name.to_string(),\n            ResolvedItemName::Cte { name, .. } =&gt; name.clone(),\n            ResolvedItemName::ContinualTask { name, .. } =&gt; name.to_string(),\n            ResolvedItemName::Error =&gt; \"error in name resolution\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn full_item_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;FullItemName {\n        match self {\n            ResolvedItemName::Item { full_name, .. } =&gt; full_name,\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"cannot call object_full_name on non-object\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn item_id(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;CatalogItemId {\n        match self {\n            ResolvedItemName::Item { id, .. } =&gt; id,\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"cannot call item_id on non-object\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn version(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;RelationVersionSelector {\n        match self {\n            ResolvedItemName::Item { version, .. } =&gt; version,\n            _ =&gt; panic!(\"cannot call version on non-object\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl AstDisplay for ResolvedItemName {\n    fn fmt&lt;W: fmt::Write&gt;",
        "signatures": {},

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/pure/sql_server.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 564,
            "column": 24
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 600,
            "column": 1
          }
        },
        "contents": "(\n    cols: &amp;[UnresolvedItemName],\n    reference_resolver: &amp;SourceReferenceResolver,\n    tables: &amp;[RequestedSourceExport&lt;SqlServerTableDesc&gt;],\n    option_name: SqlServerConfigOptionName,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;Vec&lt;WithOptionValue&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;, SqlServerSourcePurificationError&gt; {\n    let (seq, unknown): (Vec&lt;_&gt;, Vec&lt;_&gt;) = cols.into_iter().partition(|name| {\n        let (column_name, qual) = name.0.split_last().expect(\"non-empty\");\n        match reference_resolver.resolve_idx(qual) {\n            // TODO(sql_server3): This needs to also introduce the maximum qualification\n            // on  the columns, i.e. ensure they have the schema name.\n            Ok(idx) =&gt; tables[idx]\n                .meta\n                .columns\n                .iter()\n                .any(|n| &amp;*n.name == column_name.as_str()),\n            Err(_) =&gt; false,\n        }\n    });\n\n    if !unknown.is_empty() {\n        return Err(SqlServerSourcePurificationError::DanglingColumns {\n            option_name: option_name.to_string(),\n            items: unknown.into_iter().cloned().collect(),\n        });\n    }\n\n    let mut seq: Vec&lt;_&gt; = seq\n        .into_iter()\n        .cloned()\n        .map(WithOptionValue::UnresolvedItemName)\n        .collect();\n\n    seq.sort();\n    seq.dedup();\n    Ok(seq)\n}",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 581
          },
          {
            "text": "        }",

          },
          {
            "text": "    });",

            "lineNumber": 584

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/plan/statement/show.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 445,
            "column": 45
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 503,
            "column": 34
          }
        },
        "contents": " Some(&amp;[\"name\", \"type\", \"comment\"]))\n}\n\nfn show_tables&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,\n    from: Option&lt;ResolvedSchemaName&gt;,\n    on_source: Option&lt;ResolvedItemName&gt;,\n    filter: Option&lt;ShowStatementFilter&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n    let schema_spec = scx.resolve_optional_schema(&amp;from)?;\n    let mut query = format!(\n        \"SELECT name, comment\n        FROM mz_internal.mz_show_tables tables\n        WHERE tables.schema_id = '{schema_spec}'\",\n    );\n    if let Some(on_source) = &amp;on_source {\n        let on_item = scx.get_item_by_resolved_name(on_source)?;\n        if on_item.item_type() != CatalogItemType::Source {\n            sql_bail!(\n                \"cannot show tables on {} because it is a {}\",\n                on_source.full_name_str(),\n                on_item.item_type(),\n            );\n        }\n        query += &amp;format!(\" AND tables.source_id = '{}'\", on_item.id());\n    }\n    ShowSelect::new(scx, query, filter, None, Some(&amp;[\"name\", \"comment\"]))\n}\n\nfn show_sources&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,\n    from: Option&lt;ResolvedSchemaName&gt;,\n    in_cluster: Option&lt;ResolvedClusterName&gt;,\n    filter: Option&lt;ShowStatementFilter&lt;Aug&gt;&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;ShowSelect&lt;'a&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n    let schema_spec = scx.resolve_optional_schema(&amp;from)?;\n    let mut where_clause = format!(\"schema_id = '{schema_spec}'\");\n\n    if let Some(cluster) = in_cluster {\n        write!(where_clause, \" AND cluster_id = '{}'\", cluster.id)\n            .expect(\"write on string cannot fail\");\n    }\n\n    let query = format!(\n        \"SELECT name, type, cluster, comment\n        FROM mz_internal.mz_show_sources\n        WHERE {where_clause}\"\n    );\n    ShowSelect::new(\n        scx,\n        query,\n        filter,\n        None,\n        Some(&amp;[\"name\", \"type\", \"cluster\", \"comment\"]),\n    )\n}\n\nfn show_subsources&lt;'a&gt;(\n    scx: &amp;'a StatementContext&lt;'a&gt;,",

        "contents": "fn expand_select_item&lt;'a&gt;(\n    ecx: &amp;ExprContext,\n    s: &amp;'a SelectItem&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    table_func_names: &amp;BTreeMap&lt;String, Ident&gt;,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;Vec&lt;(ExpandedSelectItem&lt;'a&gt;, ColumnName)&gt;, PlanError&gt; {\n    match s {\n        SelectItem::Expr {\n            expr: Expr::QualifiedWildcard(table_name),\n            alias: _,\n        } =&gt; {\n            *ecx.qcx.scx.ambiguous_columns.borrow_mut() = true;\n            let table_name =\n                normalize::unresolved_item_name(UnresolvedItemName(table_name.clone()))?;\n            let out: Vec&lt;_&gt; = ecx\n                .scope\n                .items\n                .iter()\n                .enumerate()\n                .filter(|(_i, item)| item.is_from_table(&amp;table_name))\n                .map(|(i, item)| {\n                    let name = item.column_name.clone();\n                    (ExpandedSelectItem::InputOrdinal(i), name)\n                })\n                .collect();\n            if out.is_empty() {\n                sql_bail!(\"no table named '{}' in scope\", table_name);\n            }\n            Ok(out)\n        }\n        SelectItem::Expr {\n            expr: Expr::WildcardAccess(sql_expr)",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": ",\n        project: project_key,\n    })\n}\n\nfn plan_scalar_table_funcs(\n    qcx: &amp;QueryContext,\n    table_funcs: BTreeMap&lt;Function&lt;Aug&gt;, String&gt;,\n    table_func_names: &amp;mut BTreeMap&lt;String, Ident&gt;,\n    relation_expr: &amp;HirRelationExpr,\n    from_scope: &amp;Scope,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;(HirRelationExpr, Scope), PlanError&gt; {\n    let rows_from_qcx = qcx.derived_context(from_scope.clone(), qcx.relation_type(relation_expr));\n\n    for (table_func, id) in table_funcs.iter() {\n        table_func_names.insert(\n            id.clone(),\n            // TODO(parkmycar): Re-visit after having &#96;FullItemName&#96; use &#96;Ident&#96;s.\n            Ident::new_unchecked(table_func.name.full_item_name().item.clone()),\n        );\n    }\n    // If there's only a single table function, we can skip generating\n    // ordinality columns.\n    if table_funcs.len() == 1 {\n        let (table_func, id) = table_funcs.iter().next().unwrap();\n        let (expr, mut scope) =\n            plan_solitary_table_function(&amp;rows_from_qcx, table_func, None, false)?;\n\n        // A single table-function might return several columns as a record\n        let num_cols = scope.len();\n        for i in 0..scope.len() {\n            scope.items[i].table_name = Some(PartialItemName {\n                database: None,\n                schema: None,\n                item: id.clone(),\n            });\n            scope.items[i].from_single_column_function = num_cols == 1;\n            scope.items[i].allow_unqualified_references = false;\n        }\n        return Ok((expr, scope));\n    }\n    // Otherwise, plan as usual, emulating the ROWS FROM behavior\n    let (expr, mut scope, num_cols) =\n        plan_rows_from_internal(&amp;rows_from_qcx, table_funcs.keys(), None)?;\n\n    // Munge the scope so table names match with the generated ids.\n    let mut i = 0;\n    for (id, num_cols) in table_funcs.values().zip_eq(num_cols) {\n        for _ in 0..num_cols",

        "contents": "async fn purify_alter_source(\n    catalog: impl SessionCatalog,\n    stmt: AlterSourceStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    storage_configuration: &amp;StorageConfiguration,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;PurifiedStatement, PlanError&gt; {\n    let scx = StatementContext::new(None, &amp;catalog);\n    let AlterSourceStatement {\n        source_name: unresolved_source_name,\n        action,\n        if_exists,\n    } = stmt;\n\n    // Get name.\n    let item = match scx.resolve_item(RawItemName::Name(unresolved_source_name.clone())) {\n        Ok(item) =&gt; item,\n        Err(_) if if_exists =&gt; {\n            return Ok(PurifiedStatement::PurifiedAlterSource {\n                alter_source_stmt: AlterSourceStatement {\n                    source_name: unresolved_source_name,\n                    action,\n                    if_exists,\n                },\n            });\n        }\n        Err(e) =&gt; return Err(e),\n    };\n\n    // Ensure it's an ingestion-based and alterable source.\n    let desc = match item.source_desc()? {\n        Some(desc) =&gt; desc.clone().into_inline_connection(scx.catalog),\n        None =&gt; {\n            sql_bail!(\"cannot ALTER this type of source\")\n        }\n    };\n\n    let source_name = item.name();\n\n    let resolved_source_name = ResolvedItemName::Item {\n        id: item.id(),\n        qualifiers: item.name().qualifiers.clone(),\n        full_name: scx.catalog.resolve_full_name(source_name),\n        print_id: true,\n        version: RelationVersionSelector::Latest,\n    };\n\n    let partial_name = scx.catalog.minimal_qualification(source_name);\n\n    match action {\n        AlterSourceAction::AddSubsources {",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "async fn purify_alter_source(",

          },

            "text": ") -&gt; Result&lt;PurifiedStatement, PlanError&gt; {",

            "text": "    let scx = StatementContext::new(None, &amp;catalog);",

            "lineNumber": 1329

            "text": "    } = stmt;",

            "lineNumber": 1333

            "text": "    // Get name.",

            "text": "    let item = match scx.resolve_item(RawItemName::Name(unresolved_source_name.clone())) {",
            "lineNumber": 1335

            "lineNumber": 1336

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So when you do TableFactor::Table { name, alias }, it calls qcx.resolve_table_name(name). So the name "emp_src" gets resolved. For a source, the catalog must return something that has relation_desc(). So when you have a source in the catalog, and you reference it by name in a query, resolve_table_name must resolve to that source. So the source must have a relation_desc(). I will check - does a Source catalog item have relation_desc? Maybe when it's a source, the resolved "table" might actually be a table that was created from the source (if there's only one)? Or the source might expose a default relation.

I will look at how resolve_table_name works for sources - maybe when you have a source named emp_src, it resolves to the source, and the source's relation_desc() returns something. For a load generator with no subsources (Counter), maybe the relation_desc is empty or has different column names?

I will check the catalog - what does relation_desc() return for a Source?
</details></think>

---


Checking how the catalog exposes a source's relation when it's referenced in a query:

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So CatalogItem::Source(src) => Some(Cow::Borrowed(&src.desc)) - so a Source has a desc (RelationDesc). So when you reference a source in a query, you get its relation_desc(). The question is: what is the desc for a LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER source? When you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, the source gets a desc. That desc might be the "default" output - and for Counter, views() returns vec![], so there might be no default output, or the default might be something special.

I will check how the source's desc is set when it's a load generator - maybe for single-output sources the desc is set from the one output (counter schema, counter table → one column "counter"). So the source's desc might actually have the "counter" column. But the user said they get "column counter does not exist". So maybe when you reference the source by name "emp_src", the resolution doesn't give you the source's desc directly - maybe it gives you something else?

Actually wait - maybe the issue is that with the new "source as table" model, when you have force_source_table_syntax or similar, you're not allowed to SELECT from a source directly - you have to create a table first. So when the user runs the test, maybe that flag is set and they get UseTablesForSources? But the user said they get "column counter does not exist", not "use CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE". So the query is being allowed. So maybe in their environment, when you SELECT from emp_src, the name "emp_src" resolves to something that doesn't have a "counter" column. Perhaps when a source has multiple possible outputs (or no outputs?), the resolution is different. For COUNTER, views() is empty - so maybe the source doesn't have a "default" relation exposed for direct query?

I will look at how a Source gets its desc in the catalog when it's a load generator.
</details></think>

        "contents": "impl Source {\n    /// Creates a new &#96;Source&#96;.\n    ///\n    /// # Panics\n    /// - If an ingestion-based plan is not given a cluster_id.\n    /// - If a non-ingestion-based source has a defined cluster config in its plan.\n    /// - If a non-ingestion-based source is given a cluster_id.\n    pub fn new(\n        plan: CreateSourcePlan,\n        global_id: GlobalId,\n        resolved_ids: ResolvedIds,\n        custom_logical_compaction_window: Option&lt;CompactionWindow&gt;,\n        is_retained_metrics_object: bool,\n    ) -&gt; Source {\n        Source {\n            create_sql: Some(plan.source.create_sql),\n            data_source: match plan.source.data_source {\n                mz_sql::plan::DataSourceDesc::Ingestion(desc) =&gt; DataSourceDesc::Ingestion {\n                    desc,\n                    cluster_id: plan\n                        .in_cluster\n                        .expect(\"ingestion-based sources must be given a cluster ID\"),\n                },\n                mz_sql::plan::DataSourceDesc::OldSyntaxIngestion {\n                    desc,\n                    progress_subsource,\n                    data_config,\n                    details,\n                } =&gt; DataSourceDesc::OldSyntaxIngestion {\n                    desc,\n                    cluster_id: plan\n                        .in_cluster\n                        .expect(\"ingestion-based sources must be given a cluster ID\"),\n                    progress_subsource,\n                    data_config,\n                    details,\n                },\n                mz_sql::plan::DataSourceDesc::Progress =&gt; {\n                    assert!(\n                        plan.in_cluster.is_none(),\n                        \"subsources must not have a host config or cluster_id defined\"\n                    );\n                    DataSourceDesc::Progress\n                }\n                mz_sql::plan::DataSourceDesc::IngestionExport {\n                    ingestion_id,\n                    external_reference,",

        "contents": "        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;C: ConnectionAccess&gt; SourceConnection for GenericSourceConnection&lt;C&gt; {\n    fn name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        match self {\n            Self::Kafka(conn) =&gt; conn.name(),\n            Self::Postgres(conn) =&gt; conn.name(),\n            Self::MySql(conn) =&gt; conn.name(),\n            Self::SqlServer(conn) =&gt; conn.name(),\n            Self::LoadGenerator(conn) =&gt; conn.name(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn external_reference(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;&amp;str&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Self::Kafka(conn) =&gt; conn.external_reference(),\n            Self::Postgres(conn) =&gt; conn.external_reference(),\n            Self::MySql(conn) =&gt; conn.external_reference(),\n            Self::SqlServer(conn) =&gt; conn.external_reference(),\n            Self::LoadGenerator(conn) =&gt; conn.external_reference(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn default_key_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        match self {\n            Self::Kafka(conn) =&gt; conn.default_key_desc(),\n            Self::Postgres(conn) =&gt; conn.default_key_desc(),\n            Self::MySql(conn) =&gt; conn.default_key_desc(),\n            Self::SqlServer(conn) =&gt; conn.default_key_desc(),\n            Self::LoadGenerator(conn) =&gt; conn.default_key_desc(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn default_value_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        match self {\n            Self::Kafka(conn) =&gt; conn.default_value_desc(),\n            Self::Postgres(conn) =&gt; conn.default_value_desc(),\n            Self::MySql(conn) =&gt; conn.default_value_desc(),\n            Self::SqlServer(conn) =&gt; conn.default_value_desc(),\n            Self::LoadGenerator(conn) =&gt; conn.default_value_desc(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn timestamp_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        match self {\n            Self::Kafka(conn) =&gt; conn.timestamp_desc(),\n            Self::Postgres(conn) =&gt; conn.timestamp_desc(),\n            Self::MySql(conn) =&gt; conn.timestamp_desc(),\n            Self::SqlServer(conn) =&gt; conn.timestamp_desc(),\n            Self::LoadGenerator(conn) =&gt; conn.timestamp_desc(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn connection_id(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;CatalogItemId&gt;",

        "contents": "// Copyright Materialize, Inc. and contributors. All rights reserved.\n//\n// Use of this software is governed by the Business Source License\n// included in the LICENSE file.\n//\n// As of the Change Date specified in that file, in accordance with\n// the Business Source License, use of this software will be governed\n// by the Apache License, Version 2.0.\n\n//! Types related to load generator sources\n\nuse std::sync::LazyLock;\nuse std::time::Duration;\n\nuse mz_ore::now::NowFn;\nuse mz_proto::{ProtoType, RustType, TryFromProtoError};\nuse mz_repr::adt::numeric::NumericMaxScale;\nuse mz_repr::{CatalogItemId, Diff, GlobalId, RelationDesc, Row, SqlScalarType};\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse std::collections::BTreeSet;\n\nuse crate::AlterCompatible;\nuse crate::sources::AlterError;\nuse crate::sources::{MzOffset, SourceConnection};\n\ninclude!(concat!(\n    env!(\"OUT_DIR\"),\n    \"/mz_storage_types.sources.load_generator.rs\"\n));\n\npub const LOAD_GENERATOR_KEY_VALUE_OFFSET_DEFAULT: &amp;str = \"offset\";\n\n/// Data and progress events of the native stream.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum Event&lt;F: IntoIterator, D&gt; {\n    /// Indicates that timestamps have advanced to frontier F\n    Progress(F),\n    /// Indicates that event D happened at time T\n    Message(F::Item, D),\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct LoadGeneratorSourceConnection {\n    pub load_generator: LoadGenerator,\n    pub tick_micros: Option&lt;u64&gt;,\n    pub as_of: u64,\n    pub up_to: u64,\n}\n\npub static LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC: LazyLock&lt;RelationDesc&gt; = LazyLock::new(|| {\n    RelationDesc::builder()\n        .with_column(\"offset\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(true))\n        .finish()\n});\n\nimpl SourceConnection for LoadGeneratorSourceConnection",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": ")\n            .collect();\n\n        let connection = GenericSourceConnection::LoadGenerator(LoadGeneratorSourceConnection {\n            load_generator: LoadGenerator::Auction,\n            tick_micros: Default::default(),\n            as_of: Default::default(),\n            up_to: Default::default(),\n        });\n\n        IngestionDescription {\n            desc: SourceDesc {\n                connection,\n                timestamp_interval: Default::default(),\n            },\n            remap_metadata: CollectionMetadata {\n                persist_location: PersistLocation {\n                    blob_uri: SensitiveUrl::from_str(\"mem://\").expect(\"invalid URL\"),\n                    consensus_uri: SensitiveUrl::from_str(\"mem://\").expect(\"invalid URL\"),\n                },\n                data_shard: Default::default(),\n                relation_desc: RelationDesc::new(\n                    SqlRelationType {\n                        column_types: Default::default(),\n                        keys: Default::default(),\n                    },\n                    Vec::&lt;String&gt;::new(),\n                ),\n                txns_shard: Default::default(),\n            },\n            source_exports,\n            instance_id: StorageInstanceId::system(0).expect(\"0 is a valid ID\"),\n            remap_collection_id: GlobalId::User(remap_collection_id),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn sink_description() -&gt;",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 84,
            "column": 26
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 131,
            "column": 5
          }
        },
        "contents": "            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; {\n                let mut desc = RelationDesc::builder()\n                    .with_column(\"rowid\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false));\n                let typs = SqlScalarType::enumerate();\n                let mut names = BTreeSet::new();\n                for typ in typs {\n                    // Cut out variant information from the debug print.\n                    let mut name = format!(\"_{:?}\", typ)\n                        .split(' ')\n                        .next()\n                        .unwrap()\n                        .to_lowercase();\n                    // Incase we ever have multiple variants of the same type, create\n                    // unique names for them.\n                    while names.contains(&amp;name) {\n                        name.push('_');\n                    }\n                    names.insert(name.clone());\n                    desc = desc.with_column(name, typ.clone().nullable(true));\n                }\n                desc.finish()\n            }\n            LoadGenerator::Counter { .. } =&gt; RelationDesc::builder()\n                .with_column(\"counter\", SqlScalarType::Int64.nullable(false))\n                .finish(),\n            LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n            LoadGenerator::Tpch { .. } =&gt; RelationDesc::empty(),\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue(KeyValueLoadGenerator { include_offset, .. }) =&gt; {\n                let mut desc = RelationDesc::builder()\n                    .with_column(\"partition\", SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false))\n                    .with_column(\"value\", SqlScalarType::Bytes.nullable(false));\n\n                if let Some(offset_name) = include_offset.as_deref() {\n                    desc = desc.with_column(offset_name, SqlScalarType::UInt64.nullable(false));\n                }\n                desc.finish()\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn timestamp_desc(&amp;self) -&gt; RelationDesc {\n        LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC.clone()\n    }\n\n    fn connection_id(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;CatalogItemId&gt; {\n        None\n    }",

        "detailedLines": [

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/sql/src/pure.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

          },

            "line": 753,
            "column": 56
          }
        },
        "contents": "    /// object.\n    Required,\n}\n\nasync fn purify_create_source(\n    catalog: impl SessionCatalog,\n    now: u64,\n    mut create_source_stmt: CreateSourceStatement&lt;Aug&gt;,\n    storage_configuration: &amp;StorageConfiguration,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;PurifiedStatement, PlanError&gt; {\n    let CreateSourceStatement {\n        name: source_name,\n        col_names,\n        key_constraint,\n        connection: source_connection,\n        format,\n        envelope,\n        include_metadata,\n        external_references,\n        progress_subsource,\n        with_options,\n        ..\n    } = &amp;mut create_source_stmt;\n\n    let uses_old_syntax = !col_names.is_empty()\n        || key_constraint.is_some()\n        || format.is_some()\n        || envelope.is_some()\n        || !include_metadata.is_empty()\n        || external_references.is_some()\n        || progress_subsource.is_some();\n\n    if let Some(DeferredItemName::Named(_)) = progress_subsource {\n        sql_bail!(\"Cannot manually ID qualify progress subsource\")\n    }\n\n    let mut requested_subsource_map = BTreeMap::new();\n\n    let progress_desc = match &amp;source_connection {\n        CreateSourceConnection::Kafka { .. } =&gt; {\n            &amp;mz_storage_types::sources::kafka::KAFKA_PROGRESS_DESC\n        }\n        CreateSourceConnection::Postgres { .. } =&gt; {\n            &amp;mz_storage_types::sources::postgres::PG_PROGRESS_DESC\n        }\n        CreateSourceConnection::SqlServer { .. } =&gt; {\n            &amp;mz_storage_types::sources::sql_server::SQL_SERVER_PROGRESS_DESC\n        }\n        CreateSourceConnection::MySql { .. } =&gt; {\n            &amp;mz_storage_types::sources::mysql::MYSQL_PROGRESS_DESC\n        }\n        CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { .. } =&gt; {\n            &amp;mz_storage_types::sources::load_generator::LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC\n        }\n    };\n    let scx = StatementContext::new(None, &amp;catalog);\n\n    // Depending on if the user must or can use the &#96;CREATE TABLE .. FROM SOURCE&#96; statement\n    // to add tables after this source is created we might need to enforce that\n    // auto-generated subsources are created or not created by this source statement.\n    let reference_policy = if scx.catalog.system_vars().",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        "contents": "        match self {\n            // NOTE(aljoscha): We could move the block for ingestions into\n            // &#96;SourceEnvelope&#96; itself, but that one feels more like an internal\n            // thing and adapter should own how we represent envelopes as a\n            // string? It would not be hard to convince me otherwise, though.\n            DataSourceDesc::Ingestion { .. } =&gt; None,\n            DataSourceDesc::OldSyntaxIngestion { data_config, .. } =&gt; {\n                Some(envelope_string(&amp;data_config.envelope))\n            }\n            DataSourceDesc::IngestionExport { data_config, .. } =&gt; {\n                Some(envelope_string(&amp;data_config.envelope))\n            }\n            DataSourceDesc::Introspection(_)\n            | DataSourceDesc::Webhook { .. }\n            | DataSourceDesc::Progress =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct Source {\n    /// Parse-able SQL that defines this table.\n    pub create_sql: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n    /// [&#96;GlobalId&#96;] used to reference this source from outside the catalog.\n    pub global_id: GlobalId,\n    // TODO: Unskip: currently blocked on some inner BTreeMap&lt;X, _&gt; problems.\n    #[serde(skip)]\n    pub data_source: DataSourceDesc,\n    /// [&#96;RelationDesc&#96;] of this source, derived from the &#96;create_sql&#96;.\n    pub desc: RelationDesc,\n    /// The timeline this source exists on.\n    pub timeline: Timeline,\n    /// Other catalog objects referenced by this table, e.g. custom types.\n    pub resolved_ids: ResolvedIds,\n    /// This value is ignored for subsources, i.e. for\n    /// [&#96;DataSourceDesc::IngestionExport&#96;]. Instead, it uses the primary\n    /// sources logical compaction window.\n    pub custom_logical_compaction_window: Option&lt;CompactionWindow&gt;,\n    /// Whether the source's logical compaction window is controlled by\n    /// METRICS_RETENTION\n    pub is_retained_metrics_object: bool,\n}\n\nimpl Source {\n    /// Creates a new &#96;Source&#96;.\n    ///\n    /// # Panics",

        "contents": "    let plan = if is_table {\n        let data_source = DataSourceDesc::Webhook {\n            validate_using,\n            body_format,\n            headers,\n            cluster_id: Some(in_cluster.id()),\n        };\n        let data_source = TableDataSource::DataSource {\n            desc: data_source,\n            timeline,\n        };\n        Plan::CreateTable(CreateTablePlan {\n            name,\n            if_not_exists,\n            table: Table {\n                create_sql,\n                desc: VersionedRelationDesc::new(desc),\n                temporary: false,\n                compaction_window: None,\n                data_source,\n            },\n        })\n    } else {\n        let data_source = DataSourceDesc::Webhook {\n            validate_using,\n            body_format,\n            headers,\n            // Important: The cluster is set at the &#96;Source&#96; level.\n            cluster_id: None,\n        };\n        Plan::CreateSource(CreateSourcePlan {\n            name,\n            source: Source {\n                create_sql,\n                data_source,\n                desc,\n                compaction_window: None,\n            },\n            if_not_exists,\n            timeline,\n            in_cluster: Some(in_cluster.id()),\n        })\n    };\n\n    Ok(plan)\n}\n\npub fn plan_create_source",

            "line": 1438,

          }
        },
        "contents": "            CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { generator, options } =&gt; {\n                f.write_str(\"LOAD GENERATOR \");\n                f.write_node(generator);\n                if !options.is_empty() {\n                    f.write_str(\" (\");\n                    f.write_node(&amp;display::comma_separated(options));\n                    f.write_str(\")\");\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\nimpl_display_t!(CreateSourceConnection);\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum LoadGenerator {\n    Clock,\n    Counter,\n    Marketing,\n    Auction,\n    Datums,\n    Tpch,\n    KeyValue,\n}\n\nimpl AstDisplay for LoadGenerator {\n    fn fmt&lt;W: fmt::Write&gt;(&amp;self, f: &amp;mut AstFormatter&lt;W&gt;) {\n        match self {\n            Self::Counter =&gt; f.write_str(\"COUNTER\"),\n            Self::Clock =&gt; f.write_str(\"CLOCK\"),\n            Self::Marketing =&gt; f.write_str(\"MARKETING\"),\n            Self::Auction =&gt; f.write_str(\"AUCTION\"),\n            Self::Datums =&gt; f.write_str(\"DATUMS\"),\n            Self::Tpch =&gt; f.write_str(\"TPCH\"),\n            Self::KeyValue =&gt; f.write_str(\"KEY VALUE\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\nimpl_display!(LoadGenerator);\n\nimpl LoadGenerator {\n    /// Corresponds with the same mapping on the &#96;LoadGenerator&#96; enum defined in\n    /// src/storage-types/src/sources/load_generator.rs, but re-defined here for\n    /// cases where we only have the AST representation. This can be removed once\n    /// the &#96;ast_rewrite_sources_to_tables&#96; migration is removed.\n    pub fn schema_name(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;'static str {\n        match self {\n            LoadGenerator::Counter =&gt; \"counter\",\n            LoadGenerator::Clock =&gt; \"clock\",\n            LoadGenerator::Marketing =&gt; \"marketing\",\n            LoadGenerator::Auction =&gt; \"auction\",\n            LoadGenerator::Datums =&gt; \"datums\",\n            LoadGenerator::Tpch =&gt; \"tpch\",\n            LoadGenerator::KeyValue =&gt; \"key_value\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]\npub enum LoadGeneratorOptionName {\n    ScaleFactor,\n    TickInterval,\n    AsOf",

        "contents": "            if let Some(exclude_cols_option) = options\n                .iter_mut()\n                .find(|option| option.name == MySqlConfigOptionName::ExcludeColumns)\n            {\n                exclude_cols_option.value =\n                    Some(WithOptionValue::Sequence(normalized_exclude_columns));\n            }\n        }\n        CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { generator, options } =&gt; {\n            let load_generator =\n                load_generator_ast_to_generator(&amp;scx, generator, options, include_metadata)?;\n\n            let reference_client = SourceReferenceClient::LoadGenerator {\n                generator: &amp;load_generator,\n            };\n            retrieved_source_references = reference_client.get_source_references().await?;\n            // Filter to the references that need to be created as 'subsources', which\n            // doesn't include the default output for single-output sources.\n            // TODO(database-issues#8620): Remove once subsources are removed\n            let multi_output_sources =\n                retrieved_source_references\n                    .all_references()\n                    .iter()\n                    .any(|r| {\n                        r.load_generator_output().expect(\"is loadgen\")\n                            != &amp;LoadGeneratorOutput::Default\n                    });\n\n            match external_references",

        "contents": "    pub timestamp_interval: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl&lt;R: ConnectionResolver&gt; IntoInlineConnection&lt;SourceDesc, R&gt;\n    for SourceDesc&lt;ReferencedConnection&gt;\n{\n    fn into_inline_connection(self, r: R) -&gt; SourceDesc {\n        let SourceDesc {\n            connection,\n            timestamp_interval,\n        } = self;\n\n        SourceDesc {\n            connection: connection.into_inline_connection(&amp;r),\n            timestamp_interval,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;C: ConnectionAccess&gt; AlterCompatible for SourceDesc&lt;C&gt; {\n    /// Determines if &#96;self&#96; is compatible with another &#96;SourceDesc&#96;, in such a\n    /// way that it is possible to turn &#96;self&#96; into &#96;other&#96; through a valid\n    /// series of transformations (e.g. no transformation or &#96;ALTER SOURCE&#96;).\n    fn alter_compatible(&amp;self, id: GlobalId, other: &amp;Self) -&gt; Result&lt;(), AlterError&gt; {\n        if self == other {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n        let Self {\n            connection,\n            timestamp_interval,\n        } = &amp;self;\n\n        let compatibility_checks = [\n            (\n                connection.alter_compatible(id, &amp;other.connection).is_ok(),\n                \"connection\",\n            ),\n            (\n                timestamp_interval == &amp;other.timestamp_interval,\n                \"timestamp_interval\",\n            ),\n        ];\n\n        for (compatible, field) in compatibility_checks {\n            if !compatible {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    \"SourceDesc incompatible {field}:\\nself:\\n{:#?}\\n\\nother\\n{:#?}\",\n                    self,\n                    other\n                );\n\n                return Err(AlterError { id });\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Eq, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum GenericSourceConnection&lt;C: ConnectionAccess = InlinedConnection&gt; {\n    Kafka(KafkaSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;),\n    Postgres(PostgresSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;),\n    MySql(MySqlSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;),\n    SqlServer(SqlServerSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;),\n    LoadGenerator(LoadGeneratorSourceConnection),\n}\n\nimpl&lt;C: ConnectionAccess&gt; From&lt;KafkaSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;&gt; for",

        "contents": "impl WithOptionName for SqlServerConfigOptionName {\n    /// # WARNING\n    ///\n    /// Whenever implementing this trait consider very carefully whether or not\n    /// this value could contain sensitive user data. If you're uncertain, err\n    /// on the conservative side and return &#96;true&#96;.\n    fn redact_value(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match self {\n            SqlServerConfigOptionName::Details\n            | SqlServerConfigOptionName::TextColumns\n            | SqlServerConfigOptionName::ExcludeColumns =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]\n/// An option in a &#96;{FROM|INTO} CONNECTION ...&#96; statement.\npub struct SqlServerConfigOption&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; {\n    pub name: SqlServerConfigOptionName,\n    pub value: Option&lt;WithOptionValue&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n}\nimpl_display_for_with_option!(SqlServerConfigOption);\nimpl_display_t!(SqlServerConfigOption);\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub enum CreateSourceConnection&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; {\n    Kafka {\n        connection: T::ItemName,\n        options: Vec&lt;KafkaSourceConfigOption&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n    },\n    Postgres {\n        connection: T::ItemName,\n        options: Vec&lt;PgConfigOption&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n    },\n    SqlServer {\n        connection: T::ItemName,\n        options: Vec&lt;SqlServerConfigOption&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n    },\n    MySql {\n        connection: T::ItemName,\n        options: Vec&lt;MySqlConfigOption&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n    },\n    LoadGenerator {\n        generator: LoadGenerator,\n        options: Vec&lt;LoadGeneratorOption&lt;T&gt;&gt;,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl&lt;T: AstInfo&gt; AstDisplay for CreateSourceConnection&lt;T&gt;",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": ")\n            }\n            LOAD =&gt; {\n                self.expect_keyword(GENERATOR)?;\n                let generator = match self.expect_one_of_keywords(&amp;[\n                    CLOCK, COUNTER, MARKETING, AUCTION, TPCH, DATUMS, KEY,\n                ])? {\n                    CLOCK =&gt; LoadGenerator::Clock,\n                    COUNTER =&gt; LoadGenerator::Counter,\n                    AUCTION =&gt; LoadGenerator::Auction,\n                    TPCH =&gt; LoadGenerator::Tpch,\n                    DATUMS =&gt; LoadGenerator::Datums,\n                    MARKETING =&gt; LoadGenerator::Marketing,\n                    KEY =&gt; {\n                        self.expect_keyword(VALUE)?;\n                        LoadGenerator::KeyValue\n                    }\n                    _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n                };\n                let options = if self.consume_token(&amp;Token::LParen) {\n                    let options =\n                        self.parse_comma_separated(Parser::parse_load_generator_option)?;\n                    self.expect_token(&amp;Token::RParen)?;\n                    options\n                } else {\n                    vec![]\n                };\n                Ok(CreateSourceConnection::LoadGenerator { generator, options })\n            }\n            _ =&gt; unreachable!(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_pg_connection_option(&amp;mut self) -&gt;",

        "contents": "        for plan::CreateSourcePlanBundle {\n            item_id,\n            global_id,\n            mut plan,\n            resolved_ids,\n            available_source_references,\n        } in plans\n        {\n            let name = plan.name.clone();\n\n            match plan.source.data_source {\n                plan::DataSourceDesc::Ingestion(ref desc)\n                | plan::DataSourceDesc::OldSyntaxIngestion { ref desc, .. } =&gt; {\n                    let cluster_id = plan\n                        .in_cluster\n                        .expect(\"ingestion plans must specify cluster\");\n                    match desc.connection {\n                        GenericSourceConnection::Postgres(_)\n                        | GenericSourceConnection::MySql(_)\n                        | GenericSourceConnection::SqlServer(_)\n                        | GenericSourceConnection::Kafka(_)\n                        | GenericSourceConnection::LoadGenerator(_) =&gt; {\n                            if let Some(cluster) = self.catalog().try_get_cluster(cluster_id) {\n                                let enable_multi_replica_sources = ENABLE_MULTI_REPLICA_SOURCES\n                                    .get(self.catalog().system_config().dyncfgs());\n\n                                if !enable_multi_replica_sources &amp;&amp; cluster.replica_ids().len() &gt; 1\n                                {\n                                    return Err(AdapterError::Unsupported(\n                                        \"sources in clusters with &gt;1 replicas\",\n                                    ));\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                plan::DataSourceDesc::Webhook { .. } =&gt; {\n                    let cluster_id = plan.in_cluster.expect(\"webhook plans must specify cluster\");",

        "contents": "            }\n        };\n        DataSourceDesc::IngestionExport {\n            ingestion_id,\n            external_reference,\n            details,\n            // Subsources don't currently support non-default envelopes / encoding\n            data_config: SourceExportDataConfig {\n                envelope: SourceEnvelope::None(NoneEnvelope {\n                    key_envelope: KeyEnvelope::None,\n                    key_arity: 0,\n                }),\n                encoding: None,\n            },\n        }\n    } else if progress {\n        DataSourceDesc::Progress\n    } else {\n        panic!(\"subsources must specify one of &#96;external_reference&#96;, &#96;progress&#96;, or &#96;references&#96;\")\n    };\n\n    let if_not_exists = *if_not_exists;\n    let name = scx.allocate_qualified_name(normalize::unresolved_item_name(name.clone())?)?;\n\n    let create_sql = normalize::create_statement(scx, Statement::CreateSubsource(stmt))?;\n\n    let compaction_window = plan_retain_history_option(scx, retain_history)?;\n    let source = Source {\n        create_sql,\n        data_source,\n        desc,\n        compaction_window,\n    };\n\n    Ok(Plan::CreateSource(CreateSourcePlan {\n        name,\n        source,\n        if_not_exists,\n        timeline: Timeline::EpochMilliseconds,\n        in_cluster: None,\n    }))\n}\n\ngenerate_extracted_config!(\n    TableFromSourceOption,\n    (TextColumns, Vec::&lt;Ident&gt;, Default(vec![])),\n    (ExcludeColumns, Vec::&lt;Ident&gt;, Default(vec![])),\n    (PartitionBy, Vec&lt;Ident&gt;),\n    (RetainHistory, OptionalDuration),\n    (Details, String)\n);\n\npub fn plan_create_table_from_source(\n    scx: &amp;StatementContext,",

    {
      "codeBlock": {

        "range": {

            "column": 27
          }
        },
        "contents": "        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl&lt;C: ConnectionAccess&gt; SourceExportDataConfig&lt;C&gt; {\n    /// Returns &#96;true&#96; if this connection yields data that is\n    /// append-only/monotonic. Append-monly means the source\n    /// never produces retractions.\n    // TODO(guswynn): consider enforcing this more completely at the\n    // parsing/typechecking level, by not using an &#96;envelope&#96;\n    // for sources like pg\n    pub fn monotonic(&amp;self, connection: &amp;GenericSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;) -&gt; bool {\n        match &amp;self.envelope {\n            // Upsert and CdcV2 may produce retractions.\n            SourceEnvelope::Upsert(_) | SourceEnvelope::CdcV2 =&gt; false,\n            SourceEnvelope::None(_) =&gt; {\n                match connection {\n                    // Postgres can produce retractions (deletes).\n                    GenericSourceConnection::Postgres(_) =&gt; false,\n                    // MySQL can produce retractions (deletes).\n                    GenericSourceConnection::MySql(_) =&gt; false,\n                    // SQL Server can produce retractions (deletes).\n                    GenericSourceConnection::SqlServer(_) =&gt; false,\n                    // Whether or not a Loadgen source can produce retractions varies.\n                    GenericSourceConnection::LoadGenerator(g) =&gt; g.load_generator.is_monotonic(),\n                    // Kafka exports with &#96;None&#96; envelope are append-only.\n                    GenericSourceConnection::Kafka(_) =&gt; true,\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// An external source of updates for a relational collection.\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, Serialize, Deserialize, Eq, PartialEq)]\npub struct SourceDesc&lt;C: ConnectionAccess = InlinedConnection&gt; {\n    pub connection: GenericSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;,\n    pub timestamp_interval: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl&lt;R: ConnectionResolver&gt;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "        Ok(())",

            "lineNumber": 580

            "lineNumber": 581

          {
            "text": "    /// never produces retractions.",
            "lineNumber": 582
          },
          {
            "text": "    // TODO(guswynn): consider enforcing this more completely at the",
            "lineNumber": 583

          {
            "text": "    // parsing/typechecking level, by not using an &#96;envelope&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 584
          },
          {
            "text": "    // for sources like pg",
            "lineNumber": 585

          {
            "text": "    pub fn monotonic(&amp;self, connection: &amp;GenericSourceConnection&lt;C&gt;) -&gt; bool {",
            "lineNumber": 586

            "text": "            SourceEnvelope::Upsert(_) | SourceEnvelope::CdcV2 =&gt; false,",
            "lineNumber": 589
          },

            "text": "            SourceEnvelope::None(_) =&gt; {",
            "lineNumber": 590
          },
          {
            "text": "                match connection {",
            "lineNumber": 591
          },
          {
            "text": "                    // Postgres can produce retractions (deletes).",
            "lineNumber": 592
          },
          {
            "text": "                    GenericSourceConnection::Postgres(_) =&gt; false,",
            "lineNumber": 593
          },
          {
            "text": "                    // MySQL can produce retractions (deletes).",
            "lineNumber": 594
          },
          {
            "text": "                    GenericSourceConnection::MySql(_) =&gt; false,",
            "lineNumber": 595

            "text": "                    // SQL Server can produce retractions (deletes).",

          },

            "text": "                    GenericSourceConnection::SqlServer(_) =&gt; false,",
            "lineNumber": 597

            "text": "                    // Whether or not a Loadgen source can produce retractions varies.",

            "text": "                    GenericSourceConnection::LoadGenerator(g) =&gt; g.load_generator.is_monotonic(),",
            "lineNumber": 599
          },
          {
            "text": "                    // Kafka exports with &#96;None&#96; envelope are append-only.",
            "lineNumber": 600

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So when you CREATE SOURCE, the plan has a `desc` - and for load generators with the "old syntax" (no REFERENCE), the code path uses external_connection.timestamp_desc() in one branch. I will look at the ddl.rs again - when you create a source from LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (no REFERENCE), what desc does the source get?

Looking at plan/statement/ddl.rs around 941-962: when there's an external_reference (REFERENCE "counter"), it creates DataSourceDesc::OldSyntaxIngestion with a desc. When there's None (no REFERENCE), it uses external_connection.timestamp_desc() - that's the LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC, not the counter column! So when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (without creating a table with REFERENCE), the source might get the *progress* desc (timestamp_desc()), not the counter data desc. So when you SELECT from emp_src, you get the progress relation, which doesn't have a "counter" column - hence "column counter does not exist".

So to query the underlying *data* of the source (the counter values), you have to go through a table created with CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE ... (REFERENCE "counter"). The source object itself when used in a query might expose the progress relation, not the data.

So the answer for the user: In the current model, you don't "query the source directly" for the counter data. The source (emp_src) when referenced in a query exposes a relation that may be the progress/timestamp relation, not the counter output. To get the counter data you must create a table with CREATE TABLE t FROM SOURCE emp_src (REFERENCE "counter") and query t. So for the test, the only way to hit the "invalid selection" when the selection depends on the source is:
1. Via a view that depends on a source-export table (we have this)
2. Via the source-export table directly (we have this)
3. If we could query the source's data relation directly, we'd need something like SELECT ... FROM emp_src (REFERENCE "counter") - but I'm not sure that syntax exists for SELECT. I will check if there's a way to specify REFERENCE in a query.

Actually re-reading the doc: "SELECT refs.* FROM mz_internal.mz_source_references refs, mz_sources s WHERE s.name = '<source_name>' ..." - that's for finding upstream table names, not for querying. So there might not be a way to SELECT from a source with a specific REFERENCE in a single query. The design is: you create tables from sources with REFERENCE, and you query the tables.

So the answer to the user is: In Materialize's current model, you don't query the underlying source's data directly by name (e.g. FROM emp_src). The source object, when used in a query, may expose a different relation (e.g. progress). To read the counter data you must use a table created with CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE ... (REFERENCE "counter"). So we can't add a "direct from source" test that uses the counter column - we're limited to the two cases we have. I'll explain this and suggest we could try SELECT * FROM emp_src to at least trigger the "invalid selection" error (if the source relation is still considered a source dependency), and use that - we wouldn't need to reference the "counter" column by name.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (no REFERENCE / no progress subsource), the code takes the None branch at line 956-964: desc = external_connection.timestamp_desc(). So the source gets the *timestamp/progress* relation desc, not the counter data. That's why SELECT counter FROM emp_src fails - the source's relation doesn't have a "counter" column.

So to "query the underlying source" for the counter *data*, the user has two options in the current design:
1. Create a table with CREATE TABLE t FROM SOURCE emp_src (REFERENCE "counter") and query t - that's what we're already testing.
2. There might be a way to reference the source's data output in a query - like mz_load_generators.counter - I will check if that's a queryable object.

Actually - when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER, you're not specifying REFERENCE. So the source "emp_src" is the top-level source object. Its desc is timestamp_desc() (progress). The actual counter *data* is exposed when you do CREATE TABLE t FROM SOURCE emp_src (REFERENCE "counter") - that creates a *table* (source export) that reads from the source's "counter" output. So in the catalog you have: (1) Source emp_src with progress desc, (2) Table employees_from_src with counter desc (source export). When you SELECT from emp_src you get the source's desc = progress. When you SELECT from employees_from_src you get the table's desc = counter.

So the only way to get "counter" column is from the table. To "query the underlying source" in the sense of "get the same data", you use the table. To trigger the INSERT error by selecting from "the source" (the catalog Source object), we could do SELECT * FROM emp_src - that would (1) depend on the source, so we'd get the invalid selection error, and (2) not require a "counter" column - we'd just need to match the progress relation's columns. I will check what columns timestamp_desc() has for load generators.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So when you CREATE SOURCE emp_src FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER (no REFERENCE), the source gets desc = LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC which has one column: "offset" (UInt64, nullable). So when you SELECT * FROM emp_src, you get one column "offset", not "counter". So we can add the direct-from-source test case by doing:

INSERT INTO salary_exceptions (employee, salary, type)
SELECT 'Dave', offset::int, 'contractor' FROM emp_src LIMIT 1

That would (1) reference the source directly, (2) use the column that actually exists ("offset"), and (3) trigger the "invalid selection" error. I will add that to the test and restore the direct-from-source case.
</details></think>

---

The `counter` data is only available via a table created with `CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE ... (REFERENCE "counter")`. Adding the direct-from-source test using `SELECT ... FROM emp_src` and the `offset` column:

Recreating the test file and adding the direct-from-source case. Querying the source by name (`FROM emp_src`) exposes the source's progress relation, which has one column — `offset`, not `counter`. Using that column in the INSERT:


---

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file_v2">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file_v2** • Edit file: /Users/michael.abebe/Code/materialize/test/sqllogictest/insert-into-select-source-error.slt</summary>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Done. I've recreated the test file and added the direct-from-source case. The key insight:

- When you **query the source by name** (`FROM emp_src`), you're not getting the counter *data* — you're getting the relation that the **source object** exposes. For a LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER source created without REFERENCE, that relation is the **progress** relation (used for timestamping), which has one column: **`offset`** (from `LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC`).
- The **counter data** (column `counter`) is only available when you create a **table** with `CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE emp_src` (which implicitly uses REFERENCE "counter" for single-output load generators) and then query that table.

So to "query against the underlying source" in the sense of "have the SELECT depend on the source and trigger the error":
- Use `SELECT ... FROM emp_src` and reference the column that actually exists on that relation: **`offset`** (e.g. `SELECT 'Dave', offset::int, 'contractor' FROM emp_src LIMIT 1`).

That gives you all three error cases: via view, via source-export table, and via source directly.
</details></think>

---


Summary of what’s going on and what was added:

**Why `counter` doesn’t exist on the source**

When you use the source in a query (`FROM emp_src`), you’re not reading the counter *data*. You’re reading the relation that the **source object** exposes. For a LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER source created with plain `CREATE SOURCE ... FROM LOAD GENERATOR COUNTER` (no REFERENCE), that relation is the **progress** relation (used for timestamping). Its schema comes from `LOAD_GEN_PROGRESS_DESC` and has a single column: **`offset`** (bigint), not `counter`.

The **counter** column only exists on relations that are the *data* output of the load generator. That’s what you get when you create a **table** from the source, e.g. `CREATE TABLE employees_from_src FROM SOURCE emp_src` (which uses the default REFERENCE for the single output). So:

- **`FROM emp_src`** → progress relation → column **`offset`**
- **`FROM employees_from_src`** → counter data → column **`counter`**

**How to query “against the underlying source” for this test**

To hit the “invalid selection” error by selecting from the source itself (no table in between), you have to use the column that actually exists on that relation: **`offset`**.

The test file was recreated and the direct-from-source case was added as:

```sql
INSERT INTO salary_exceptions (employee, salary, type)
SELECT 'Dave', offset::int, 'contractor' FROM emp_src LIMIT 1
```

So you do query the underlying source (`emp_src`); you just use `offset` instead of `counter` because that’s the only column on the relation you get when you select from the source by name. The test now has all three failing cases: via view, via source-export table, and directly from the source.

---

_**User**_

It seems like I can't select offset or offset::int from emp_src, but I can select *. So I need a way to get the * into the column

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is saying they can SELECT * FROM emp_src but they can't select "offset" or "offset::int" by name. So the column might not be named "offset" in the scope, or there's something different about how it's exposed. But SELECT * works.

They need to get the * (the single column from emp_src) into one of the INSERT columns. So they need something like:
INSERT INTO salary_exceptions (employee, salary, type)
SELECT 'Dave', <something that is the single column from emp_src>, 'contractor' FROM emp_src LIMIT 1

If SELECT * FROM emp_src returns one column (let's call it col0 or whatever), they could use a subquery or row semantics. In SQL, one way to get "the single column" when you have SELECT * is to use a subquery that returns one row and one column, then reference it. For example:
- (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) - this is a table subquery, not a scalar
- In some SQL you can do: SELECT 'Dave', (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1).* - but that might expand to multiple columns
- If there's exactly one column, (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) in a scalar context might give the one value - but in standard SQL a subquery in scalar context must return exactly one row and one column. So (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) - if it returns one row and one column, might work as a scalar in some dialects. I will check - in PostgreSQL/Materialize, if you have SELECT (SELECT * FROM t LIMIT 1) and t has one column, does that work? Actually I think in PostgreSQL, (SELECT * FROM t LIMIT 1) when t has one column returns a row type (record), not a scalar. So you'd need to unpack it.

Another approach: use a derived table and reference by position. For example:
INSERT INTO salary_exceptions (employee, salary, type)
SELECT 'Dave', f1, 'contractor' FROM (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) AS t(f1)

That gives the single column an alias f1, then we can use f1 in the outer SELECT. That should work!
</details></think>

---


Using a derived table to alias the single column from `emp_src` so we can reference it:

I'll change the direct-from-source case to use a derived table so we can reference the single column by alias. The source's relation has one column (offset); we do SELECT * FROM emp_src in a subquery and give it a column alias, e.g. (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) AS t(f1), then use f1 in the outer SELECT.
</details></think>

</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Done. The pattern is:
FROM (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) AS t(f1)

This gives the single column from emp_src the alias "f1" in the outer scope, so we can use f1::int in the SELECT list. The INSERT still depends on emp_src (the source), so it should still trigger the "invalid selection" error.
</details></think>

---


Updated the test to use a derived table so the single column from `emp_src` gets a usable name:

```sql
SELECT 'Dave', f1::int, 'contractor' FROM (SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) AS t(f1)
```

`(SELECT * FROM emp_src LIMIT 1) AS t(f1)` exposes that one column as `f1`, so the outer query can reference `f1::int`. The INSERT still reads from the source `emp_src`, so it should still raise the "invalid selection" error.

[Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...](https://github.com/dolthub/sqllogictest)<br/>3. [Result Verification](https://duckdb.org/docs/1.1/dev/sqllogictest/result_verification.html)<br/><br/># SQLLogicTest Query Types and Formats<br/><br/>**SQLLogictest** is a program designed to verify that SQL database engines compute correct results by comparing outputs across different database systems.[1]<br/><br/>## Query Types and Result Formats<br/><br/>SQLLogictest uses test scripts containing SQL statements and queries. The key components mentioned in relation to query types include:<br/><br/>- **Query Type Indicators**: The format uses single letters to denote query types, including:<br/>  - **T** (Text/String results)<br/>  - **I** (Integer results)<br/>  - **R** (Real/floating-point results)[3][5]<br/><br/>- **Result Ordering**: The **rowsort** directive is used to sort query results by row, which helps with consistent result comparison across different database engines.[3][5]<br/><br/>- **Format Options**: The **format** parameter specifies how results should be presented in test scripts.[3][5]<br/><br/>## Operation Modes<br/><br/>SQLLogictest operates in two modes:[1]<br/>1. **Completion mode**: Reads prototype scripts (without results) and generates full scripts with results inserted<br/>2. **Validation mode**: Compares actual database output against expected results in full scripts<br/><br/>This allows for comprehensive testing by automatically generating test vectors and comparing results across multiple database engines like SQLite, MySQL, and DuckDB.[1] |

</details>
</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So:
- **T** = Text/String
- **I** = Integer
- **R** = Real/floating-point

So **TIT** means: first column is Text, second is Integer, third is Text. That matches our salary_exceptions table: (employee text, salary int, type text).

**rowsort** means: sort the result rows before comparing to the expected output, so that row order doesn't matter (e.g. if the database returns rows in a different order, the test still passes).
</details></think>

---


In sqllogictest, the line means:

- **`query`** – run the following SQL as a query and compare its result to the expected output below `----`.

- **`TIT`** – expected **types** of the result columns (one letter per column):
  - **T** = Text (string)
  - **I** = Integer
  - **R** = Real (floating point)

So **TIT** = first column text, second integer, third text, which matches `salary_exceptions (employee text, salary int, type text)`.

- **`rowsort`** – before comparing to the expected result, **sort the result rows**. That makes the test insensitive to the order of rows (e.g. different execution plans). The expected block is still written in the order you want (here, one row), but the runner sorts both actual and expected by row and then compares.

So the full line:

```text
query TIT rowsort
```

means: “Run this query; expect 3 columns with types Text, Integer, Text; sort the result rows before comparing to the expected output.”

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is showing a test failure. The expected output is one value "Carol 100 full_time" (as if it's a single column/row), but the actual output is three separate values: "Carol", "100", "full_time" (three columns).

So the issue is how the expected result is formatted in the .slt file. In sqllogictest, when you have multiple columns, the expected values are typically separated - so for 3 columns (TIT), you'd have three values per row. The test has:

```
query TIT rowsort
SELECT * FROM salary_exceptions
----
Carol 100 full_time
```

So we're saying we have 3 columns (TIT) but we're only providing one "value" on the expected line: "Carol 100 full_time". The runner is interpreting that as one value, but the query returns three columns, so the actual result is ["Carol", "100", "full_time"].

In cockroach mode (which might be the default or what's used), multiple columns might be space-separated on the same line. So "Carol 100 full_time" could be read as three values: "Carol", "100", "full_time". But the error says expected is Values(["Carol 100 full_time"]) - so it's being parsed as a single value.

Looking at the doc again: "In `mode cockroach`, spaces in the query result are treated as column delimiters except when the expected result has only one column."

So when we have 3 columns (TIT), the expected line "Carol 100 full_time" might need to be formatted differently. Maybe we need to put each value on its own line (mode standard style), or use a different format. I will check - the actual result is ["Carol", "100", "full_time"] and expected is ["Carol 100 full_time"]. So the test file has one string "Carol 100 full_time" but we need three values. In cockroach mode, I think multiple columns are space-separated on one line, so "Carol 100 full_time" should be parsed as three values: Carol, 100, full_time. Unless - the issue is that the test file doesn't have "mode cockroach" set, so it's in mode standard? In mode standard, "spaces in the query result are treated as column delimiters only when it comes to column names" - so the data might be in a different format.

Looking at the doc again for mode standard:
```
row1col1
row1col2
row2col1
row2col2
```
So in standard mode, each value is on its own line! So for one row with three columns we'd need:
Carol
100
full_time

I will try that - put the three column values on separate lines for the expected output.

---


It passed with **one line + `mode cockroach`** because in cockroach mode spaces in the expected result are column delimiters, so `Carol 100 full_time` is parsed as three values (one row, three columns).

**Why it was “expecting” multiline before:** the file had no `mode` directive, so the runner used **`mode standard`** (the default, from SQLite sqllogictest). In standard mode:

- Spaces in the **expected data** are **not** column delimiters (only in column names).
- So `Carol 100 full_time` was read as **one** value: the literal string `"Carol 100 full_time"`.
- For multi-column results, standard mode uses **one value per line** in **column-major** order (all of column 1, then all of column 2, etc.).

So with the original single line and no mode:

- Expected: 1 value → `["Carol 100 full_time"]`
- Actual: 3 columns → `["Carol", "100", "full_time"]`  
→ mismatch.

The “multiline” layout (Carol, then 100, then full_time on separate lines) is the **standard**-mode way to write one row with three columns: one value per line, column-major. That’s why it passed when we switched to that format without changing mode.

**Summary:**  
- **Standard mode (default):** one value per line, column-major → multiline for multi-column.  
- **Cockroach mode:** spaces separate columns on a line → one line per row is fine.  

Adding `mode cockroach` and using `Carol 100 full_time` on one line is the right fix if you want “one row, three columns” written as a single line.

_**User**_

Ok, lets now work to understand when this error shows up. I see it is output based on InvalidTableMutationSelection based on validate_read_dependencies. When isn't the dependency valid?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user wants to understand when the InvalidTableMutationSelection error shows up - i.e. when is a dependency considered invalid by validate_read_dependencies. I already have the code in context from the previous read. I will summarize the logic clearly.

From the code:

```rust
fn validate_read_dependencies(
    catalog: &Catalog,
    id: &CatalogItemId,
) -> Result<(), AdapterError> {
    use CatalogItemType::*;
    use mz_catalog::memory::objects;
    let mut ids_to_check = Vec::new();
    let valid = match catalog.try_get_entry(id) {
        Some(entry) => {
            // First: check for mz_now in views/MVs
            if let CatalogItem::View(...) | CatalogItem::MaterializedView(...) = entry.item() {
                if optimized_expr.contains_temporal() {
                    return Err(AdapterError::Unsupported("calls to mz_now in write statements"));
                }
            }
            match entry.item().typ() {
                typ @ (Func | View | MaterializedView | ContinualTask) => {
                    ids_to_check.extend(entry.uses());
                    let valid_id = id.is_user() || matches!(typ, Func);
                    valid_id  // valid if user id or Func
                }
                Source | Secret | Connection => false,  // INVALID
                Sink | Index => unreachable!(),
                Table => {
                    if !id.is_user() {
                        false  // INVALID: non-user table
                    } else {
                        entry.source_export_details().is_none()  // valid only if NOT a source-export table
                    }
                }
                Type => true,  // valid
            }
        }
        None => false,  // INVALID: item not found
    };
    if !valid {
        return Err(AdapterError::InvalidTableMutationSelection);
    }
    for id in ids_to_check {
        validate_read_dependencies(catalog, &id)?;  // recurse
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

So InvalidTableMutationSelection is returned when `valid` is false. That happens when:

1. **Source | Secret | Connection** – The dependency is a source, secret, or connection. Always invalid.

2. **Table** – Invalid when:
   - The table is not a user table (`!id.is_user()`), OR
   - The table is a source-export table (`entry.source_export_details().is_some()`).

3. **Func | View | MaterializedView | ContinualTask** – Invalid when `valid_id` is false, i.e. when `!(id.is_user() || matches!(typ, Func))`. So invalid when it's a View, MaterializedView, or ContinualTask that is NOT a user object (e.g. system view). Func is always valid (`valid_id` is true because of `matches!(typ, Func)`).

4. **None** – The catalog entry doesn't exist. Invalid.

So in plain language, a dependency is **invalid** (and you get InvalidTableMutationSelection) when:
- It's a **Source**, **Secret**, or **Connection**
- It's a **Table** that is either non-user (e.g. system table) or a source-export (table created FROM SOURCE)
- It's a **View**, **MaterializedView**, or **ContinualTask** that is not a user object (e.g. system view)
- The item **doesn't exist** in the catalog

And validation is recursive: if the item is a View/MV/Func/ContinualTask, we also validate all of `entry.uses()`. So any transitive dependency that is a source, source-export table, etc. will eventually fail.
</details></think>

---


Here’s when **InvalidTableMutationSelection** is returned from `validate_read_dependencies` in `inner.rs`.

A dependency is **invalid** (and you get this error) when:

1. **Source | Secret | Connection**  
   The dependency is a source, secret, or connection → always invalid.

2. **Table**  
   Invalid if either:
   - The table is **not** a user table (`!id.is_user()`), or  
   - The table **is** a source-export (`entry.source_export_details().is_some()`), i.e. created with `CREATE TABLE ... FROM SOURCE ...`.

3. **View | MaterializedView | ContinualTask**  
   Invalid when the object is **not** a user object (`!id.is_user()`). So user-created views/MVs are valid as long as their transitive dependencies are valid; system views etc. are invalid.

4. **Func**  
   Treated as valid for the “user vs system” check (`valid_id` is true for `Func`). Invalid only if some transitive dependency (via `entry.uses()`) is invalid.

5. **Type**  
   Always valid.

6. **Missing entry**  
   `catalog.try_get_entry(id)` is `None` → invalid.

Validation is **recursive**: for Func / View / MaterializedView / ContinualTask, `validate_read_dependencies` is called on every `entry.uses()`. So even if the direct dependency is a view, you still get **InvalidTableMutationSelection** if any transitive dependency is:

- a **Source**, **Secret**, or **Connection**, or  
- a **Table** that is non-user or a source-export, or  
- a non-user **View** / **MaterializedView** / **ContinualTask**, or  
- missing in the catalog.

So in short: the dependency is invalid whenever the selection (directly or transitively) refers to a source, a source-export table, a secret, a connection, or a non-user table/view/MV/task.
